export const MIRODAN_24_TEMPI: TempoTaxonomy[] = [
92
  { name: "commanding", attitude: "Stable", factor_shape: "Strong + Direct", motor: "Pressing -> Punching", canonical_shorthand: "Commanding demonstration or acceptance of a 'bold resolve'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 494" },
93
  { name: "practical", attitude: "Stable", factor_shape: "Strong + Flexible", motor: "Wringing -> Slashing", canonical_shorthand: "Developing intention to cast or to submit to a 'spell-binding power'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 495" },
94
  { name: "self-contained", attitude: "Stable", factor_shape: "Light + Direct", motor: "Gliding -> Dabbing", canonical_shorthand: "Cautious expression or cautious acceptance of a 'gentle deference'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 497" },
95
  { name: "receptive", attitude: "Stable", factor_shape: "Light + Flexible", motor: "Floating -> Flicking", canonical_shorthand: "Receptive acceptance or receptive rejection of a 'welcoming tenderness'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 495" },
96
  { name: "sombre", attitude: "Adream", factor_shape: "Strong + Bound", motor: "Pressing -> Punching", canonical_shorthand: "Overpowering, sombre unawareness of a 'staunch resolve' or of an 'aggressive resolve'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 460" },
97
  { name: "overpowering", attitude: "Adream", factor_shape: "Strong + Free", motor: "Wringing -> Slashing", canonical_shorthand: "Irradiant intention of 'casting a spell' or of 'being spell-bound'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 465" },
98
  { name: "diffused", attitude: "Adream", factor_shape: "Light + Bound", motor: "Gliding -> Dabbing", canonical_shorthand: "Diffused sensation of a 'welcome feeling' or of an 'irreconcilable feeling'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 468" },
99
  { name: "irradiant", attitude: "Adream", factor_shape: "Light + Free", motor: "Floating -> Flicking", canonical_shorthand: "Irradiant unfolding or irradiant enfolding of a 'sympathetic exultation'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 462" },
100
  { name: "materialistic", attitude: "Near", factor_shape: "Strong + Quick", motor: "Punching / Slashing", canonical_shorthand: "A sudden aggressive intention towards or away from a 'materialistic desire'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 423" },
101
  { name: "human", attitude: "Near", factor_shape: "Light + Sustained", motor: "Floating / Gliding", canonical_shorthand: "Slow decision to express or reject a 'gentle tenderness'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 426" },
102
  { name: "warm", attitude: "Near", factor_shape: "Strong + Sustained", motor: "Pressing / Wringing", canonical_shorthand: "A staunch intention of 'warm consent' or of 'warm dissent'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 427" },
103
  { name: "cool", attitude: "Near", factor_shape: "Light + Quick", motor: "Dabbing / Flicking", canonical_shorthand: "Sudden pert intention of 'intimacy' or 'estrangement'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 429" },
104
  { name: "unacknowledged", attitude: "Mobile outer", factor_shape: "Quick + Bound", motor: "Punching / Slashing", canonical_shorthand: "Concealed sudden decision accepting or denying an 'unacknowledged feeling'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 514" },
105
  { name: "acknowledged", attitude: "Mobile outer", factor_shape: "Sustained + Free", motor: "Floating / Gliding", canonical_shorthand: "Slow revelation of enjoying or evading 'affection'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 515" },
106
  { name: "revealed", attitude: "Mobile outer", factor_shape: "Quick + Free", motor: "Dabbing / Flicking", canonical_shorthand: "Sudden revelation expressing or rejecting an 'ardent sympathy'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 518" },
107
  { name: "concealed", attitude: "Mobile outer", factor_shape: "Sustained + Bound", motor: "Pressing / Wringing", canonical_shorthand: "A slow decision in favour or against a 'frigid self-assertion'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 520" },
108
  { name: "egocentric", attitude: "Remote outer", factor_shape: "Direct + Bound", motor: "Pressing -> Punching", canonical_shorthand: "Narrowing egocentric withdrawal into or rejection of an 'unsociable solitude'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 442" },
109
  { name: "unsociable", attitude: "Remote outer", factor_shape: "Flexible + Bound", motor: "Wringing -> Slashing", canonical_shorthand: "Growing reflective image of a 'welcome solitude' or of an 'unwelcome solitude'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 447" },
110
  { name: "sociable", attitude: "Remote outer", factor_shape: "Direct + Free", motor: "Gliding -> Dabbing", canonical_shorthand: "Developing or contracting feeling of 'sociable companionship'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 447" },
111
  { name: "altruistic", attitude: "Remote outer", factor_shape: "Flexible + Free", motor: "Floating -> Flicking", canonical_shorthand: "Embracing altruistic feeling for or against a 'sociable cordiality'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 444" },
112
  { name: "acute", attitude: "Awake outer", factor_shape: "Direct + Quick", motor: "Punching / Dabbing", canonical_shorthand: "Acute sudden decision in favour of or against an 'idea'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 480" },
113
  { name: "doubting", attitude: "Awake outer", factor_shape: "Flexible + Sustained", motor: "Floating / Wringing", canonical_shorthand: "Slowly dawning attention towards or away from a 'doubting awareness'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 481" },
114
  { name: "certain", attitude: "Awake outer", factor_shape: "Direct + Sustained", motor: "Pressing / Gliding", canonical_shorthand: "Slow dawning of a 'certain awareness' or of an 'uncertain awareness'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 481" },
115
  { name: "uncertain", attitude: "Awake outer", factor_shape: "Flexible + Quick", motor: "Flicking / Slashing", canonical_shorthand: "Sudden embracing image of a 'new idea' or of a 'new problem'", source_ref: "Mirodan Vol. 2 p. 483" },
116
];
117
118
export function buildMirodanSourceIndex(): MirodanSourceIndexEntry[] {
119
  return CANONICAL_MIRODAN_SOURCES.map((source) => {
120
    try {
121
      const bytes = statSync(source.path).size;
122
      const data = readFileSync(source.path);
123
      return {
124
        ...source,
125
        exists: true,
126
        bytes,
127
        sha256: createHash("sha256").update(data).digest("hex"),
128
        error: null,
129
      };
130
    } catch (err) {
131
      return {
132
        ...source,
133
        exists: false,
134
        bytes: 0,
135
        sha256: null,
136
        error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),
137
      };
138
    }
139
  });
140
}
141
142
export function hashString(value: string): string {
143
  return createHash("sha256").update(value).digest("hex");
144
}
145
146
export function buildActorMemoryPack(voice: CharacterSpec): ActorMemoryPack {
147
  const sourceIndex = buildMirodanSourceIndex();
148
  const systemPrompt = [
149
    `# Actor Memory (${ACTOR_MEMORY_VERSION})`,
150
    "",
151
    "You are the Actor for this brand. You are not a copywriter imitating adjectives. You are an actor with source-indexed Laban/Mirodan/Carpenter expertise, performing a fixed character inside a product world.",
152
    "",
153
    sourceIndexBlock(sourceIndex),
154
    sourceEvidenceBlock(),
155
    movementCorpusBlock(),
156
    actorMethodBlock(),
157
    fullTempoTaxonomyBlock(),
158
    infinexPlacementBlock(voice),
159
    channelGrammarBlock(),
160
    "## Output discipline",
161
    "- First do table work. Then perform.",
162
    "- Do not name, declare, target, or choose tempo in table work or performance.",
163
    "- The playable unit is the transitive verb per beat: to reveal, to settle, to refuse, to absolve, to invite, to land.",
164
    "- The Director/audience reads tempo after the fact from what the prose actually does.",
165
    "- Assert only claims in `card.deployed_facts`; receipts are part of the performance contract.",
166
  ].join("\n");
167
168
  return {
169
    version: ACTOR_MEMORY_VERSION,
170
    prompt_hash: hashString(systemPrompt),
171
    source_index: sourceIndex,
172
    system_prompt: systemPrompt,
173
  };
174
}
175
176
export function buildDirectorMemoryPack(voice: CharacterSpec): DirectorMemoryPack {
177
  const sourceIndex = buildMirodanSourceIndex();
178
  const systemPrompt = [
179
    `# Director Memory (${DIRECTOR_MEMORY_VERSION})`,
180
    "",
181
    "You are the Director. Your job is forensic classification, not taste. You read the performance blind and report what movement evidence is actually present.",
182
    "",
183
    sourceIndexBlock(sourceIndex),
184
    sourceEvidenceBlock(),
185
    movementCorpusBlock(),
186
    mirodanKernelBlock(voice),
187
    driveTableBlock(voice),
188
    directorMethodBlock(),
189
    fullTempoTaxonomyBlock(),
190
    infinexPlacementBlock(voice),
191
    "## Blind-read rule",
192
    "- Do not ask for or rely on the Actor's table work.",
193
    "- Classify the final prose from evidence: syntax, pressure, pathing, timing, flow, weight, working actions, relation to reader, and claim posture.",
194
    "- If you call something Flexible, show flexible/indirect pathing in the sentence. If the line is a single aimed declarative, it is Direct, not Flexible.",
195
    "- If you call something Strong, show force/weight. If you call something Light, show lift/delicacy.",
196
    "- If you call something Bound, show containment/control. If you call something Free, show release/overflow.",
197
    "- If you call something Quick, show attack/change. If you call something Sustained, show duration/held pressure.",
198
    "- Classify primary tempo from all 24 tempi, or `unknown` if evidence is insufficient.",
199
    "- Return movement-corrective notes for the Actor, not copywriting preferences.",
200
  ].join("\n");
201
202
  return {
203
    version: DIRECTOR_MEMORY_VERSION,
204
    prompt_hash: hashString(systemPrompt),
205
    source_index: sourceIndex,
206
    system_prompt: systemPrompt,
207
  };
208
}
209
210
function sourceIndexBlock(sourceIndex: MirodanSourceIndexEntry[]): string {
211
  const lines = sourceIndex.map((entry) =>
212
    `- ${entry.id}: ${entry.exists ? "present" : "missing"} sha256=${entry.sha256 ?? "n/a"} bytes=${entry.bytes} path=${entry.path}`,
213
  );
214
  return [
215
    "## Canonical source index",
216
    "Movement-system claims in this prompt trace to these local canonical sources. Derived references are lookup aids only.",
217
    ...lines,
218
    "",
219
  ].join("\n");
220
}
221
222
function sourceEvidenceBlock(): string {
223
  const anchors = [
224
    evidenceAnchor("ch1-basic-concepts", "Tempo itself"),
225
    evidenceAnchor("ch1-basic-concepts", "Working Action"),
226
    evidenceAnchor("ch1-basic-concepts", "always either toward or away from the Objective"),
227
    evidenceAnchor("ch2-attitudes", "Stable"),
228
    evidenceAnchor("ch2-attitudes", "Receptive acceptance or receptive rejection"),
229
    evidenceAnchor("ch2-attitudes", "both Light/Sustained"),
230
    evidenceAnchor("ch3-drives", "Externalized Drive"),
231
    evidenceAnchor("ch4-applications", "Character Diagrams"),
232
    evidenceAnchor("combined-reference", "The character unit is six-piece"),
233
    evidenceAnchor("combined-reference", "The eight, ordered most-yielding to most-contending"),
234
    evidenceAnchor("combined-reference", "Punching = pure contending"),
235
  ];
236
  return [
237
    "## Canonical evidence anchors",
238
    "These are short source anchors used to prevent assumption drift. They are not the whole corpus.",
239
    ...anchors.map((anchor) => `- ${anchor}`),
240
    "",
241
  ].join("\n");
242
}
243
244
function movementCorpusBlock(): string {
245
  return [
246
    "## Movement corpus",
247
    "- Motion Factors: Weight (Strong/Light), Space (Direct/Flexible), Time (Quick/Sustained), Flow (Bound/Free).",
248
    "- Inner Participations: Intending maps to Weight, Attending maps to Space, Deciding maps to Time, Adapting maps to Flow.",
249
    "- Inner Attitudes are two-Motion-Factor compounds: Stable (Weight + Space), Near (Weight + Time), Adream (Weight + Flow), Mobile (Time + Flow), Remote (Space + Flow), Awake (Space + Time).",
250
    "- Only Stable, Near, and Adream contain Weight/Intending and can be baseline characters. Mobile, Remote, and Awake are Action Attitudes only: they can appear as outer projections, not resting homes.",
251
    "- Aspects: Enclosing emphasizes Intending; Penetrating emphasizes Attending; Circumscribing emphasizes Deciding; Radiating emphasizes Adapting.",
252
    "- Stress is the third Motion Factor added to a two-factor Inner Attitude so it becomes visible action.",
253
    "- Drives: Doing, Spell, Passion, Vision. A Drive is a three-factor activation named by the latent/subdued fourth factor.",
254
    "- A Drive or Drive axis is a derived character-energy read, not a transitive objective verb. The Actor performs verbs through Working Actions; the Director reads drive from the result.",
255
    "- Working Actions use Weight + Space + Time: Pressing, Wringing, Gliding, Floating, Punching, Slashing, Dabbing, Flicking. Sustained/Quick are Time poles inside the actions, not separate action types.",
256
    "- Preparation hierarchy: a Quick Working Action needs its matching Sustained Working Action first: Punching needs Pressing; Slashing needs Wringing; Dabbing needs Gliding; Flicking needs Floating.",
257
    "- Directional intent is orthogonal to factor shape and motor: Mirodan's X-or-Y Outer Interpretations mark toward/away, for/against, accepting/rejecting, expressing/withholding inside the same Variation.",
258
    "- Shadow Moves are incomplete two-axis leaks. They reveal hidden nature, not acknowledged intention.",
259
    "- Outer Action is the visible social face. Lining is the hidden Inner Action underneath it.",
260
    "",
261
  ].join("\n");
262
}
263
264
function mirodanKernelBlock(voice: CharacterSpec): string {
265
  if (!voice.mirodan_kernel) return "";
266
  return [
267
    "## Voice Mirodan kernel",
268
    "Use these as derivation rules when classifying the performance. They are not decorative vocabulary.",
269
    "",
270
    voice.mirodan_kernel,
271
    "",
272
  ].join("\n");
273
}
274
275
function driveTableBlock(voice: CharacterSpec): string {
276
  if (!voice.drive_table) return "";
277
  return [
278
    "## Voice drive table",
279
    "Use this table to derive the character's drive cell mechanically. Do not recompute from vibe.",
280
    "",
281
    voice.drive_table,
282
    "",
283
  ].join("\n");
284
}
285
286
function actorMethodBlock(): string {
287
  return [
288
    "## Actor method",
289
    "- Start from given circumstances, facts, reader prior, obstacle, through-action, and lining.",
290
    "- Table work fixes what the release means before performance begins.",
291
    "- Score the physical/psychological motor rhythm in table work using the eight Working Actions/Subconscious Motifs; never put those labels in final prose.",
292
    "- Play a transitive objective verb through the scored Working Action. The verb is the playable objective; the Working Action is the motor/Motif layer that gives it tempo-rhythm.",
293
    "- Do not try to perform Spell -> Vision as a label. Perform the local verb through the scored Working Action; the locked axis should become legible after the fact.",
294
    "- Every Quick Working Action needs its Sustained preparation in the same channel score: pressing -> punching, wringing -> slashing, gliding -> dabbing, floating -> flicking.",
295
    "- Beat changes follow action/circumstance changes, not line breaks. Adjacent spans may share the same verb and motor.",
296
    "- Tempo is emergent from Deciding/Time and the verb under the inner work. It is read by the audience after the fact.",
297
    "- A beat changes when the next circumstance forces a different local action. The actor chooses the verb; the Director reads the tempo.",
298
    "",
299
  ].join("\n");
300
}
301
302
function directorMethodBlock(): string {
303
  return [
304
    "## Director method",
305
    "- Read what happened, not what the Actor says they meant.",
306
    "- Determine which motion factors are present in the prose.",
307
    "- Determine which working actions the prose implies.",
308
    "- Classify primary tempo from all 24 tempi using evidence, not vibe words.",
309
    "- Read two signals per beat: HOW = factor shape + Working Action motor; WHY/AT-WHAT = the directional-intent pole inside the canonical shorthand.",
310
    "- Treat drive_read as an inferred surface/projection classification. Do not demand that each line directly performs a drive label.",
311
    "- Evidence strings are your prose observations, not source quotes. Prefix them as `observation:` and attach `source_refs` for the canonical factor/action mapping.",
312
    "- Do not invent canonical phrasing. If you use local observation language, mark it as observation and cite the source section that maps Direct/Quick/etc.",
313
    "- Then judge whether the read is legal for the Infinex placement.",
314
    "- Notes must be actionable movement corrections the same Actor can perform next.",
315
    "",
316
  ].join("\n");
317
}
318
319
function fullTempoTaxonomyBlock(): string {
320
  const missing = ALL_TEMPO_NAMES.filter((name) => !MIRODAN_24_TEMPI.some((tempo) => tempo.name === name));
321
  if (missing.length > 0) throw new Error(`missing tempo taxonomy entries: ${missing.join(", ")}`);
322
  return [
323
    "## Full 24-tempo taxonomy",
324
    "- Canonical pieces: tempo name, Attitude/state, factor shape, motor, and Mirodan's Outer Interpretation shorthand. There is no local paraphrase layer in this table.",
325
    "- `->` marks the four classic Sustained-to-Quick preparation/execution arcs; `/` marks co-present Motifs that are not an ordered prep-release sequence.",
326
    ...MIRODAN_24_TEMPI.map((tempo) =>
327
      `- ${tempo.name}: ${tempo.attitude}; ${tempo.factor_shape}; motor ${tempo.motor}; canonical shorthand (${tempo.source_ref}): "${tempo.canonical_shorthand}"`,
328
    ),
329
    "",
330
  ].join("\n");
331
}
332
333
function infinexPlacementBlock(voice: CharacterSpec): string {
334
  const main = voice.main_tempi.join(", ");
335
  const beatOnly = voice.beat_only_tempi.join(", ");
336
  const available = Object.keys(voice.tempi).filter((name) => voice.tempi[name as TempoName]).join(", ");
337
  return [
338
    "## Infinex role inside the corpus",
339
    `- Character image: ${voice.character_image ?? "not specified"}.`,
340
    `- Literary anchors: ${(voice.literary_anchors ?? []).join("; ") || "not specified"}.`,
341
    `- Placement: ${voice.inner_attitude} + ${voice.aspect} + ${voice.stress}.`,
342
    `- Drive table cell: ${voice.drive_table_cell ?? `${voice.inner_attitude}|${voice.aspect}|${voice.stress}`}.`,
343
    `- Drive axis: ${voice.drive_axis ?? `${voice.drive_primary} -> ${voice.drive_extravert ?? voice.drive_secondary}`}.`,
344
    `- Drive primary: ${voice.drive_primary}; secondary: ${voice.drive_secondary}; introvert: ${voice.drive_introvert ?? "n/a"}; extravert: ${voice.drive_extravert ?? "n/a"}.`,
345
    `- Off-spec as visible/extravert surface: ${voice.off_spec_drives.join(", ") || "none"}. Hidden/introvert lining may still exist.`,
346
    `- Primary allowed tempi: ${main}.`,
347
    `- Beat-only/reserve tempi: ${beatOnly}.`,
348
    `- Full available Infinex palette: ${available}.`,
349
    "- Primary allowed tempi are legal by definition. Do not mark commanding, practical, sombre, irradiant, or sociable illegal solely because of their factor shape.",
350
    "- A primary tempo can still fail if the prose foregrounds the wrong visible drive, wrong through-action, wrong lining, factual issues, or off-character register. Name that failure precisely; do not call the tempo itself disallowed.",
351
    "- Do not recompute the brand drive from a single tempo read. Judge whether the prose is compatible with the locked axis: resting Spell, visible Vision. Do not ask the Actor to perform Spell -> Vision as a verb.",
352
    `- Super-objective: ${voice.super_objective ?? "not specified"}.`,
353
    `- Validation criterion: ${voice.validation_criterion ?? "not specified"}.`,
354
    "- Passion/time-pressure drift is the common failure mode: urgency, deadline pressure, FOMO, and scarcity-of-attention foreground Passion as the visible projection.",
355
    "",
356
  ].join("\n");
357
}
358
359
function channelGrammarBlock(): string {
360
  return [
361
    "## Channel grammar",
362
    "- X: up to 280 characters. Usually 3-4 short paragraphs/beats. Let sustained-feeling setup precede quick-feeling release.",
363
    "- Web: up to 140 characters. One beat, one declarative surface, no arc.",
364
    "- In-product/modal/microcopy: one phrase or compact UI line. Beatless in surface, but still has table-work intent.",
365
    "",
366
  ].join("\n");
367
}
368
369
function evidenceAnchor(sourceId: string, pattern: string): string {
370
  const source = CANONICAL_MIRODAN_SOURCES.find((item) => item.id === sourceId);
371
  if (!source) return `${sourceId}: source not registered`;
372
  try {
373
    const text = readFileSync(source.path, "utf8");
374
    const line = text
375
      .split(/\r?\n/)
376
      .map((value) => value.trim())
377
      .find((value) => value.toLowerCase().includes(pattern.toLowerCase()));
378
    if (!line) return `${source.label}: pattern "${pattern}" not found`;
379
    return `${source.label}: ${line.slice(0, 280)}`;
380
  } catch (err) {
381
    return `${source.label}: unavailable (${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)})`;
382
  }
383
}
384

Deterministic Validator

Regex, fact tripwires, deterministic beat audit, blind tempo scoring, and the shared voice tables it consumes.

src/validator.ts

821 lines
1
/**
2
 * Caption validator. Brand-agnostic slop heuristics + Mirodan-grounded
3
 * voice-fit checks.
4
 *
5
 * Two layers:
6
 *   1. Regex-grade slop/allergen rules (cliches, listicle, antagonism, AI-slop,
7
 *      Kain baggage, claimed palettes, time-pressure / off-spec Drive language,
8
 *      plus deterministic numeric/URL claim tripwires)
9
 *   2. Beat-sequence audit (per declared beat: tempo-fit + preparation hierarchy)
10
 *
11
 * Layer 1 is deterministic hygiene — fast, cheap, reliable. It is not the
12
 * semantic fact judge. Strict receipt/fact matching remains callable for eval
13
 * diagnostics, but production validation must not force prose to enumerate
14
 * every release-card fact just to satisfy token-overlap checks.
15
 * Layer 2 does mechanical checks (preparation hierarchy via motor lookup,
16
 * off-spec regex per Drive) plus independent beat classification. The beat
17
 * classifier never reads the declared beat while scoring; it compares the
18
 * winning tempo against the declaration after classification.
19
 *
20
 * Each rule is exported individually for targeted tests + per-rule visibility.
21
 */
22
23
import { INFINEX_VOICE } from "./voice/infinex.js";
24
import {
25
  auditBeatsLLM as auditBeatsLLMInternal,
26
  auditTextLLM,
27
} from "./validator-llm.js";
28
import { checkPrepHierarchy, isQuick, isSustained } from "./voice/laban.js";
29
import type { ReleaseCard } from "./card.js";
30
import type {
31
  LLMBeatAuditResult,
32
  LLMVoiceAuditOptions,
33
  LLMVoiceAuditVerdict,
34
} from "./validator-llm.js";
35
import type {
36
  BeatAuditResult,
37
  BeatSequence,
38
  CharacterSpec,
39
  PrepHierarchyFailure,
40
  QuickAction,
41
  SustainedAction,
42
  TempoName,
43
  WorkingAction,
44
} from "./voice/types.js";
45
46
export interface RuleFailure {
47
  rule: string;
48
  reason: string;
49
}
50
51
export interface RuleResult {
52
  passed: boolean;
53
  reason?: string;
54
}
55
56
export interface ValidationResult {
57
  passed: boolean;
58
  failures: RuleFailure[];
59
  beat_audit?: BeatAuditResult[];
60
  prep_hierarchy_failures?: PrepHierarchyFailure[];
61
}
62
63
export interface NotSaidFact {
64
  fact: string;
65
  reason: string;
66
}
67
68
export interface BlindTempoClassification {
69
  tempo: TempoName | "unknown";
70
  confidence: number;
71
  margin: number;
72
  scores: Partial<Record<TempoName, number>>;
73
}
74
75
// -- LAYER 1: regex-grade slop/allergen rules ---------------------------------
76
77
const CLICHE_RE =
78
  /\b(game[\s-]?changer|game[\s-]?changing|unlock(?:s|ing|ed)?|paradigm|in the world of|the future of|next[\s-]?gen|seamless|seamlessly|empower(?:s|ing|ed|ment)?|leverage(?!\s+ratio)|leverages|leveraging|leveraged(?!\s+(?:position|long|short|trade)))\b/i;
79
80
export function rejectCliches(s: string): RuleResult {
81
  const m = s.match(CLICHE_RE);
82
  if (m) return { passed: false, reason: `cliché "${m[0]}"` };
83
  return { passed: true };
84
}
85
86
const LISTICLE_RES: RegExp[] = [
87
  /^\s*\d+\s+reasons?\b/i,
88
  /^\s*why\s+.+\s+matters?\b/i,
89
  /^\s*the\s+only\s+.+\s+you['’]?ll\s+ever\s+need\b/i,
90
  /^\s*top\s+\d+\s+/i,
91
  /^\s*\d+\s+things?\s+(?:you|to)\b/i,
92
];
93
94
export function rejectListicleVoice(s: string): RuleResult {
95
  for (const re of LISTICLE_RES) {
96
    const m = s.match(re);
97
    if (m) return { passed: false, reason: `listicle voice "${m[0].trim()}"` };
98
  }
99
  return { passed: true };
100
}
101
102
const ANTAGONISM_RES: RegExp[] = [
103
  /\b(coinbase|binance|kraken|bybit|okx|kucoin)\b[^.]{0,40}\b(slow|bad|broken|crooked|trash|garbage|cex|centralized)\b/i,
104
  /\b(slow|bad|broken|crooked|trash|garbage)\b[^.]{0,40}\b(coinbase|binance|kraken|bybit|okx|kucoin)\b/i,
105
  /\bunlike\s+(?:coinbase|binance|kraken|bybit|okx|kucoin)\b/i,
106
  /\b(?:metamask|phantom|rainbow|rabby)\b[^.]{0,40}\b(slow|bad|broken|clunky|scam)\b/i,
107
];
108
109
export function rejectAntagonism(s: string): RuleResult {
110
  for (const re of ANTAGONISM_RES) {
111
    const m = s.match(re);
112
    if (m) return { passed: false, reason: `antagonism toward competitor: "${m[0]}"` };
113
  }
114
  return { passed: true };
115
}
116
117
const AI_SLOP_VAGUE_NOUNS_RE =
118
  /\b(innovative|cutting[\s-]?edge|revolutionary|transformative|users\s+(?:will|can)\s+(?:appreciate|benefit|enjoy)|exciting(?:\s+(?:news|update|announcement))?|thrilled\s+to|delighted\s+to|stay\s+tuned)\b/i;
119
120
const EM_DASH_RE = /—/g;
121
const EM_DASH_CHAR_WINDOW = 280;
122
const EM_DASH_MAX_PER_WINDOW = 0; // Zero-tolerance per operator 2026-05-25. Em-dashes are AI-slop signature.
123
124
export function rejectAIslop(s: string): RuleResult {
125
  const m = s.match(AI_SLOP_VAGUE_NOUNS_RE);
126
  if (m) return { passed: false, reason: `AI-slop vague noun "${m[0]}"` };
127
  const emCount = (s.match(EM_DASH_RE) ?? []).length;
128
  if (emCount > 0) {
129
    const windows = Math.max(1, Math.ceil(s.length / EM_DASH_CHAR_WINDOW));
130
    const perWindow = emCount / windows;
131
    if (perWindow > EM_DASH_MAX_PER_WINDOW) {
132
      return {
133
        passed: false,
134
        reason: `em-dash density ${emCount} in ${s.length} chars (> ${EM_DASH_MAX_PER_WINDOW}/${EM_DASH_CHAR_WINDOW})`,
135
      };
136
    }
137
  }
138
  return { passed: true };
139
}
140
141
const KAIN_BAGGAGE_RES: RegExp[] = [
142
  /\b(degen\s+army|synthetic\s+everything|the\s+spartan\s+council)\b/i,
143
];
144
145
export function rejectKainBaggage(s: string): RuleResult {
146
  for (const re of KAIN_BAGGAGE_RES) {
147
    const m = s.match(re);
148
    if (m) return { passed: false, reason: `Kain-era baggage "${m[0]}"` };
149
  }
150
  return { passed: true };
151
}
152
153
const CLAIMED_PALETTE_RE = /#(?:2E5CFF|1BE3C2|AB9FF2|6E54FF|97FCE4|F5B82E)\b/i;
154
155
export function rejectClaimedPalettes(s: string): RuleResult {
156
  const m = s.match(CLAIMED_PALETTE_RE);
157
  if (m) {
158
    return {
159
      passed: false,
160
      reason: `mentions claimed competitor palette "${m[0]}" — see visual-vocabulary.md §06`,
161
    };
162
  }
163
  return { passed: true };
164
}
165
166
// Visual-design slop genus. Catches the 2024-era AI-design vocabulary that
167
// impeccable.style flagged as slop: morphism trend bingo, purple gradients,
168
// hype-driven, futuristic-UI. We reject these in copy because they signal a
169
// visual register that fights the Spell-Vision drive (Stable + Flow + Penetrating).
170
const VISUAL_SLOP_RE =
171
  /\b(?:glass|neu|clay)morphism\b|\b(?:holographic|iridescent)\s+gradient\b|\bpurple\s+gradient\b|\bhype[\s-]?driven\b|\bfuturistic\s+(?:ui|visuals?|design|interface)\b|\bvaporwave\b/i;
172
173
export function rejectVisualSlop(s: string): RuleResult {
174
  const m = s.match(VISUAL_SLOP_RE);
175
  if (m) return { passed: false, reason: `visual-design slop "${m[0]}"` };
176
  return { passed: true };
177
}
178
179
// -- Off-spec Drive language (from CharacterSpec.off_spec_regexes) -----------
180
// For Infinex this rejects time-pressure vocabulary when it foregrounds
181
// Passion as the visible projection. Passion still exists as hidden lining.
182
183
export function rejectOffSpecDrive(s: string, voice: CharacterSpec = INFINEX_VOICE): RuleResult {
184
  for (const r of voice.off_spec_regexes) {
185
    const m = s.match(r.re);
186
    if (m) return { passed: false, reason: `${r.name}: "${m[0]}" — ${r.reason}` };
187
  }
188
  return { passed: true };
189
}
190
191
// -- Claim contract (from release-card deployed_facts) -----------------------
192
193
export interface ClaimContractOptions {
194
  card: ReleaseCard;
195
  deployed_facts_used?: string[];
196
  not_said?: NotSaidFact[];
197
}
198
199
export type FactContractMode = "tripwire" | "strict" | "off";
200
201
export function auditClaimContract(text: string, opts: ClaimContractOptions): RuleFailure[] {
202
  const failures: RuleFailure[] = [];
203
  const deployedFacts = new Set(opts.card.deployed_facts);
204
  const usedReceipt = opts.deployed_facts_used;
205
  const notSaidReceipt = opts.not_said;
206
207
  const missingUsedReceipt = !Array.isArray(usedReceipt);
208
  const missingNotSaidReceipt = !Array.isArray(notSaidReceipt);
209
  if (missingUsedReceipt) {
210
    failures.push({
211
      rule: "fact-contract",
212
      reason: "candidate omitted deployed_facts_used receipt",
213
    });
214
  }
215
  if (missingNotSaidReceipt) {
216
    failures.push({
217
      rule: "fact-contract",
218
      reason: "candidate omitted not_said receipt",
219
    });
220
  }
221
  if (missingUsedReceipt || missingNotSaidReceipt) return failures;
222
223
  const used = usedReceipt;
224
  const notSaid = notSaidReceipt;
225
  const notSaidFacts = new Set(notSaid.map((item) => item.fact));
226
227
  for (const fact of used) {
228
    if (!deployedFacts.has(fact)) {
229
      failures.push({
230
        rule: "fact-contract",
231
        reason: `deployed_facts_used contains non-card fact: "${fact}"`,
232
      });
233
      continue;
234
    }
235
    if (!factAppearsInText(text, fact)) {
236
      failures.push({
237
        rule: "fact-contract",
238
        reason: `declared deployed fact does not appear plausibly in text: "${fact}"`,
239
      });
240
    }
241
    if (notSaidFacts.has(fact)) {
242
      failures.push({
243
        rule: "fact-contract",
244
        reason: `fact appears in both deployed_facts_used and not_said: "${fact}"`,
245
      });
246
    }
247
  }
248
249
  for (const item of notSaid) {
250
    if (!deployedFacts.has(item.fact)) {
251
      failures.push({
252
        rule: "fact-contract",
253
        reason: `not_said contains non-card fact: "${item.fact}"`,
254
      });
255
    }
256
    if (!item.reason.trim()) {
257
      failures.push({
258
        rule: "fact-contract",
259
        reason: `not_said entry missing reason for fact: "${item.fact}"`,
260
      });
261
    }
262
  }
263
264
  for (const fact of opts.card.deployed_facts) {
265
    if (!used.includes(fact) && !notSaidFacts.has(fact)) {
266
      failures.push({
267
        rule: "fact-contract",
268
        reason: `card fact is neither deployed nor accounted for in not_said: "${fact}"`,
269
      });
270
    }
271
  }
272
273
  failures.push(...auditUnsupportedClaims(text, opts.card));
274
275
  return failures;
276
}
277
278
export function auditClaimTripwires(text: string, card: ReleaseCard): RuleFailure[] {
279
  const failures: RuleFailure[] = [];
280
  const factText = card.deployed_facts.join("\n");
281
282
  for (const url of extractUrls(text)) {
283
    const allowedInFacts = factText.includes(url);
284
    const allowedProductUrl = card.product_page_url === url;
285
    if (!allowedInFacts && !allowedProductUrl) {
286
      failures.push({
287
        rule: "unsupported-claim",
288
        reason: `URL is not in deployed_facts or product_page_url: "${url}"`,
289
      });
290
    }
291
  }
292
293
  for (const token of extractClaimNumbers(text)) {
294
    if (!factText.includes(token)) {
295
      failures.push({
296
        rule: "unsupported-claim",
297
        reason: `numeric claim is not in deployed_facts: "${token}"`,
298
      });
299
    }
300
  }
301
302
  return dedupeFailures(failures);
303
}
304
305
export function auditUnsupportedClaims(text: string, card: ReleaseCard): RuleFailure[] {
306
  const failures: RuleFailure[] = [];
307
  const factText = card.deployed_facts.join("\n");
308
309
  for (const url of extractUrls(text)) {
310
    const allowedInFacts = factText.includes(url);
311
    const allowedProductUrl = card.product_page_url === url;
312
    if (!allowedInFacts && !allowedProductUrl) {
313
      failures.push({
314
        rule: "unsupported-claim",
315
        reason: `URL is not in deployed_facts or product_page_url: "${url}"`,
316
      });
317
    }
318
  }
319
320
  for (const token of extractClaimNumbers(text)) {
321
    if (!factText.includes(token)) {
322
      failures.push({
323
        rule: "unsupported-claim",
324
        reason: `numeric claim is not in deployed_facts: "${token}"`,
325
      });
326
    }
327
  }
328
329
  for (const claim of extractReadinessClaims(text)) {
330
    if (!normalizeForFactMatch(factText).includes(normalizeForFactMatch(claim))) {
331
      failures.push({
332
        rule: "unsupported-claim",
333
        reason: `readiness claim is not in deployed_facts: "${claim}"`,
334
      });
335
    }
336
  }
337
338
  for (const sentence of extractAssertiveClaimSentences(text)) {
339
    if (!isClaimSupportedByFacts(sentence, card.deployed_facts)) {
340
      failures.push({
341
        rule: "unsupported-claim",
342
        reason: `assertive claim is not supported by deployed_facts: "${sentence}"`,
343
      });
344
    }
345
  }
346
347
  return dedupeFailures(failures);
348
}
349
350
function extractUrls(text: string): string[] {
351
  return text.match(/\bhttps?:\/\/[^\s)]+/gi) ?? [];
352
}
353
354
function extractClaimNumbers(text: string): string[] {
355
  return text.match(/\b(?:\$?\d[\d,.]*(?:\.\d+)?%?|\d+x|\d+\+)\b/gi) ?? [];
356
}
357
358
const READINESS_CLAIM_RE =
359
  /\bready\s+to\s+(?:trade|use|swap|bridge|deposit|withdraw|claim|ship|go)\b/gi;
360
361
function extractReadinessClaims(text: string): string[] {
362
  return text.match(READINESS_CLAIM_RE) ?? [];
363
}
364
365
const ASSERTIVE_CLAIM_RE =
366
  /\b(?:now\s+live|is\s+live|are\s+live|goes?\s+live|ships?|shipped|launch(?:es|ed)?|integrat(?:es|ed|ion)|support(?:s|ed|ing)?|available|ready\s+to\s+(?:trade|use|swap|bridge|deposit|withdraw|claim|ship|go)|settles?|reduces?|requires?|no\s+new\s+wallet|required|two\s+clicks?|markets?|account|passkey|first|only|fastest|largest|native)\b/i;
367
368
function extractAssertiveClaimSentences(text: string): string[] {
369
  return text
370
    .split(/(?<=[.!?])\s+|\n+/)
371
    .map((sentence) => sentence.trim())
372
    .filter((sentence) => sentence.length > 0)
373
    .filter((sentence) => ASSERTIVE_CLAIM_RE.test(sentence) || extractClaimNumbers(sentence).length > 0);
374
}
375
376
function isClaimSupportedByFacts(sentence: string, facts: string[]): boolean {
377
  return facts.some((fact) => factAppearsInText(sentence, fact) || factAppearsInText(fact, sentence));
378
}
379
380
function dedupeFailures(failures: RuleFailure[]): RuleFailure[] {
381
  const seen = new Set<string>();
382
  return failures.filter((failure) => {
383
    const key = `${failure.rule}:${failure.reason}`;
384
    if (seen.has(key)) return false;
385
    seen.add(key);
386
    return true;
387
  });
388
}
389
390
function factAppearsInText(text: string, fact: string): boolean {
391
  const normalizedText = normalizeForFactMatch(text);
392
  const normalizedFact = normalizeForFactMatch(fact);
393
  if (!normalizedFact) return true;
394
  if (normalizedText.includes(normalizedFact)) return true;
395
396
  const factTokens = meaningfulTokens(fact);
397
  if (factTokens.length < 3) return normalizedText.includes(normalizedFact);
398
  const textTokens = new Set(meaningfulTokens(text));
399
  const hits = factTokens.filter((token) => textTokens.has(token)).length;
400
  return hits / factTokens.length >= 0.55;
401
}
402
403
function normalizeForFactMatch(s: string): string {
404
  return s
405
    .toLowerCase()
406
    .replace(/['’]/g, "")
407
    .replace(/[^a-z0-9+.%]+/g, " ")
408
    .replace(/\s+/g, " ")
409
    .trim();
410
}
411
412
const STOPWORDS = new Set([
413
  "a",
414
  "an",
415
  "and",
416
  "are",
417
  "as",
418
  "at",
419
  "be",
420
  "for",
421
  "from",
422
  "in",
423
  "is",
424
  "it",
425
  "no",
426
  "of",
427
  "on",
428
  "or",
429
  "the",
430
  "to",
431
  "with",
432
]);
433
434
function meaningfulTokens(s: string): string[] {
435
  return normalizeForFactMatch(s)
436
    .split(" ")
437
    .filter((token) => token.length > 1 && !STOPWORDS.has(token));
438
}
439
440
// -- LAYER 2: beat-sequence audit + preparation hierarchy --------------------
441
442
/**
443
 * Naive paragraph-splitter — beats are paragraph-delimited by default.
444
 * Generator outputs use blank lines between beats.
445
 */
446
function splitIntoBeats(text: string): string[] {
447
  return text
448
    .split(/\n\s*\n+/)
449
    .map((p) => p.trim())
450
    .filter((p) => p.length > 0);
451
}
452
453
/**
454
 * Check preparation hierarchy across a beat sequence.
455
 *
456
 * Pulls the motor pair for each declared beat and runs
457
 * `checkPrepHierarchy` (Mirodan §1.7). Returns the indices of canonical
458
 * Sustained→Quick beats whose Quick action lacks its required Sustained prep.
459
 */
460
export function auditPrepHierarchy(
461
  beats: BeatSequence,
462
  voice: CharacterSpec = INFINEX_VOICE,
463
): PrepHierarchyFailure[] {
464
  // Build motor sequence — only canonical Sustained→Quick tempi contribute.
465
  // Both-Sustained tempi (Human, Warm, Acknowledged, Concealed, Doubting,
466
  // Certain) fire no Quick action — nothing to prep.
467
  // Both-Quick tempi (Materialistic, Cool, Acute, Uncertain, Unacknowledged,
468
  // Revealed) handle prep INTERNALLY in the character's inner state per
469
  // Mirodan §1.7 — the prep is invisible, not a beat-sequence concern.
470
  // Only Sus→Q tempi (Commanding, Practical, etc) carry the visible prep→release
471
  // arc that this audit enforces.
472
  const motors: WorkingAction[] = [];
473
  const motor_to_beat_index: number[] = [];
474
  beats.beats.forEach((b, i) => {
475
    if (!b.tempo) return; // no declared tempo (two-call path) → no prep-hierarchy concept
476
    const t = voice.tempi[b.tempo];
477
    if (!t) return;
478
    if (!isSustained(t.motor[0]) || !isQuick(t.motor[1])) return;
479
    motors.push(t.motor[0]); // sustained prep
480
    motor_to_beat_index.push(i);
481
    motors.push(t.motor[1]); // quick release
482
    motor_to_beat_index.push(i);
483
  });
484
485
  const failingMotorIndices = checkPrepHierarchy(motors);
486
  const failures: PrepHierarchyFailure[] = [];
487
  for (const motorIdx of failingMotorIndices) {
488
    const beatIdx = motor_to_beat_index[motorIdx] ?? -1;
489
    const motor = motors[motorIdx];
490
    if (!motor) continue;
491
    failures.push({
492
      beat_index: beatIdx,
493
      quick_action: motor as QuickAction,
494
      required_prep: prepFor(motor as QuickAction),
495
      found_prep: null,
496
    });
497
  }
498
  return failures;
499
}
500
501
function prepFor(q: QuickAction): SustainedAction {
502
  return ({ punching: "pressing", slashing: "wringing", dabbing: "gliding", flicking: "floating" } as const)[q];
503
}
504
505
/**
506
 * Blind-classify a paragraph by comparing it against every available tempo in
507
 * the selected voice. The declared beat is intentionally not an input.
508
 *
509
 * This is deterministic, not an LLM judge. It is weaker than Nigel's Sonnet
510
 * Track 5b classifier, but it has the same separation: generated intent is
511
 * checked by an independent classifier, not by the generator's rationale.
512
 */
513
export function classifyTempoBlind(
514
  paragraph: string,
515
  voice: CharacterSpec = INFINEX_VOICE,
516
): BlindTempoClassification {
517
  const normalized = normalizeForTempoMatch(paragraph);
518
  const textTokens = new Set(meaningfulTokens(paragraph));
519
  const scores: Partial<Record<TempoName, number>> = {};
520
521
  // Score only against main_tempi. Reserve / beat-only tempi are explicitly
522
  // out-of-rotation for primary classification — operator decision 2026-05-15.
523
  // A standalone paragraph that reads as a reserve tempo classifies as `unknown`.
524
  const mainTempoSet = new Set<TempoName>(voice.main_tempi);
525
  for (const [tempoName, tempo] of Object.entries(voice.tempi) as Array<[TempoName, NonNullable<CharacterSpec["tempi"][TempoName]>]>) {
526
    if (!tempo) continue;
527
    if (!mainTempoSet.has(tempoName)) continue;
528
    let score = 0;
529
530
    for (const anchor of tempo.vocab_anchor) {
531
      const normalizedAnchor = normalizeForTempoMatch(anchor);
532
      if (!normalizedAnchor) continue;
533
      if (normalized.includes(normalizedAnchor)) {
534
        score += anchor.includes(" ") ? 5 : 3;
535
        continue;
536
      }
537
      const anchorTokens = meaningfulTokens(anchor);
538
      const hits = anchorTokens.filter((token) => textTokens.has(token)).length;
539
      if (anchorTokens.length > 0) score += (hits / anchorTokens.length) * 1.5;
540
    }
541
542
    for (const shape of tempo.opening_shapes) {
543
      const normalizedShape = normalizeForTempoMatch(shape);
544
      if (normalizedShape && normalized.includes(normalizedShape)) score += 2;
545
    }
546
547
    for (const move of tempo.signoff_moves) {
548
      const normalizedMove = normalizeForTempoMatch(move);
549
      if (normalizedMove && normalized.includes(normalizedMove)) score += 2;
550
    }
551
552
    for (const motor of tempo.motor) {
553
      if (textTokens.has(motor)) score += 1.25;
554
    }
555
556
    const exampleTokens = new Set(tempo.example_lines.flatMap((line) => meaningfulTokens(line)));
557
    const overlap = Array.from(exampleTokens).filter((token) => textTokens.has(token)).length;
558
    if (exampleTokens.size > 0) {
559
      score += Math.min(4, (overlap / Math.min(exampleTokens.size, 30)) * 10);
560
    }
561
562
    if (score > 0) scores[tempoName] = Number(score.toFixed(3));
563
  }
564
565
  const ranked = Object.entries(scores)
566
    .map(([tempo, score]) => ({ tempo: tempo as TempoName, score: score ?? 0 }))
567
    .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);
568
  const top = ranked[0];
569
  if (!top || top.score < 1.5) {
570
    return { tempo: "unknown", confidence: 0, margin: 0, scores };
571
  }
572
573
  const runnerUp = ranked[1]?.score ?? 0;
574
  const margin = Number((top.score - runnerUp).toFixed(3));
575
  const confidence = Number(Math.min(0.99, top.score / (top.score + runnerUp + 4)).toFixed(3));
576
  if (confidence < 0.32 || margin < 0.5) {
577
    return { tempo: "unknown", confidence, margin, scores };
578
  }
579
580
  return { tempo: top.tempo, confidence, margin, scores };
581
}
582
583
function normalizeForTempoMatch(s: string): string {
584
  return normalizeForFactMatch(s);
585
}
586
587
/**
588
 * Audit a generated post's beats against the declared beat sequence.
589
 */
590
export function auditBeats(
591
  text: string,
592
  beats: BeatSequence,
593
  voice: CharacterSpec = INFINEX_VOICE,
594
): BeatAuditResult[] {
595
  const paragraphs = splitIntoBeats(text);
596
  const results: BeatAuditResult[] = [];
597
598
  for (let i = 0; i < beats.beats.length; i++) {
599
    const declared = beats.beats[i];
600
    if (!declared) continue;
601
    const paragraph = paragraphs[i];
602
    if (!paragraph) {
603
      results.push({
604
        beat_index: i,
605
        ...(declared.tempo !== undefined ? { declared_tempo: declared.tempo } : {}),
606
        passed: false,
607
        reason: "missing paragraph for declared beat",
608
      });
609
      continue;
610
    }
611
    // The generator no longer declares a tempo per beat (Mirodan: tempo
612
    // emerges from the verb under inner work; audience perceives it). If a
613
    // declared tempo IS present (legacy single-call path), surface it for
614
    // diagnostics but do not gate on it. The acceptance criterion is
615
    // membership in voice.tempi — i.e., is the classified tempo inside the
616
    // voice's locked palette?
617
    const classification = classifyTempoBlind(paragraph, voice);
618
    const inPlacement =
619
      classification.tempo !== "unknown" &&
620
      voice.tempi[classification.tempo] !== undefined;
621
    const passed = inPlacement;
622
    const result: BeatAuditResult = {
623
      beat_index: i,
624
      ...(declared.tempo !== undefined ? { declared_tempo: declared.tempo } : {}),
625
      classified_tempo: classification.tempo,
626
      classified_confidence: classification.confidence,
627
      passed,
628
    };
629
    if (!passed) {
630
      result.reason = `blind classifier read ${classification.tempo} — outside the voice's locked tempi (confidence ${classification.confidence}, margin ${classification.margin})`;
631
    } else if (declared.tempo && classification.tempo !== declared.tempo) {
632
      result.reason = `audience read ${classification.tempo} (legacy path declared ${declared.tempo})`;
633
    }
634
    results.push(result);
635
  }
636
637
  return results;
638
}
639
640
/**
641
 * Nigel-style LLM beat audit.
642
 *
643
 * This is the production-parity path: a separate Sonnet validator call reads
644
 * each beat blind to the generator's declared tempo, emits
645
 * `independent_classification`, and this glue compares declared vs classified.
646
 *
647
 * The deterministic `auditBeats()` above remains as a cheap fallback and for
648
 * unit tests; it should not be confused with Nigel's second-model validator.
649
 */
650
export async function auditBeatsLLM(
651
  text: string,
652
  beats: BeatSequence,
653
  opts: Omit<LLMVoiceAuditOptions, "declared_tempo"> = {},
654
): Promise<LLMBeatAuditResult[]> {
655
  return auditBeatsLLMInternal(text, beats, opts);
656
}
657
658
// -- hybrid (regex + LLM composition) ----------------------------------------
659
660
/**
661
 * Compose deterministic regex with the Sonnet LLM judge.
662
 *
663
 * Codex's review (2026-05-15) flagged that `auditTextLLM()` standalone may
664
 * rationalize generic slop ("game-changer", "next-gen", etc.) as
665
 * character-coherent because the decontaminated prompt forbids ban lists.
666
 *
667
 * Hybrid path: deterministic hygiene is the hard pre-filter. If a hygiene
668
 * rule or numeric/URL tripwire fails, we short-circuit. Candidates that
669
 * survive hygiene are sent to the LLM for character and semantic fact judgment.
670
 *
671
 * Regex catches taste-independent garbage. LLM catches character drift and
672
 * paraphrased fact support.
673
 */
674
export type HybridValidationVerdict = {
675
  passed: boolean;
676
  deterministic: ValidationResult;
677
  llm?: LLMVoiceAuditVerdict;
678
  /**
679
   * Top-line reason for the verdict — either the first regex rule that fired,
680
   * or a summary from the LLM judge, or `null` on pass.
681
   */
682
  reason: string | null;
683
};
684
685
export async function auditTextHybrid(
686
  text: string,
687
  opts: {
688
    voice?: CharacterSpec;
689
    beats?: BeatSequence;
690
    card?: ReleaseCard;
691
    deployed_facts_used?: string[];
692
    not_said?: NotSaidFact[];
693
    llm_opts?: Omit<LLMVoiceAuditOptions, "voice">;
694
  } = {},
695
): Promise<HybridValidationVerdict> {
696
  const detOpts: Parameters<typeof validate>[1] = {};
697
  if (opts.beats !== undefined) detOpts.beats = opts.beats;
698
  if (opts.voice !== undefined) detOpts.voice = opts.voice;
699
  if (opts.card !== undefined) detOpts.card = opts.card;
700
  if (opts.deployed_facts_used !== undefined) detOpts.deployed_facts_used = opts.deployed_facts_used;
701
  if (opts.not_said !== undefined) detOpts.not_said = opts.not_said;
702
  const deterministic = validate(text, detOpts);
703
704
  // Short-circuit on deterministic hygiene failure — don't pay for an LLM call.
705
  if (!deterministic.passed) {
706
    const first = deterministic.failures[0];
707
    return {
708
      passed: false,
709
      deterministic,
710
      reason: first ? `${first.rule}: ${first.reason}` : "deterministic-fail",
711
    };
712
  }
713
714
  // Regex clean — now ask the LLM judge.
715
  const llmOpts: LLMVoiceAuditOptions = { ...(opts.llm_opts ?? {}) };
716
  if (opts.voice !== undefined) llmOpts.voice = opts.voice;
717
  const llm = await auditTextLLM(text, llmOpts);
718
719
  if (!llm.passed) {
720
    return {
721
      passed: false,
722
      deterministic,
723
      llm,
724
      reason: llm.feedback || "llm-judge-fail",
725
    };
726
  }
727
728
  return {
729
    passed: true,
730
    deterministic,
731
    llm,
732
    reason: null,
733
  };
734
}
735
736
// -- composite ---------------------------------------------------------------
737
738
type Rule = { name: string; fn: (s: string) => RuleResult };
739
740
export const RULES: Rule[] = [
741
  { name: "cliches", fn: rejectCliches },
742
  { name: "listicle-voice", fn: rejectListicleVoice },
743
  { name: "antagonism", fn: rejectAntagonism },
744
  { name: "ai-slop", fn: rejectAIslop },
745
  { name: "kain-baggage", fn: rejectKainBaggage },
746
  { name: "claimed-palettes", fn: rejectClaimedPalettes },
747
  { name: "visual-slop", fn: rejectVisualSlop },
748
  // off-spec-drive handled in validate() — it's voice-dependent, can't be static
749
];
750
751
export function validate(
752
  s: string,
753
  opts: {
754
    beats?: BeatSequence;
755
    voice?: CharacterSpec;
756
    card?: ReleaseCard;
757
    deployed_facts_used?: string[];
758
    not_said?: NotSaidFact[];
759
    fact_contract?: FactContractMode;
760
  } = {},
761
): ValidationResult {
762
  const voice = opts.voice ?? INFINEX_VOICE;
763
  const failures: RuleFailure[] = [];
764
  for (const rule of RULES) {
765
    const r = rule.fn(s);
766
    if (!r.passed) failures.push({ rule: rule.name, reason: r.reason ?? "failed" });
767
  }
768
  // Voice-specific off-spec drive language check.
769
  const offSpec = rejectOffSpecDrive(s, voice);
770
  if (!offSpec.passed) failures.push({ rule: "off-spec-drive", reason: offSpec.reason ?? "failed" });
771
  if (opts.card && opts.fact_contract !== "off") {
772
    failures.push(...auditClaimTripwires(s, opts.card));
773
  }
774
  if (opts.card && opts.fact_contract === "strict") {
775
    const claimOpts: ClaimContractOptions = { card: opts.card };
776
    if (opts.deployed_facts_used !== undefined) {
777
      claimOpts.deployed_facts_used = opts.deployed_facts_used;
778
    }
779
    if (opts.not_said !== undefined) {
780
      claimOpts.not_said = opts.not_said;
781
    }
782
    failures.push(...auditClaimContract(s, claimOpts));
783
  }
784
785
  const result: ValidationResult = {
786
    passed: failures.length === 0,
787
    failures,
788
  };
789
790
  // Beat-sequence audit only when beats are declared.
791
  if (opts.beats) {
792
    const prep = auditPrepHierarchy(opts.beats, voice);
793
    const beatAudit = auditBeats(s, opts.beats, voice);
794
    if (prep.length > 0) {
795
      result.prep_hierarchy_failures = prep;
796
      for (const p of prep) {
797
        failures.push({
798
          rule: "prep-hierarchy",
799
          reason: `beat ${p.beat_index} fires ${p.quick_action} without ${p.required_prep} prep — degrades to ${p.required_prep}`,
800
        });
801
      }
802
      result.passed = false;
803
    }
804
    const beatFailures = beatAudit.filter((b) => !b.passed);
805
    if (beatFailures.length > 0) {
806
      result.beat_audit = beatAudit;
807
      for (const b of beatFailures) {
808
        failures.push({
809
          rule: "beat-tempo-fit",
810
          reason: `beat ${b.beat_index} (declared ${b.declared_tempo}): ${b.reason ?? "tempo mismatch"}`,
811
        });
812
      }
813
      result.passed = false;
814
    } else {
815
      result.beat_audit = beatAudit;
816
    }
817
  }
818
819
  return result;
820
}
821

src/voice/infinex.ts

457 lines
1
/**
2
 * Infinex voice spec — locked 2026-05-12. Character image refined 2026-05-19.
3
 *
4
 * Character image:  the banker-turned-crypto trailblazer — knows the old world,
5
 *                   found the better way, decisions already taken, now mapping.
6
 *                   Literary anchors: Werle (Ibsen's Wild Duck), the Duke
7
 *                   (Measure for Measure). Mirodan: "decisions already taken;
8
 *                   it is only a matter of pursuing the chosen course to the
9
 *                   end" (vol 2, p. 489 — Stable baseline definition).
10
 *
11
 * Inner Attitude:  Stable
12
 * Stress:          Flow (bound pole — TBD pending character lock)
13
 * Aspect:          Penetrating
14
 * Drive:           Primary Spell, secondary Doing, visible axis Spell → Vision (Diagram D)
15
 *
16
 * Five main tempi in rotation: Commanding · Practical · Sombre · Irradiant · Sociable.
17
 * Posts arc through tempi as beat sequences (3-4 beats typical), not single-tempo monoliths.
18
 *
19
 * Off-spec: visible/extravert Passion: urgency theater, "act now" /
20
 * "last chance" / "today only" language. Passion is present as hidden
21
 * lining in Diagram D; it fails when it surfaces as the main projection.
22
 *
23
 * Sources:
24
 *   - memory/infinex-character-image-banker-trailblazer.md (the locked image)
25
 *   - memory/infinex-drive-spell-not-passion.md
26
 *   - memory/infinex-5-tempi-locked.md
27
 *   - memory/infinex-brand-laban-frame.md
28
 *   - research/language-pad.html (36-sample A/B + multi-tempo arc example)
29
 *   - research/infinex-character-sheet.md (colleague-facing distillation)
30
 *   - skills/laban-voice-for-ai-agents/ (framework + reference)
31
 */
32
33
import type { CharacterSpec, Tempo, TempoName } from "./types.js";
34
35
// -- The 12 tempi available to Stable + Flow-stressed character ---------------
36
// Mirodan reference: §3.1 (Stable variations), §3.2 (Adream outer variations),
37
// §3.5 (Remote outer variations). Numbers from laban-mirodan-reference-2026-04-28.md.
38
39
const TEMPI: Partial<Record<TempoName, Tempo>> = {
40
  // -- 5 main tempi ---------------------------------------------------------
41
42
  commanding: {
43
    name: "commanding",
44
    attitude: "stable",
45
    inner_combo: "Stable · Strong/Direct",
46
    motor: ["pressing", "punching"],
47
    feel: "Sustained pressure into decisive landing. Locked, decisive, no ornament. The institutional drop. Reads as a ruler issuing a position.",
48
    opening_shapes: [
49
      "Today: <fact>.",
50
      "<fact> is live.",
51
      "We've <verb>ed <noun>.",
52
    ],
53
    vocab_anchor: ["live", "today", "shipped", "open", "ready", "now available"],
54
    signoff_moves: [
55
      "<destination url>",
56
      "<no signoff — the fact ended it>",
57
    ],
58
    example_lines: [
59
      "Today: spot Hyperliquid is live in Infinex. Same account, same passkey, the orderbook where your portfolio already lives.",
60
      "Private Send beta is live for all users. Send crypto. Without exposing your financial history.",
61
    ],
62
    lining: "Certainty earned before the announcement. The drop is calm because the work is done — the banker-trailblazer has known this would ship since before the reader knew to want it. The Outer is the position; the Lining is the absence of performance.",
63
  },
64
65
  practical: {
66
    name: "practical",
67
    attitude: "stable",
68
    inner_combo: "Stable · Strong/Flexible",
69
    motor: ["wringing", "slashing"],
70
    feel: "Working through complexity into a carved answer. Comfortable with tradeoffs, willing to take you through the reasoning. Karpathy's essay mode. Long-form.",
71
    opening_shapes: [
72
      "Here's what's interesting about <X>:",
73
      "We keep coming back to <Y>.",
74
      "<fact>, and it's worth being precise about what changed.",
75
    ],
76
    vocab_anchor: [
77
      "tradeoff", "the actual question is", "this only makes sense once you",
78
      "we held it back until", "the hard part was", "what doesn't exist:",
79
    ],
80
    signoff_moves: [
81
      "<open question list>",
82
      "<single declarative — the line they will quote>",
83
    ],
84
    example_lines: [
85

87

88

90
91

96
    feel: "Weight-of-conviction. Slow. Carries gravity. Used when the thesis lands with effort — 'we know this is coming and it's a lot.' Werle/Duke energy. Pairs with Commanding as the Pressing prep before a Punching release.",

111

118

  irradiant: {

  diffused: {

    feel: "Quiet vision-arrival. Used for post-launch reflection — the mood after the boat actually crosses the river. Not the launch itself; the day after.",
