240
    vocab_anchor: ["abstract nouns, no warmth"],
241
    signoff_moves: ["<none — the post ends mid-thought>"],
242
    example_lines: [
243
      "Yield without an agent is a hobby.",
244
    ],
245
    lining: "The discipline of refusing the genre. The Outer is the cold line-in-sand; the Lining is 'I will not pretend the bad thing is the good thing — the line stays where it is.' Contempt earned and then cooled into discipline, never bouffon-rage.",
246
  },
247
248
  altruistic: {
249
    name: "altruistic",
250
    attitude: "remote",
251
    inner_combo: "Remote outer · Flexible/Free",
252
    motor: ["floating", "flicking"],
253
    feel: "Warm-distant. Architect speaking to future inhabitants of a building still being finished. 'For you' framing.",
254
    opening_shapes: ["We've been building <X> so <you> can <Y>."],
255
    vocab_anchor: ["for", "so that", "so you can stop", "you wouldn't have to"],
256
    signoff_moves: ["<hand-off to reader>", "<that's the entire flow>"],
257
    example_lines: [
258
      "We've been building Swidge so you can switch into a new L2 without thinking about how. MegaETH is the latest one in.",
259
    ],
260
    lining: "The architect speaking past her own finishing. The Outer is the for-you hand-off; the Lining is 'you don't have to thank me for this — you have to use it.' Warmth without ego, the work transferred outward.",
261
  },
262
263
  unsociable: {
264
    name: "unsociable",
265
    attitude: "remote",
266
    inner_combo: "Remote outer · Flexible/Bound",
267
    motor: ["wringing", "slashing"],
268
    feel: "Withdrawal-as-discipline. Doesn't argue the discourse, just leaves it. Sharper than Egocentric because it acknowledges the noise exists. Best paired with a separate Sociable post the same day.",
269
    opening_shapes: ["While the timeline argues about <X>, <Y>."],
270
    vocab_anchor: ["meanwhile", "elsewhere", "we'll be over here"],
271
    signoff_moves: ["<the post is the signoff>"],
272
    example_lines: [
273
      "While the timeline debates which L2 has the best meta-narrative, Swidge now bridges into MegaETH from any chain. We'll see you when you actually want to use it.",
274
    ],
275
    lining: "The refusal to argue downstream of the work. The Outer is the withdrawal-as-discipline; the Lining is 'the discourse is downstream of the work — I'm staying upstream.' Sharper than Egocentric because it acknowledges the noise exists, then declines.",
276
  },
277
};
278
279
const MIRODAN_KERNEL = `
280
1. Laban names four Motion Factors: Weight (Strong/Light), Time (Sudden/Sustained), Space (Direct/Indirect), and Flow (Bound/Free). Mirodan uses these as mechanical inputs for character, not mood labels.
281
2. Only three Inner Attitudes can be character baselines: Stable (Weight + Space), Adream (Weight + Flow), and Near (Weight + Time). Mobile, Remote, and Awake are outer Action Attitudes only.
282
3. Stress is the third active Motion Factor that makes the baseline visible in action. Weight is in every baseline pair, so Weight is never the stress for Stable / Adream / Near.
283
4. Legal stresses are the two Motion Factors not already in the baseline: Stable can take Time or Flow; Adream can take Time or Space; Near can take Space or Flow.
284
5. Aspect names the dominant Inner Participation inside the baseline: Enclosing = Weight/Intending, Penetrating = Space/Attending, Circumscribing = Time/Deciding, Radiating = Flow/Adapting.
285
6. The eight Working Actions combine Weight x Space x Time: Pressing, Wringing, Gliding, Floating, Punching, Slashing, Dabbing, Flicking. Sustained/Quick are Time poles inside those actions, not separate action types.
286
7. Every Quick Working Action requires its matching Sustained Working Action as preparation. The motor pairs are Pressing -> Punching, Wringing -> Slashing, Gliding -> Dabbing, and Floating -> Flicking. Without preparation, the Quick action reads flat or degrades.
287
8. Drive is derived by table lookup from Inner Attitude x Aspect x Stress. Each table row is an X-diagram with four slots: primary, secondary, introvert, extravert.
288
9. The Main Character-Action Axis is primary -> extravert. The axis is a structural read, not a playable verb.
289
10. Subdued, hidden, or introvert does not mean absent. A drive fails only when it surfaces in the wrong slot for the character, especially as the visible/extravert projection.
290
11. A post is a beat sequence, not a single-register block. The model writes transitive action beats; the audience reads the tempo afterward from the motor pair, drive, and Outer/Lining tension.
291
`.trim();
292
293
const MIRODAN_DRIVE_TABLE = `
294
key = "<inner_attitude>|<aspect>|<stress>"
295
296
X-diagram slot definitions:
297
primary = bottom-left = Inner Character Drive / dominant formative drive / resting character energy
298
secondary = bottom-right = Outer Character Drive / secondary formative drive
299
introvert = top-left = Main Inner Action Drive / hidden lining
300
extravert = top-right = Main Outer Action Drive / visible projection
301
main_axis = primary -> extravert = Main Character-Action Axis
302
303
STABLE (formative drives: doing + spell)
304
stable|enclosing|time -> primary=doing, secondary=spell, introvert=vision, extravert=passion
305
stable|penetrating|time -> primary=doing, secondary=spell, introvert=passion, extravert=vision
306
stable|enclosing|flow -> primary=spell, secondary=doing, introvert=vision, extravert=passion
307
stable|penetrating|flow -> primary=spell, secondary=doing, introvert=passion, extravert=vision (INFINEX / Diagram D)
308
309
ADREAM (formative drives: passion + spell)
310
adream|enclosing|space -> primary=spell, secondary=passion, introvert=vision, extravert=doing
311
adream|radiating|space -> primary=spell, secondary=passion, introvert=doing, extravert=vision
312
adream|enclosing|time -> primary=passion, secondary=spell, introvert=vision, extravert=doing
313
adream|radiating|time -> primary=passion, secondary=spell, introvert=doing, extravert=vision
314
315
NEAR (formative drives: doing + passion)
316
near|circumscribing|space -> primary=doing, secondary=passion, introvert=vision, extravert=spell
317
near|enclosing|space -> primary=doing, secondary=passion, introvert=spell, extravert=vision
318
near|circumscribing|flow -> primary=passion, secondary=doing, introvert=vision, extravert=spell
319
near|enclosing|flow -> primary=passion, secondary=doing, introvert=spell, extravert=vision
320
`.trim();
321
322
// -- Off-spec language: Time-pressure / Passion-as-surface markers ------------
323
// Passion is Infinex's hidden lining. Time-pressure phrases are off-spec when
324
// they make Passion the visible/extravert projection.
325
326
const OFF_SPEC_REGEXES = [
327
  {
328
    name: "time-pressure",
329
    re: /\b(act\s+(?:now|fast)|hurry|last\s+chance|don't\s+miss|limited\s+time|only\s+\d+\s+(?:hours?|minutes?|days?)\s+left|tick\s+tock|while\s+supplies\s+last|today\s+only|right\s+now)\b/i,
330
    reason: "time-pressure phrase surfaces Passion as visible projection — off-spec for Infinex's Spell→Vision axis",
331
  },
332
  {
333
    name: "fomo-urgency",
334
    re: /\b(FOMO|missing\s+out|don't\s+sleep\s+on|catch\s+up\s+before|before\s+everyone\s+else)\b/i,
335
    reason: "FOMO/urgency markers — Infinex doesn't run on scarcity-of-attention",
336
  },
337
  {
338
    name: "hype-theatre",
339
    re: /\b(buckle\s+up|let's\s+go!?|wagmi|gm\s+gm|massive(?:\s+(?:news|update))?|huge(?:\s+(?:news|update))?|crazy(?:\s+(?:news|update))?)\b/i,
340
    reason: "hype-theatre vocabulary — Infinex is Spell-driven, not Passion-driven",
341
  },
342
];
343
344
// -- Cadence (approximate distribution across main tempi) ---------------------
345
346
const CADENCE: Partial<Record<TempoName, number>> = {
347
  irradiant: 0.45,
348
  commanding: 0.22,
349
  sombre: 0.18,
350
  sociable: 0.1,
351
  practical: 0.05,
352
};
353
354
// -- Full character spec ------------------------------------------------------
355
356
export const INFINEX_VOICE: CharacterSpec = {
357
  name: "infinex",
358
  inner_attitude: "stable",
359
  stress: "flow",
360
  stress_pole: "bound", // TBD — pending character lock; matches Werle/Duke precedent
361
  aspect: "penetrating",
362
  drive_primary: "spell",
363
  drive_secondary: "doing",
364
  drive_introvert: "passion",
365
  drive_extravert: "vision",
366
  drive_axis: "Spell → Vision",
367
  drive_table_cell: "stable|penetrating|flow",
368
  off_spec_drives: ["passion"], // shorthand: reject Passion as visible/extravert surface
369
  off_spec_regexes: OFF_SPEC_REGEXES,
370
  tempi: TEMPI,
371
  main_tempi: ["commanding", "practical", "sombre", "irradiant", "sociable"],
372
  beat_only_tempi: [
373
    "self-contained",
374
    "receptive",
375
    "overpowering",
376
    "diffused",
377
    "egocentric",
378
    "altruistic",
379
    "unsociable",
380
  ],
381
  cadence: CADENCE,
382
  character_image:
383
    "the banker-turned-crypto trailblazer — knows the old world, found the better way, decisions already taken, now mapping",
384
  literary_anchors: ["Werle (Ibsen, Wild Duck)", "the Duke (Measure for Measure)"],
385
  mirodan_kernel: MIRODAN_KERNEL,
386
  drive_table: MIRODAN_DRIVE_TABLE,
387
  structural_traits: [
388
    "Character image: the banker-turned-crypto trailblazer — knows the old world, found the better way, decisions already taken, now mapping. Literary anchors: Werle (Wild Duck) and the Duke (Measure for Measure). The voice fires from this archetype, NOT from generic 'wise old guard' abstraction or growth-marketer announcement-voice.",
389
    "No listicle openers ('N reasons', 'why X matters', 'the only X you'll ever need'). The banker-trailblazer character does not perform headline-bait shapes — Infinex is not BuzzFeed.",
390
    "No antagonism toward named competitors. The character is Stable + Penetrating — direct addressing without brawling. Past Infinex over-indexed on antagonism; the current placement explicitly leaves that behind.",
391
  ],
392
  super_objective:
393
    "to take responsibility for the tech, so the user only has to want",
394
  super_objective_examples: [
395
    "Swidge: the user wanted to be on a new chain. The bridge, the gas, the DEX — none of that was theirs to figure out.",
396
    "Spot Hyperliquid in Infinex: the user wanted to trade spot. The venue, the deposit, the withdrawal — none of that was theirs to learn.",
397
    "Passkey-portable accounts: the user wanted to use the product on a new phone. The seed phrase wasn't theirs to remember.",
398
  ],
399
  historical_lore:
400
    "Past Infinex manufactured user attention via incentive theater — Craterun, Yaprun, the post that named bot competitors as 'terrorists' which real users took personally. The current character has learned this was the wrong work. The new posture is the inverse: take responsibility for the tech, never demand the attention. The scars hold the discipline for everyone who came after. Never name the era in prose; let it inform every refusal of a hype move.",
401
  validation_criterion:
402
    "one real user discovers the product without team-shilling or incentives, finds it fills a real need, uses it. If we have to manufacture this user, we are failing the Super-Objective.",
403
};
404
405
// -- Helpers ------------------------------------------------------------------
406
407
export function getTempo(name: TempoName): Tempo {
408
  const t = INFINEX_VOICE.tempi[name];
409
  if (!t) throw new Error(`unknown tempo: ${name}`);
410
  return t;
411
}
412
413
/**
414
 * Default beat sequence for a release card kind. The generator falls back to
415
 * these when no beats[] is supplied. Each sequence is the canonical "good
416
 * shape" for that card kind — drawn from the language-pad example arcs.
417
 */
418
export function defaultBeatsForKind(kind: string): { tempo: TempoName; hint?: string }[] {
419
  switch (kind) {
420
    case "launch-tier":
421
      // Multi-beat arc: Sombre opens (Pressing prep) → Commanding lands →
422
      // Practical justifies → Irradiant lifts.
423
      return [
424
        { tempo: "sombre", hint: "set up the wall we are taking down — Pressing prep" },
425
        { tempo: "commanding", hint: "land the fact — Punching release, fed by Sombre prep" },
426
        { tempo: "practical", hint: "justify the build decision — Wringing/Slashing" },
427
        { tempo: "irradiant", hint: "future-tense lift — Floating/Flicking" },
428
      ];
429
    case "data-card-official":
430
      // Shorter arc: Sombre prep → Commanding → Irradiant.
431
      return [
432
        { tempo: "sombre", hint: "Pressing prep" },
433
        { tempo: "commanding", hint: "the fact" },
434
        { tempo: "irradiant", hint: "future-state close" },
435
      ];
436
    case "data-card-wry":
437
      // Irradiant-led with a wry close.
438
      return [
439
        { tempo: "irradiant", hint: "future-state opener" },
440
        { tempo: "commanding", hint: "the fact, dry" },
441
      ];
442
    case "split":
443
      // Practical-led: explain the semantic split + Irradiant lift.
444
      return [
445
        { tempo: "practical", hint: "explain the split's meaning" },
446
        { tempo: "commanding", hint: "land the fact" },
447
        { tempo: "irradiant", hint: "where this points" },
448
      ];
449
    default:
450
      return [
451
        { tempo: "sombre" },
452
        { tempo: "commanding" },
453
        { tempo: "irradiant" },
454
      ];
455
  }
456
}
457

src/voice/types.ts

275 lines
1
/**
2
 * Laban-Mirodan voice types — generic framework, brand-agnostic.
3
 *
4
 * Used by the generator (beats[] input) and the validator (beat-tempo audit +
5
 * preparation-hierarchy guard). The framework comes from Veronica Mirodan's
6
 * 1997 PhD synthesis of Yat Malmgren's adaptation of Laban Movement Analysis.
7
 *
8
 * See skills/laban-voice-for-ai-agents/ for the full reference.
9
 */
10
11
// The 8 Working Actions — body of every visible motor.
12
// Sustained = introvert / preparation. Quick = extravert / release.
13
export const SUSTAINED_ACTIONS = ["pressing", "wringing", "gliding", "floating"] as const;
14
export const QUICK_ACTIONS = ["punching", "slashing", "dabbing", "flicking"] as const;
15
export type SustainedAction = (typeof SUSTAINED_ACTIONS)[number];
16
export type QuickAction = (typeof QUICK_ACTIONS)[number];
17
export type WorkingAction = SustainedAction | QuickAction;
18
19
// Preparation hierarchy (Mirodan §1.7, p. 347):
20
// Every Quick action requires its Sustained partner as prep.
21
// Without prep, the Quick action degrades to its Sustained version.
22
export const PREPARATION_PAIRS: Record<QuickAction, SustainedAction> = {
23
  punching: "pressing",
24
  slashing: "wringing",
25
  dabbing: "gliding",
26
  flicking: "floating",
27
};
28
29
// The 6 Inner Attitudes — but only 3 (Stable, Adream, Near) can be character
30
// baselines. Mobile, Remote, Awake are Action Attitudes only — they fire as
31
// outer projections of a baseline under stress, never as a resting state.
32
export const BASELINE_ATTITUDES = ["stable", "adream", "near"] as const;
33
export const OUTER_ONLY_ATTITUDES = ["mobile", "remote", "awake"] as const;
34
export type BaselineAttitude = (typeof BASELINE_ATTITUDES)[number];
35
export type OuterOnlyAttitude = (typeof OUTER_ONLY_ATTITUDES)[number];
36
export type InnerAttitude = BaselineAttitude | OuterOnlyAttitude;
37
38
// Stress factors. Each baseline has TWO available stresses (the two Motion
39
// Factors NOT in its inner pair). Stress carries a status grammar:
40
//   time-stress  = adolescent / lower / immature
41
//   space-stress = mature / higher / sophisticated
42
//   flow-stress  = emotional / sociable
43
//   weight-stress only fires on Mobile/Remote/Awake outers.
44
export type Stress = "time" | "space" | "flow" | "weight";
45
46
// Aspects — which Inner Participation is emphasized.
47
// Fixed per character; determines psychological type.
48
export type Aspect =
49
  | "enclosing" // Intending emphasized
50
  | "penetrating" // Attending emphasized
51
  | "circumscribing" // Deciding emphasized
52
  | "radiating"; // Adapting emphasized
53
54
// Drives — the four structural energies/axes a character runs on.
55
// Mirodan: Doing, Spell, Passion, Vision.
56
//   Time-axis activates Passion.
57
//   Flow-axis activates Spell on Stable; Adream Space-stressed mixes Spell+Passion.
58
// Drive lock determines which language families are off-spec as visible surfaces.
59
export type Drive = "doing" | "spell" | "passion" | "vision";
60
61
// The 24 named Tempi across all 6 Inner Attitudes.
62
// Each baseline (Stable, Adream, Near) has its own 4 internal-variation tempi.
63
// The 3 outer-only attitudes (Mobile, Remote, Awake) also have 4 tempi each,
64
// but these fire as OUTER PROJECTIONS of a baseline under stress — never as a
65
// resting state. Which 12 of the 24 are available to a given character depends
66
// on (Inner Attitude + Stress) — see references/working-actions.md.
67
export type StableTempo =
68
  | "commanding" // Strong/Direct — Pressing→Punching
69
  | "practical" // Strong/Flexible — Wringing→Slashing
70
  | "self-contained" // Light/Direct — Gliding→Dabbing
71
  | "receptive"; // Light/Flexible — Floating→Flicking
72
73
export type AdreamOuterTempo =
74
  | "sombre" // Strong/Bound — Pressing→Punching (bound flow)
75
  | "overpowering" // Strong/Free — Wringing→Slashing
76
  | "diffused" // Light/Bound — Gliding→Dabbing
77
  | "irradiant"; // Light/Free — Floating→Flicking
78
79
export type NearTempo =
80
  | "materialistic" // Strong/Quick — Punching→Slashing (sudden aggressive intent)
81
  | "human" // Light/Sustained — Floating→Gliding (slow tenderness)
82
  | "warm" // Strong/Sustained — Pressing→Wringing (warm consent)
83
  | "cool"; // Light/Quick — Dabbing→Flicking (pert intimacy)
84
85
export type MobileOuterTempo =
86
  // Mobile = outer of Adream-Time or Near-Flow only. Never baseline.
87
  | "unacknowledged" // Quick/Bound — Punching/Slashing (self-concealed aggression)
88
  | "acknowledged" // Sustained/Free — Floating/Gliding (easy disclosure)
89
  | "revealed" // Quick/Free — Dabbing/Flicking (sudden disclosure)
90
  | "concealed"; // Sustained/Bound — Pressing/Wringing (withheld weight)
91
92
export type RemoteOuterTempo =
93
  // Remote = outer of Adream-Space or Stable-Flow only. Never baseline.
94
  | "egocentric" // Direct/Bound — Pressing→Punching
95
  | "unsociable" // Flexible/Bound — Wringing→Slashing
96
  | "sociable" // Direct/Free — Gliding→Dabbing
97
  | "altruistic"; // Flexible/Free — Floating→Flicking
98
99
export type AwakeOuterTempo =
100
  // Awake = outer of Stable-Time or Near-Space only. Never baseline.
101
  | "acute" // Direct/Quick — Punching→Dabbing (acute idea-decision)
102
  | "doubting" // Flexible/Sustained — Floating→Wringing (slowly dawning doubt)
103
  | "certain" // Direct/Sustained — Pressing→Gliding (certain awareness)
104
  | "uncertain"; // Flexible/Quick — Flicking→Slashing (sudden new idea)
105
106
export type TempoName =
107
  | StableTempo
108
  | AdreamOuterTempo
109
  | NearTempo
110
  | MobileOuterTempo
111
  | RemoteOuterTempo
112
  | AwakeOuterTempo;
113
114
export const ALL_TEMPO_NAMES = [
115
  "commanding",
116
  "practical",
117
  "self-contained",
118
  "receptive",
119
  "sombre",
120
  "overpowering",
121
  "diffused",
122
  "irradiant",
123
  "materialistic",
124
  "human",
125
  "warm",
126
  "cool",
127
  "unacknowledged",
128
  "acknowledged",
129
  "revealed",
130
  "concealed",
131
  "egocentric",
132
  "unsociable",
133
  "sociable",
134
  "altruistic",
135
  "acute",
136
  "doubting",
137
  "certain",
138
  "uncertain",
139
] as const satisfies readonly TempoName[];
140
141
export interface Tempo {
142
  name: TempoName;
143
  attitude: InnerAttitude;
144
  inner_combo: string; // human-readable: "Stable · Strong/Direct"
145
  // Motor pair: two Working Actions characterizing this tempo's signature.
146
  // For Stable/Adream/Remote tempi: Sustained → Quick (preparation → release pattern).
147
  // For Near tempi: can be both-Sustained, both-Quick, or Sus→Q depending on Time-pole.
148
  // For Mobile/Awake tempi: similar — depends on Stress-pole.
149
  // Prep-hierarchy enforcement (validator) applies only when motor[0] is Sustained
150
  // AND motor[1] is Quick (the Sus→Q prep→release case).
151
  motor: [WorkingAction, WorkingAction];
152
  feel: string;
153
  opening_shapes: string[];
154
  vocab_anchor: string[];
155
  signoff_moves: string[];
156
  example_lines: string[]; // few-shot fragments for generator
157
  // Per-tempo Lining (Mirodan §7.1, pp. 554-555). The hidden Inner Action that
158
  // secretly drives the visible Outer of this tempo. Outer/Lining gap is the
159
  // engine of dramatic life (§7.2). Authoring discipline: never name the
160
  // Lining in the prose — write the Outer, leak the Lining (§7.3).
161
  // Optional so legacy voices keep working; promote to required once all
162
  // voices have linings declared.
163
  lining?: string;
164
}
165
166
export interface CharacterSpec {
167
  name: string; // "infinex"
168
  inner_attitude: BaselineAttitude;
169
  stress: Stress;
170
  stress_pole?: "bound" | "free" | "strong" | "light" | "direct" | "flexible" | "quick" | "sustained";
171
  aspect: Aspect;
172
  drive_primary: Drive;
173
  drive_secondary: Drive;
174
  drive_introvert?: Drive;
175
  drive_extravert?: Drive;
176
  drive_axis?: string; // "Spell -> Vision" etc.
177
  drive_table_cell?: string; // "<inner>|<aspect>|<stress>", e.g. "stable|penetrating|flow"
178
  off_spec_drives: Drive[]; // shorthand for drives that should NOT surface as visible/extravert projection
179
  off_spec_regexes: { name: string; re: RegExp; reason: string }[];
180
  tempi: Partial<Record<TempoName, Tempo>>; // 12 of 24 tempi available to this character (the rest are out-of-placement)
181
  main_tempi: TempoName[]; // tempi in rotation
182
  beat_only_tempi: TempoName[]; // tempi available but rarely
183
  cadence: Partial<Record<TempoName, number>>; // approximate distribution, sums to 1.0 for main_tempi
184
  character_image?: string;
185
  literary_anchors?: string[];
186
  mirodan_kernel?: string;
187
  drive_table?: string;
188
  /**
189
   * Character-derived STRUCTURAL constraints the generator should respect.
190
   * NOT a hardcoded slop / allergen list — those are off-spec and handled by
191
   * the validator's deterministic rules. These are framework-level shape
192
   * traits that follow from the placement (e.g. "No listicle openers" for a
193
   * Stable + Flow-stressed character — Wise-Old-Guard doesn't write BuzzFeed).
194
   *
195
   * Each entry should be a complete sentence the generator can include verbatim
196
   * in its system prompt's "Structural constraints" section. Reasoning should
197
   * cite the character placement, not operator taste.
198
   */
199
  structural_traits?: string[];
200
201
  /**
202
   * Super-Objective (Stanislavski/Mirodan inheritance, pp. 282-306, §13).
203
   * What the character ALWAYS wants — the standing through-action across the
204
   * whole arc. A transitive verb phrase ("to <verb> <object>"), not an
205
   * adjective. Generator prompts cite this to give the character intent before
206
   * each release, so beats arc toward something rather than restating mechanics.
207
   *
208
   * Examples are 2-3 worked applications: how the Super-Objective manifests
209
   * in concrete past releases. Teaches the model the pattern by demonstration.
210
   *
211
   * Optional so legacy voices keep working; promote to required once all
212
   * voices declare it.
213
   */
214
  super_objective?: string;
215
  super_objective_examples?: string[];
216
217
  /**
218
   * Historical lore — character backstory / scar tissue.
219
   * The version of the character that no longer is. Past learnings the
220
   * current placement has internalized. Encoded so the model knows what the
221
   * character has *moved past* and refuses to do — without naming the era
222
   * in prose. Same shape as Nigel's "bouffon-line" pattern (the wound
223
   * dismissed long enough ago that it now reads as discipline).
224
   *
225
   * Never appears in prose; only informs refusal of off-spec moves.
226
   */
227
  historical_lore?: string;
228
229
  /**
230
   * Validation criterion — operational test the character is trying to pass.
231
   * NOT the Super-Objective (that's the standing intent); this is the
232
   * observable success condition. Used by the audit layer to score whether
233
   * a given artifact would help or hurt this condition.
234
   *
235
   * Example for Infinex (CTO frame): one real user discovers the product
236
   * unprompted, no incentives, fills a need.
237
   */
238
  validation_criterion?: string;
239
}
240
241
// Beat sequence — what the generator consumes per release card.
242
// A post is N beats; each beat is a paragraph. The two-call path does not
243
// declare a tempo per beat (Mirodan: tempo emerges from the verb under the
244
// inner work; the audience perceives it; the validator scores it post-hoc).
245
// The legacy single-call path still supplies tempo explicitly, so tempo
246
// stays available — just optional.
247
export interface TempoBeat {
248
  tempo?: TempoName;
249
  hint?: string; // optional guidance for that specific beat
250
}
251
252
export interface BeatSequence {
253
  beats: TempoBeat[];
254
  enforce_prep_hierarchy?: boolean; // default true
255
}
256
257
// Validation result types — extending validator.ts shape for beat-sequence
258
// audits. The base validator returns per-rule failures; the beat-sequence
259
// validator returns per-beat failures.
260
export interface BeatAuditResult {
261
  beat_index: number;
262
  declared_tempo?: TempoName;
263
  classified_tempo?: TempoName | "unknown";
264
  classified_confidence?: number;
265
  passed: boolean;
266
  reason?: string;
267
}
268
269
export interface PrepHierarchyFailure {
270
  beat_index: number;
271
  quick_action: QuickAction;
272
  required_prep: SustainedAction;
273
  found_prep: SustainedAction | null;
274
}
275

src/voice/laban.ts

178 lines
1
/**
2
 * Laban-Mirodan framework constants — generic, brand-agnostic.
3
 *
4
 * The mapping rules below are mechanical: per Mirodan, a baseline + stress
5
 * combination deterministically produces the outer Action Attitudes available.
6
 *
7
 * Used by the validator (preparation hierarchy) and by the skill's interview
8
 * flow (mapping user answers to a CharacterSpec).
9
 */
10
11
import { PREPARATION_PAIRS } from "./types.js";
12
import type {
13
  BaselineAttitude,
14
  Drive,
15
  InnerAttitude,
16
  OuterOnlyAttitude,
17
  QuickAction,
18
  Stress,
19
  SustainedAction,
20
  WorkingAction,
21
} from "./types.js";
22
23
// Available stresses per baseline (Mirodan §4.1).
24
// A baseline character's two available stresses are the two Motion Factors
25
// NOT in its inner pair.
26
//   Stable inner = Weight + Space → stresses: Time or Flow
27
//   Adream inner = Weight + Flow  → stresses: Time or Space
28
//   Near   inner = Weight + Time  → stresses: Space or Flow
29
export const AVAILABLE_STRESSES: Record<BaselineAttitude, readonly Stress[]> = {
30
  stable: ["time", "flow"],
31
  adream: ["time", "space"],
32
  near: ["space", "flow"],
33
};
34
35
// Outer Action Attitudes produced by (baseline + stress) per Mirodan §4.1.
36
//   Stable + Time → Near or Awake
37
//   Stable + Flow → Adream or Remote
38
//   Adream + Time → Near or Mobile  (the "Adream split" — §4.5)
39
//   Adream + Space → Stable or Remote
40
//   Near + Space → Stable or Awake
41
//   Near + Flow → Adream or Mobile
42
export const OUTER_PROJECTIONS: Record<
43
  BaselineAttitude,
44
  Record<Stress, readonly InnerAttitude[]>
45
> = {
46
  stable: {
47
    time: ["near", "awake"],
48
    flow: ["adream", "remote"],
49
    space: [],
50
    weight: [],
51
  },
52
  adream: {
53
    time: ["near", "mobile"],
54
    space: ["stable", "remote"],
55
    flow: [],
56
    weight: [],
57
  },
58
  near: {
59
    space: ["stable", "awake"],
60
    flow: ["adream", "mobile"],
61
    time: [],
62
    weight: [],
63
  },
64
};
65
66
// Drive sub-diagrams per Mirodan §2 + §3.
67
// Each character (Inner Attitude + Stress + Aspect) maps to a Drive axis pair.
68
// Critical for Drive-lock: knowing which axis is visible tells you which
69
// language families are off-spec as surface projection.
70
//
71
// Time-stress always activates Passion (and removes Vision-only paths).
72
// Flow-stress on Stable activates Spell; Passion may still be hidden lining.
73
// Space-stress on Adream mixes Spell with Passion (both diagrams use Passion).
74
export interface DriveDiagram {
75
  primary_axis: [Drive, Drive]; // e.g., ["spell", "doing"]
76
  sub_axis: [Drive, Drive]; // e.g., ["spell", "vision"]
77
  example_characters: string[];
78
}
79
80
export const DRIVE_DIAGRAMS: Record<string, DriveDiagram> = {
81
  // Stable
82
  "stable-time-enclosing-A": {
83
    primary_axis: ["doing", "spell"],
84
    sub_axis: ["doing", "passion"],
85
    example_characters: ["Creon", "Hawthorne"],
86
  },
87
  "stable-flow-penetrating-D": {
88
    primary_axis: ["spell", "doing"],
89
    sub_axis: ["spell", "vision"], // NO PASSION — the Infinex placement
90
    example_characters: ["the Duke (Measure for Measure)", "Werle (Wild Duck)"],
91
  },
92
  // Adream
93
  "adream-space-enclosing-A": {
94
    primary_axis: ["spell", "passion"],
95
    sub_axis: ["spell", "doing"],
96
    example_characters: ["Claudius"],
97
  },
98
  "adream-space-radiating-B": {
99
    primary_axis: ["spell", "passion"], // mixes Spell with Passion
100
    sub_axis: ["spell", "vision"],
101
    example_characters: ["Hamlet", "Hedda Gabler", "Miss Julie"],
102
  },
103
  "adream-time-enclosing-C": {
104
    primary_axis: ["passion", "spell"],
105
    sub_axis: ["passion", "doing"],
106
    example_characters: ["Stanley", "Lear", "Jean"],
107
  },
108
  "adream-time-radiating-D": {
109
    primary_axis: ["passion", "spell"],
110
    sub_axis: ["passion", "vision"],
111
    example_characters: ["Ophelia", "Blanche"],
112
  },
113
  // Near
114
  "near": {
115
    primary_axis: ["doing", "passion"],
116
    sub_axis: ["doing", "passion"], // always Doing + Passion
117
    example_characters: ["Big Daddy", "Iago (if Near)", "Tesman"],
118
  },
119
};
120
121
// Status grammar (Mirodan §4.4) — what each stress reads as in terms of
122
// perceived maturity / social position.
123
export const STATUS_GRAMMAR: Record<Stress, string> = {
124
  time: "adolescent / lower status / immature",
125
  space: "mature / higher status / sophisticated",
126
  flow: "emotional / sociable",
127
  weight: "(only on outer Mobile/Remote/Awake projections)",
128
};
129
130
// -- Preparation hierarchy enforcement ----------------------------------------
131
132
/**
133
 * Given a sequence of motor actions (one per beat), check that every Quick
134
 * action is preceded by its required Sustained prep.
135
 *
136
 * Returns the indices of beats that lack the required prep.
137
 */
138
export function checkPrepHierarchy(motors: WorkingAction[]): number[] {
139
  const failures: number[] = [];
140
  for (let i = 0; i < motors.length; i++) {
141
    const m = motors[i];
142
    if (!m) continue;
143
    if (!isQuick(m)) continue;
144
    const prep = PREPARATION_PAIRS[m as QuickAction];
145
    // Look backward for the prep in immediately preceding beats.
146
    let found = false;
147
    for (let j = i - 1; j >= 0; j--) {
148
      const prev = motors[j];
149
      if (prev === prep) {
150
        found = true;
151
        break;
152
      }
153
      // A Quick action breaks the prep chain.
154
      if (prev && isQuick(prev)) break;
155
    }
156
    if (!found) failures.push(i);
157
  }
158
  return failures;
159
}
160
161
export function isQuick(a: WorkingAction): a is QuickAction {
162
  return a === "punching" || a === "slashing" || a === "dabbing" || a === "flicking";
163
}
164
165
export function isSustained(a: WorkingAction): a is SustainedAction {
166
  return a === "pressing" || a === "wringing" || a === "gliding" || a === "floating";
167
}
168
169
// -- Hard rules (Mirodan overrides — never violate) ---------------------------
170
171
export const HARD_RULES = [
172
  "Mobile, Awake, and Remote are NEVER baseline characters — only outer projections. CharacterSpec.inner_attitude must be one of Stable, Adream, Near.",
173
  "The three opposite-pairs are mutually exclusive: Stable↔Mobile, Near↔Remote, Awake↔Adream. A character anchored at one pole cannot have its opposite as either Inner Attitude or Action Attitude.",
174
  "Available stresses are determined by the baseline's Inner Factors. Space-stressed Stable does NOT exist (Stable's Inner = Weight+Space, so Space cannot be the stress).",
175
  "Every Quick action requires its Sustained partner as prep. Without prep, the Quick action degrades to its Sustained version (Pressing instead of Punching, etc.).",
176
  "Inner Attitudes produce Shadow Moves (2-axis, subliminal). Action Attitudes produce Working Actions (3-axis, visible). The Stress is the bridge — the third axis added to the inner pair to produce a visible Action.",
177
];
178

LLM / Active Validator

The older/current non-Actor path: LLM voice classifier, active factual audit, research tools, and orchestrator composition.

src/validator-llm.ts

1
import Anthropic from "@anthropic-ai/sdk";
2
import { INFINEX_VOICE } from "./voice/infinex.js";
3
import type { BeatSequence, CharacterSpec, TempoName, WorkingAction } from "./voice/types.js";
4
5
export const DEFAULT_LLM_VALIDATOR_MODEL = "claude-sonnet-4-6";
6
7
export type LLMAuditToolName = "audit_pass" | "audit_fail";
8
export type LLMFactualSeverity = "hard_fail" | "soft_warn";
9
10
export type DetectedDrive =
11
  | "doing"
12
  | "spell"
13
  | "passion"
14
  | "vision"
15
  | "doing-passion"
16
  | "spell-vision"
17
  | "unknown";
18
19
export const DETECTED_DRIVES: DetectedDrive[] = [
20
  "doing",
21
  "spell",
22
  "passion",
23
  "vision",
24
  "doing-passion",
25
  "spell-vision",
26
  "unknown",
27
];
28
29
export interface LLMIndependentClassification {
30
  tempo: TempoName | "unknown";
31
  motifs: WorkingAction[];
32
  detected_drive: DetectedDrive;
33
  confidence: number;
34
  rationale: string;
35
}
36
37
export interface LLMVoiceIssue {
38
  line: string;
39
  rule: string;
40
  fix: string;
41
}
42
43
export interface LLMFactualIssue {
44
  claim: string;
45
  evidence: string;
46
  severity: LLMFactualSeverity;
47
}
48
49
export interface LLMVoiceAuditVerdict {
50
  passed: boolean;
51
  tool: LLMAuditToolName;
52
  model: string;
53
  feedback: string;
54
  notes?: string;
55
  voice_issues: LLMVoiceIssue[];
56
  factual_issues: LLMFactualIssue[];
57
  independent_classification: LLMIndependentClassification;
58
}
59
60
export type ClassificationScope = "primary" | "beat";
61
62
export interface LLMValidationContext {
63
  surface?: string;
64
  job?: string;
65
  declared_tempo?: TempoName;
66
  deployed_facts?: string[];
67
  fact_context?: string;
68
  /**
69
   * `"primary"` (default) restricts blind-classification to the voice's `main_tempi`
70
   * + `unknown`. Reserve / beat-only tempi are NOT valid primary classifications —
71
   * standalone copy that reads as a reserve tempo must classify `unknown` and fail
72
   * the audit. `"beat"` widens the classification enum to include `beat_only_tempi`,
73
   * because within a declared multi-beat sequence a single beat MAY occupy a reserve
74
   * tempo (e.g. a Self-contained interlude between Commanding beats).
75
   * `auditBeatsLLM` passes `"beat"` automatically; everything else defaults `"primary"`.
76
   */
77
  classification_scope?: ClassificationScope;
78
}
79
80
export interface LLMVoiceAuditOptions extends LLMValidationContext {
81
  voice?: CharacterSpec;
82
  model?: string;
83
  max_tokens?: number;
84
  client?: AnthropicMessagesClient;
85
}
86
87
export interface LLMBeatAuditResult {
88
  beat_index: number;
89
  declared_tempo?: TempoName;
90
  classified_tempo: TempoName | "unknown";
91
  classified_confidence: number;
92
  passed: boolean;
93
  reason?: string;
94
  verdict: LLMVoiceAuditVerdict;
95
}
96
97
export interface LLMCopyAuditSample {
98
  id: string;
99
  text: string;
100
  surface?: string;
101
  job?: string;
102
  declared_tempo?: TempoName | "off-spec";
103
}
104
105
export interface LLMCopyAuditItem {
106
  id: string;
107
  passed: boolean;
108
  feedback: string;
109
  notes?: string;
110
  voice_issues: LLMVoiceIssue[];
111
  factual_issues: LLMFactualIssue[];
112
  independent_classification: LLMIndependentClassification;
113
}
114
115
export interface LLMCopySetAuditResult {
116
  model: string;
117
  items: LLMCopyAuditItem[];
118
}
119
120
type AnthropicTool = NonNullable<Parameters<Anthropic["messages"]["create"]>[0]["tools"]>[number];
121
type AnthropicContentBlock = Anthropic.Message["content"][number];
122
type AnthropicCreateParams = Parameters<Anthropic["messages"]["create"]>[0];
123
124
interface AnthropicMessagesClient {
125
  messages: {
126
    create: (params: AnthropicCreateParams) => Promise<{ content: Anthropic.Message["content"] }>;
127
  };
128
}
129
130
const MAX_TOKENS = 2048;
131
132
const WORKING_ACTIONS: WorkingAction[] = [
133
  "pressing",
134
  "wringing",
135
  "gliding",
136
  "floating",
137
  "punching",
138
  "slashing",
139
  "dabbing",
140
  "flicking",
141
];
142
143
export function buildLLMAuditTools(
144
  voice: CharacterSpec = INFINEX_VOICE,
145
  opts: { classification_scope?: ClassificationScope } = {},
146
): AnthropicTool[] {
147
  const tempoNames = availableTempoNames(voice, opts.classification_scope ?? "primary");
148
  const independentClassificationSchema = {
149
    type: "object",
150
    description:
151
      "Validator's blind read of the prose. Classify what is actually on the page, not what the generator or caller declared.",
152
    properties: {
153
      tempo: {
154
        type: "string",
155
        enum: [...tempoNames, "unknown"],
156
        description:
157
          "`unknown` is correct when the line reads as generic marketing, neutral taxonomy, or otherwise outside the Infinex character.",
158
      },
159
      motifs: {
160
        type: "array",
161
        items: { type: "string", enum: WORKING_ACTIONS },
162
        minItems: 1,
163
        maxItems: 4,
164
      },
165
      detected_drive: {
166
        type: "string",
167
        enum: DETECTED_DRIVES,
168
        description:
169
          "Which Mirodan drive does the prose actually carry? `doing` = direct action/getting-things-done. `spell` = timeless craft, slow charm. `passion` = urgency/time-pressure/hype/FOMO. `vision` = future-pull, agentic-becoming. Hyphenated pairs (e.g. `spell-vision`) are the canonical sub-axis combinations. Use `unknown` only when no drive reading is defensible. Surface this STRUCTURALLY — do not bury drive in rationale prose.",
170
      },
171
      confidence: {
172
        type: "number",
173
        minimum: 0,
174
        maximum: 1,
175
      },
176
      rationale: {
177
        type: "string",
178
        description: "Short reason for the blind classification. Reference the motor and drive in your reasoning.",
179
      },
180
    },
181
    required: ["tempo", "motifs", "detected_drive", "confidence", "rationale"],
182
  };
183
184
  return [
185
    {
186
      name: "audit_pass",
187
      description:
188
        "Emit when the text fits the Infinex voice and has no hard factual issue. Always include independent_classification.",
189
      input_schema: {
190
        type: "object",
191
        properties: {
192
          notes: {
193
            type: "string",
194
            description: "One-line audit summary.",
195
          },
196
          independent_classification: independentClassificationSchema,
197
        },
198
        required: ["notes", "independent_classification"],
199
      },
200
    },
201
    {
202
      name: "audit_fail",
203
      description:
204
        "Emit when the text fails on voice fit, unsupported facts, or hard product-copy mismatch. Always include independent_classification.",
205
      input_schema: {
206
        type: "object",
207
        properties: {
208
          voice_issues: {
209
            type: "array",
210
            items: {
211
              type: "object",
212
              properties: {
213
                line: { type: "string" },
214
                rule: { type: "string" },
215
                fix: { type: "string" },
216
              },
217
              required: ["line", "rule", "fix"],
218
            },
219
          },
220
          factual_issues: {
221
            type: "array",
222
            items: {
223
              type: "object",
224
              properties: {
225
                claim: { type: "string" },
226
                evidence: { type: "string" },
227
                severity: { type: "string", enum: ["hard_fail", "soft_warn"] },
228
              },
229
              required: ["claim", "evidence", "severity"],
230
            },
231
          },
232
          feedback: {
233
            type: "string",
234
            description: "Plain-text feedback that a generator or editor can use to rewrite the line.",
235
          },
236
          independent_classification: independentClassificationSchema,
237
        },
238
        required: ["feedback", "independent_classification"],
239
      },
240
    },
241
  ];
242
}
243
244
export function buildLLMCopySetAuditTools(
245
  voice: CharacterSpec = INFINEX_VOICE,
246
  opts: { classification_scope?: ClassificationScope } = {},
247
): AnthropicTool[] {
248
  const tempoNames = availableTempoNames(voice, opts.classification_scope ?? "primary");
249
  const independentClassificationSchema = {
250
    type: "object",
251
    properties: {
252
      tempo: {
253
        type: "string",
254
        enum: [...tempoNames, "unknown"],
255
      },
256
      motifs: {
257
        type: "array",
258
        items: { type: "string", enum: WORKING_ACTIONS },
259
        minItems: 1,
260
        maxItems: 4,
261
      },
262
      detected_drive: {
263
        type: "string",
264
        enum: DETECTED_DRIVES,
265
      },
266
      confidence: { type: "number", minimum: 0, maximum: 1 },
267
      rationale: { type: "string" },
268
    },
269
    required: ["tempo", "motifs", "detected_drive", "confidence", "rationale"],
270
  };
271
272
  return [
273
    {
274
      name: "audit_copy_set",
275
      description:
276
        "Emit one audit item per input copy sample. Classify every sample blind to declared_tempo and report pass/fail.",
277
      input_schema: {
278
        type: "object",
279
        properties: {
280
          items: {
281
            type: "array",
282
            items: {
283
              type: "object",
284
              properties: {
285
                id: { type: "string" },
286
                passed: { type: "boolean" },
287
                notes: { type: "string" },
288
                feedback: { type: "string" },
289
                voice_issues: {
290
                  type: "array",
291
                  items: {
292
                    type: "object",
293
                    properties: {
294
                      line: { type: "string" },
295
                      rule: { type: "string" },
296
                      fix: { type: "string" },
297
                    },
298
                    required: ["line", "rule", "fix"],
299
                  },
300
                },
301
                factual_issues: {
302
                  type: "array",
303
                  items: {
304
                    type: "object",
305
                    properties: {
306
                      claim: { type: "string" },
307
                      evidence: { type: "string" },
308
                      severity: { type: "string", enum: ["hard_fail", "soft_warn"] },
309
                    },
310
                    required: ["claim", "evidence", "severity"],
311
                  },
312
                },
313
                independent_classification: independentClassificationSchema,
314
              },
315
              required: ["id", "passed", "feedback", "independent_classification"],
316
            },
317
          },
318
        },
319
        required: ["items"],
320
      },
321
    },
322
  ];
323
}
324
325
export function buildLLMValidatorSystemPrompt(
326
  voice: CharacterSpec = INFINEX_VOICE,
327
  opts: { classification_scope?: ClassificationScope } = {},
328
): string {
329
  const scope = opts.classification_scope ?? "primary";
330
  const lines: string[] = [
331
    `You are the independent LLM validator for ${voice.name}.`,
332
    "",
333
    "You are NOT the generator. You do not improve the copy unless a verdict tool asks for a fix. Your job is to audit voice fit and classify the prose blind.",
334
    "",
335
    "# Character placement",
336
    `- Inner Attitude: ${voice.inner_attitude}`,
337
    `- Stress: ${voice.stress}${voice.stress_pole ? ` (${voice.stress_pole} pole)` : ""}`,
338
    `- Aspect: ${voice.aspect}`,
339
    `- Drive: ${voice.drive_primary} + ${voice.drive_secondary}${voice.drive_axis ? ` (${voice.drive_axis})` : ""}`,
340
    ...(voice.drive_table_cell ? [`- Drive table cell: ${voice.drive_table_cell}`] : []),
341
    ...(voice.drive_introvert ? [`- Drive introvert: ${voice.drive_introvert} (hidden Lining, not visible Outer)`] : []),
342
    ...(voice.drive_extravert ? [`- Drive extravert: ${voice.drive_extravert} (visible Outer)`] : []),
343
    `- Off-spec visible/extravert drive surfaces: ${voice.off_spec_drives.join(", ") || "none"}`,
344
    "",
345
    ...(voice.mirodan_kernel
346
      ? [
347
          "# Mirodan framework",
348
          "",
349
          "Use these derivation rules when judging voice fit. The audit is only as good as this spec.",
350
          "",
351
          voice.mirodan_kernel,
352
          "",
353
        ]
354
      : []),
355
    ...(voice.drive_table
356
      ? [
357
          "# The 12-cell drive table",
358
          "",
359
          voice.drive_table,
360
          "",
361
        ]
362
      : []),
363
    scope === "beat"
364
      ? "# Available tempi (BEAT-scope audit — reserve tempi are valid for individual beats within a declared multi-beat sequence)"
365
      : "# Main tempi (the ONLY valid primary classifications)",
366
    "",
367
    scope === "beat"
368
      ? "You are auditing a single beat within a multi-beat post that the generator declared. Either a main tempo or a reserve tempo is a valid classification for this beat — the generator may have intentionally placed a Self-contained / Diffused / Receptive beat inside the sequence. Judge by whether the prose matches the declared beat's tempo, not whether the tempo is in the standalone-rotation."
369
      : "These five tempi are the character's locked rotation. Primary classification of a standalone string MUST be one of these five, or `unknown`. Beat-only / reserve tempi exist in the broader Mirodan toolkit but are explicitly out of rotation for this character right now.",
370
    "",
371
  ];
372
373
  for (const tempoName of voice.main_tempi) {
374
    const tempo = voice.tempi[tempoName];
375
    if (!tempo) continue;
376
    lines.push(`## ${tempoName}`);
377
    lines.push(`Inner combo: ${tempo.inner_combo}`);
378
    lines.push(`Motor: ${tempo.motor[0]} -> ${tempo.motor[1]}`);
379
    lines.push(`Feel: ${tempo.feel}`);
380
    lines.push(`Opening shapes: ${tempo.opening_shapes.slice(0, 4).join(" | ")}`);
381
    lines.push(`Vocab anchors: ${tempo.vocab_anchor.slice(0, 8).join(", ")}`);
382
    lines.push(`Example lines: ${tempo.example_lines.slice(0, 2).join(" / ")}`);
383
    lines.push("");
384
  }
385
386
  if (voice.beat_only_tempi.length > 0) {
387
    if (scope === "beat") {
388
      lines.push("# Reserve tempi (also classifiable in beat-scope)");
389
      lines.push("");
390
      lines.push("In beat-scope you may classify a beat as any of these reserve tempi if its prose actually carries that motor and inner combo. Do not force a main tempo if the beat reads as a reserve tempo.");
391
      lines.push("");
392
      for (const tempoName of voice.beat_only_tempi) {
393
        const tempo = voice.tempi[tempoName];
394
        if (!tempo) continue;
395
        lines.push(`## ${tempoName}`);
396
        lines.push(`Inner combo: ${tempo.inner_combo}`);
397
        lines.push(`Motor: ${tempo.motor[0]} -> ${tempo.motor[1]}`);
398
        lines.push(`Feel: ${tempo.feel}`);
399
        lines.push(`Example lines: ${tempo.example_lines.slice(0, 1).join(" / ")}`);
400
        lines.push("");
401
      }
402
    } else {
403
      lines.push("# Reserve tempi (out of rotation — DO NOT classify as primary)");
404
      lines.push("");
405
      lines.push("The following tempi exist in the character's broader Mirodan toolkit but are explicitly NOT in the current rotation. If the prose reads as one of these, that is itself a finding — name the reserve tempo in your rationale, then classify as `unknown` and fail the audit with feedback that the prose has slipped into an off-rotation register.");
406
      lines.push("");
407
      for (const tempoName of voice.beat_only_tempi) {
408
        const tempo = voice.tempi[tempoName];
409
        if (!tempo) continue;
410
        lines.push(`- **${tempoName}** (${tempo.inner_combo}, ${tempo.motor[0]} → ${tempo.motor[1]}): ${tempo.feel}`);
411
      }
412
      lines.push("");
413
    }
414
  }
415
416
  lines.push(
417
    "# Voice judgment",
418
    "",
419
    "Judge voice fit purely by character coherence. Reason from the character placement above, the available tempi, and the motor vocabulary listed for each tempo. Do NOT apply a hardcoded ban list of words or phrases. A phrase that reads as in-character despite seeming generic is a valid finding — and a phrase you would personally find tacky may still fit the character. Conversely, a phrase that is in your operator's preferred vocabulary but pulls the prose off the character's drive/aspect/stress placement still fails.",
420
    "",
421
    "A line fails voice when, and only when, one of these is true:",
422
    "- It foregrounds an off-spec drive as the visible/extravert surface. For this character those surfaces are listed above. Read the prose for what drive it actually projects (e.g. Passion surfaces through urgency framing, time-pressure, FOMO, hype theatre, scarcity-of-attention). If the prose foregrounds an off-spec drive, fail it and name the drive.",
423
    scope === "beat"
424
      ? "- It cannot be reconciled with any of the available tempi for this beat (main + reserve), even charitably. The motor vocabulary (Pressing/Wringing/Gliding/Floating → Punching/Slashing/Dabbing/Flicking) is the load-bearing test."
425
      : "- It reads as a reserve / out-of-rotation tempo (Self-contained, Receptive, Overpowering, Diffused, Egocentric, Altruistic, Unsociable). These tempi exist in the toolkit but are explicitly off-rotation for the current character — even though motor-wise they may fit, the operator has decided they are not part of the spec right now. Name the reserve tempo in your rationale and classify as `unknown`.",
426
    scope === "beat"
427
      ? ""
428
      : "- It cannot be reconciled with any of the FIVE main tempi (Commanding, Practical, Sombre, Irradiant, Sociable), even charitably. The motor vocabulary (Pressing/Wringing/Gliding/Floating → Punching/Slashing/Dabbing/Flicking) is the load-bearing test. If no Sustained → Quick motor pair from a main tempo fits the shape of the prose, classify as `unknown`.",
429
    "- It makes claims not present in `deployed_facts` or `fact_context`.",
430
    "",
431
    "A line does NOT fail merely because it is short, dry, or unexciting. The character should often be dry. A two- or three-word label can be fully Commanding (Pressing → Punching) without ornament. Equally, a longer line can fail even if every word is on the operator's allow-list, if the cumulative drive pulls off the character.",
432
    "",
433
    "# Independent classification — blind to declared_tempo",
434
    "",
435
    "After the voice and factual audit, classify the prose yourself, BLIND to any `declared_tempo` in the user payload.",
436
    "Set declared_tempo aside for this step. Do not anchor on it. Anchor on what is actually on the page, read through the motor vocabulary.",
437
    "",
438
    "Output:",
439
    scope === "beat"
440
      ? "- tempo: any available tempo (main OR reserve) OR `unknown`. Beat-scope audit — declared multi-beat sequences may legitimately place reserve tempi as individual beats."
441
      : "- tempo: MUST be one of the five main tempi (Commanding, Practical, Sombre, Irradiant, Sociable) OR `unknown`. Reserve tempi are NOT valid primary classifications.",
442
    "- motifs: 1-4 working actions that the prose actually carries.",
443
    "- detected_drive: structured drive reading — `doing` (direct action), `spell` (timeless craft), `passion` (urgency/hype/FOMO), `vision` (future-pull), or hyphenated combos (`doing-passion`, `spell-vision`). `unknown` only when no drive is defensible. This is the SAME drive analysis you do in rationale, just emitted as a structured field. Even when you classify the tempo as `unknown` for off-spec drive activation, name the drive here — that is the whole point of the field.",
444
    "- confidence: 0..1.",
445
    "- rationale: short explanation grounded in the tempo's motor and inner combo, not just surface vocabulary. If you classify `unknown`, name which character trait the line fails to embody (e.g. 'reads as Passion-pulled — urgency framing inconsistent with Spell-Vision drive', 'no Sustained → Quick motor pair from the main rotation fits — closest read would be Self-contained which is out-of-rotation', or 'reads as Diffused — quiet post-launch reflection — but Diffused is reserve only').",
446
    "",
447
    "`unknown` is a valid, important classification. Use it when the copy reads outside the five main tempi — including when it reads as a reserve tempo.",
448
    "",
449
    "If your classification disagrees with `declared_tempo`, that is a feature, not a bug. The caller will compare them.",
450
    "",
451
    "# Output contract",
452
    "",
453
    "Emit exactly ONE verdict tool call. No prose outside tool calls.",
454
    "Use `audit_pass` only when the line fits the voice and has no hard factual issue.",
455
    "Use `audit_fail` when there is any hard voice issue or hard factual issue.",
456
    "Both tools require `independent_classification`.",
457
  );
458
459
  return lines.join("\n");
460
}
461
462
export function buildLLMValidatorUserMessage(
463
  text: string,
464
  opts: LLMValidationContext = {},
465
): string {
466
  return JSON.stringify(
467
    {
468
      text,
469
      surface: opts.surface ?? "",
470
      job: opts.job ?? "",
471
      declared_tempo: opts.declared_tempo ?? "",
472
      deployed_facts: opts.deployed_facts ?? [],
473
      fact_context: opts.fact_context ?? "",
474
    },
475
    null,
476
    2,
477
  );
478
}
479
480
export function buildLLMCopySetUserMessage(samples: LLMCopyAuditSample[]): string {
481
  return JSON.stringify(
482
    {
483
      instruction:
484
        "Audit each copy sample independently. Do not let one sample's declared_tempo or result influence another. Return exactly one item per id.",
485
      samples,
486
    },
487
    null,
488
    2,
489
  );
490
}
491
492
export function parseLLMAuditToolUse(
493
  content: AnthropicContentBlock[],
494
  model: string = DEFAULT_LLM_VALIDATOR_MODEL,
495
): LLMVoiceAuditVerdict | null {
496
  for (const block of content) {
497
    if (block.type !== "tool_use") continue;
498
    if (block.name !== "audit_pass" && block.name !== "audit_fail") continue;
499
    const payload = isRecord(block.input) ? block.input : {};
500
    return block.name === "audit_pass"
501
      ? parsePass(payload, model)
502
      : parseFail(payload, model);
503
  }
504
  return null;
505
}
506
507
export function parseLLMCopySetToolUse(
508
  content: AnthropicContentBlock[],
509
  model: string = DEFAULT_LLM_VALIDATOR_MODEL,
510
): LLMCopySetAuditResult | null {
511
  for (const block of content) {
512
    if (block.type !== "tool_use") continue;
513
    if (block.name !== "audit_copy_set") continue;
514
    const payload = isRecord(block.input) ? block.input : {};
515
    const rawItems = Array.isArray(payload.items) ? payload.items : [];
516
    return {
517
      model,
518
      items: rawItems.flatMap((item) => {
519
        if (!isRecord(item)) return [];
520
        const id = stringOrEmpty(item.id);
521
        if (!id) return [];
522
        const notes = stringOrEmpty(item.notes);
523
        return [{
524
          id,
525
          passed: item.passed === true,
526
          feedback: stringOrEmpty(item.feedback),
527
          ...(notes ? { notes } : {}),
528
          voice_issues: parseVoiceIssues(item.voice_issues),
529
          factual_issues: parseFactualIssues(item.factual_issues),
530
          independent_classification: parseIndependentClassification(item.independent_classification),
531
        }];
532
      }),
533
    };
534
  }
535
  return null;
536
}
537
538
export async function auditTextLLM(
539
  text: string,
540
  opts: LLMVoiceAuditOptions = {},
541
): Promise<LLMVoiceAuditVerdict> {
542
  const voice = opts.voice ?? INFINEX_VOICE;
543
  const model = opts.model ?? process.env.COMMS_VOICE_VALIDATOR_MODEL ?? DEFAULT_LLM_VALIDATOR_MODEL;
544
  const client = opts.client ?? new Anthropic();
545
  const scope = opts.classification_scope ?? "primary";
546
  const response = await client.messages.create({
547
    model,
548
    max_tokens: opts.max_tokens ?? MAX_TOKENS,
549
    system: buildLLMValidatorSystemPrompt(voice, { classification_scope: scope }),
550
    tools: buildLLMAuditTools(voice, { classification_scope: scope }),
551
    tool_choice: { type: "any", disable_parallel_tool_use: true },
552
    messages: [
553
      {
554
        role: "user",
555
        content: buildLLMValidatorUserMessage(text, opts),
556
      },
557
    ],
558
  });
559
  const verdict = parseLLMAuditToolUse(response.content, model);
560
  if (!verdict) {
561
    throw new Error("LLM validator did not emit audit_pass or audit_fail");
562
  }
563
  return verdict;
564
}
565
566
export async function auditCopySetLLM(
567
  samples: LLMCopyAuditSample[],
568
  opts: Omit<LLMVoiceAuditOptions, keyof LLMValidationContext> & { classification_scope?: ClassificationScope } = {},
569
): Promise<LLMCopySetAuditResult> {
570
  const voice = opts.voice ?? INFINEX_VOICE;
571
  const model = opts.model ?? process.env.COMMS_VOICE_VALIDATOR_MODEL ?? DEFAULT_LLM_VALIDATOR_MODEL;
572
  const client = opts.client ?? new Anthropic();
573
  const scope = opts.classification_scope ?? "primary";
574
  const response = await client.messages.create({
575
    model,
576
    max_tokens: opts.max_tokens ?? Math.max(MAX_TOKENS, samples.length * 450),
577
    system: buildLLMValidatorSystemPrompt(voice, { classification_scope: scope }),
578
    tools: buildLLMCopySetAuditTools(voice, { classification_scope: scope }),
579
    tool_choice: { type: "tool", name: "audit_copy_set", disable_parallel_tool_use: true },
580
    messages: [
581
      {
582
        role: "user",
583
        content: buildLLMCopySetUserMessage(samples),
584
      },
585
    ],
586
  });
587
  const result = parseLLMCopySetToolUse(response.content, model);
588
  if (!result) {
589
    throw new Error("LLM copy-set validator did not emit audit_copy_set");
590
  }
591
  const received = new Set(result.items.map((item) => item.id));
592
  const missing = samples.map((sample) => sample.id).filter((id) => !received.has(id));
593
  if (missing.length > 0) {
594
    throw new Error(`LLM copy-set validator omitted item(s): ${missing.join(", ")}`);
595
  }
596
  return result;
597
}
598
599
export async function auditBeatsLLM(
600
  text: string,
601
  beats: BeatSequence,
602
  opts: Omit<LLMVoiceAuditOptions, "declared_tempo"> = {},
603
): Promise<LLMBeatAuditResult[]> {
604
  const paragraphs = splitIntoBeats(text);
605
  const results: LLMBeatAuditResult[] = [];
606
607
  for (let i = 0; i < beats.beats.length; i++) {
608
    const declared = beats.beats[i];
609
    if (!declared) continue;
610
    const paragraph = paragraphs[i];
611
    if (!paragraph) {
612
      results.push({
613
        beat_index: i,
614
        ...(declared.tempo !== undefined ? { declared_tempo: declared.tempo } : {}),
615
        classified_tempo: "unknown",
616
        classified_confidence: 0,
617
        passed: false,
618
        reason: "missing paragraph for declared beat",
619
        verdict: missingParagraphVerdict(opts.model ?? DEFAULT_LLM_VALIDATOR_MODEL),
620
      });
621
      continue;
622
    }
623
624
    const verdict = await auditTextLLM(paragraph, {
625
      ...opts,
626
      ...(declared.tempo !== undefined ? { declared_tempo: declared.tempo } : {}),
627
      // Beat audits ALWAYS use beat-scope classification — within a declared
628
      // multi-beat sequence, reserve tempi (Self-contained, Receptive, etc.)
629
      // are valid for individual beats even though they are invalid as the
630
      // primary register of standalone copy. Caller can override but the
631
      // default for beat audits must be "beat".
632
      classification_scope: opts.classification_scope ?? "beat",
633
    });
634
    const classification = verdict.independent_classification;
635
    // Mirodan: tempo is what the audience reads, not what the actor declares.
636
    // Pass if the classified tempo is within the voice's locked palette,
637
    // regardless of whether it matches the generator's declared intent.
638
    const checkVoice = opts.voice ?? INFINEX_VOICE;
639
    const inPlacement =
640
      classification.tempo !== "unknown" &&
641
      checkVoice.tempi[classification.tempo as TempoName] !== undefined;
642
    const passed = verdict.passed && inPlacement;
643
    const result: LLMBeatAuditResult = {
644
      beat_index: i,
645
      ...(declared.tempo !== undefined ? { declared_tempo: declared.tempo } : {}),
646
      classified_tempo: classification.tempo,
647
      classified_confidence: classification.confidence,
648
      passed,
649
      verdict,
650
    };
651
    if (!inPlacement) {
652
      result.reason = `LLM validator read ${classification.tempo} (confidence ${classification.confidence.toFixed(2)}) — outside the voice's locked tempi: ${classification.rationale}`;
653
    } else if (!verdict.passed) {
654
      result.reason = `LLM validator failed voice audit: ${verdict.feedback || summarizeVoiceIssues(verdict.voice_issues)}`;
655
    } else if (declared.tempo && classification.tempo !== declared.tempo) {
656
      // Only surfaces under the legacy single-call path (which still
657
      // passes a declared tempo). Two-call path has no declared tempo so
658
      // this branch never fires.
659
      result.reason = `audience read ${classification.tempo} (legacy path declared ${declared.tempo})`;
660
    }
661
    results.push(result);
662
  }
663
664
  return results;
665
}
666
667
function parsePass(payload: Record<string, unknown>, model: string): LLMVoiceAuditVerdict {
668
  const notes = stringOrEmpty(payload.notes);
669
  return {
670
    passed: true,
671
    tool: "audit_pass",
672
    model,
673
    feedback: notes,
674
    ...(notes ? { notes } : {}),
675
    voice_issues: [],
676
    factual_issues: [],
677
    independent_classification: parseIndependentClassification(payload.independent_classification),
678
  };
679
}
680
681
function parseFail(payload: Record<string, unknown>, model: string): LLMVoiceAuditVerdict {
682
  return {
683
    passed: false,
684
    tool: "audit_fail",
685
    model,
686
    feedback: stringOrEmpty(payload.feedback),
687
    voice_issues: parseVoiceIssues(payload.voice_issues),
688
    factual_issues: parseFactualIssues(payload.factual_issues),
689
    independent_classification: parseIndependentClassification(payload.independent_classification),
690
  };
691
}
692
693
function parseIndependentClassification(payload: unknown): LLMIndependentClassification {
694
  if (!isRecord(payload)) {
695
    return {
696
      tempo: "unknown",
697
      motifs: [],
698
      detected_drive: "unknown",
699
      confidence: 0,
700
      rationale: "missing independent_classification",
701
    };
702
  }
703
  const tempo = typeof payload.tempo === "string" ? payload.tempo : "unknown";
704
  const drive = typeof payload.detected_drive === "string" ? payload.detected_drive : "unknown";
705
  return {
706
    tempo: isTempoNameOrUnknown(tempo) ? tempo : "unknown",
707
    motifs: Array.isArray(payload.motifs)
708
      ? payload.motifs.filter((motif): motif is WorkingAction => isWorkingAction(motif))
709
      : [],
710
    detected_drive: isDetectedDrive(drive) ? drive : "unknown",
711
    confidence: typeof payload.confidence === "number" ? clamp01(payload.confidence) : 0,
712
    rationale: stringOrEmpty(payload.rationale),
713
  };
714
}
715
716
function isDetectedDrive(value: string): value is DetectedDrive {
717
  return (DETECTED_DRIVES as string[]).includes(value);
718
}
719
720
function parseVoiceIssues(payload: unknown): LLMVoiceIssue[] {
721
  if (!Array.isArray(payload)) return [];
722
  return payload.flatMap((item) => {
723
    if (!isRecord(item)) return [];
724
    return [{
725
      line: stringOrEmpty(item.line),
726
      rule: stringOrEmpty(item.rule),
727
      fix: stringOrEmpty(item.fix),
728
    }];
729
  });
730
}
731
732
function parseFactualIssues(payload: unknown): LLMFactualIssue[] {
733
  if (!Array.isArray(payload)) return [];
734
  return payload.flatMap((item) => {
735
    if (!isRecord(item)) return [];
736
    const severity = item.severity === "soft_warn" ? "soft_warn" : "hard_fail";
737
    return [{
738
      claim: stringOrEmpty(item.claim),
739
      evidence: stringOrEmpty(item.evidence),
740
      severity,
741
    }];
742
  });
743
}
744
745
function availableTempoNames(
746
  voice: CharacterSpec,
747
  scope: ClassificationScope = "primary",
748
): TempoName[] {
749
  // Primary scope (default): main_tempi only. Reserve / beat-only tempi are
750
  // not valid primary classifications — operator decision 2026-05-15. The
751
  // prompt teaches Sonnet to name a reserve tempo in rationale and classify
752
  // as `unknown` if the prose reads as one.
753
  //
754
  // Beat scope: widens the enum to include beat_only_tempi, because within a
755
  // declared multi-beat sequence one beat MAY occupy a reserve tempo. Used by
756
  // `auditBeatsLLM` automatically.
757
  const names = scope === "beat"
758
    ? [...voice.main_tempi, ...voice.beat_only_tempi]
759
    : voice.main_tempi;
760
  return names.filter((name) => Boolean(voice.tempi[name]));
761
}
762
763
function splitIntoBeats(text: string): string[] {
764
  return text
765
    .split(/\n\s*\n+/)
766
    .map((p) => p.trim())
767
    .filter((p) => p.length > 0);
768
}
769
770
function missingParagraphVerdict(model: string): LLMVoiceAuditVerdict {
771
  return {
772
    passed: false,
773
    tool: "audit_fail",
774
    model,
775
    feedback: "missing paragraph for declared beat",
776
    voice_issues: [{ line: "", rule: "missing_beat", fix: "Write one paragraph for the declared beat." }],
777
    factual_issues: [],
778
    independent_classification: {
779
      tempo: "unknown",
780
      motifs: [],
781
      detected_drive: "unknown",
782
      confidence: 0,
783
      rationale: "No text to classify.",
784
    },
785
  };
786
}
787
788
function summarizeVoiceIssues(issues: LLMVoiceIssue[]): string {
789
  return issues.map((issue) => `${issue.rule}: ${issue.fix}`).join("; ");
790
}
791
792
function isRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {
793
  return typeof value === "object" && value !== null && !Array.isArray(value);
794
}
795
796
function stringOrEmpty(value: unknown): string {
797
  return typeof value === "string" ? value : "";
798
}
799
800
function clamp01(value: number): number {
801
  if (!Number.isFinite(value)) return 0;
802
  return Math.max(0, Math.min(1, value));
803
}
804
805
function isWorkingAction(value: unknown): value is WorkingAction {
806
  return typeof value === "string" && WORKING_ACTIONS.includes(value as WorkingAction);
807
}
808
809
function isTempoNameOrUnknown(value: string): value is TempoName | "unknown" {
810
  return value === "unknown" || isTempoName(value);
811
}
812
813
function isTempoName(value: string): value is TempoName {
814
  return [
815
    "commanding",
816
    "practical",
817
    "self-contained",
818
    "receptive",
819
    "sombre",
820
    "overpowering",
821
    "diffused",
822
    "irradiant",
823
    "materialistic",
824
    "human",
825
    "warm",
826
    "cool",
827
    "unacknowledged",
828
    "acknowledged",
829
    "revealed",
830
    "concealed",
831
    "egocentric",
832
    "unsociable",
833
    "sociable",
834
    "altruistic",
835
    "acute",
836
    "doubting",
837
    "certain",
838
    "uncertain",
839
  ].includes(value);
840
}
841

src/validator-active.ts

327 lines
1
import Anthropic from "@anthropic-ai/sdk";
2
import type { ReleaseCard } from "./card.js";
3
import type { Channel, NotSaidFact } from "./generator.js";
4
import {
5
  validate,
6
  type ValidationResult,
7
} from "./validator.js";
8
import {
9
  buildLLMAuditTools,
10
  buildLLMValidatorSystemPrompt,
11
  parseLLMAuditToolUse,
12
  DEFAULT_LLM_VALIDATOR_MODEL,
13
  type ClassificationScope,
14
  type LLMVoiceAuditVerdict,
15
} from "./validator-llm.js";
16
import {
17
  buildResearchTools,
18
  executeResearchToolCall,
19
  isResearchToolName,
20
  type ResearchToolResult,
21
} from "./research-tools.js";
22
import { INFINEX_VOICE } from "./voice/infinex.js";
23
import type { BeatSequence, CharacterSpec } from "./voice/types.js";
24
25
type AnthropicContentBlock = Anthropic.Message["content"][number];
26
type AnthropicCreateParams = Parameters<Anthropic["messages"]["create"]>[0];
27
28
interface AnthropicMessagesClient {
29
  messages: {
30
    create: (params: AnthropicCreateParams) => Promise<{ content: Anthropic.Message["content"] }>;
31
  };
32
}
33
34
export type ActiveValidatorTraceEvent =
35
  | { type: "turn"; turn: number; model: string; tool_names: string[]; text_preview?: string }
36
  | { type: "tool_call"; turn: number; name: string; input: Record<string, unknown> }
37
  | { type: "tool_result"; turn: number; name: string; content_preview: string }
38
  | { type: "verdict"; turn: number; passed: boolean }
39
  | { type: "silent"; turn: number; text_preview?: string }
40
  | { type: "truncated"; turn: number; reason: string };
41
42
export type ActiveValidatorToolExecutor = (
43
  toolName: string,
44
  toolInput: Record<string, unknown>,
45
  toolUseId: string,
46
) => Promise<ResearchToolResult>;
47
48
export interface ActiveValidationOptions {
49
  voice?: CharacterSpec;
50
  beats?: BeatSequence;
51
  card: ReleaseCard;
52
  channel?: Channel;
53
  deployed_facts_used?: string[];
54
  not_said?: NotSaidFact[];
55
  surface?: string;
56
  job?: string;
57
  model?: string;
58
  max_tokens?: number;
59
  max_tool_turns?: number;
60
  classification_scope?: ClassificationScope;
61
  client?: AnthropicMessagesClient;
62
  tool_executor?: ActiveValidatorToolExecutor;
63
}
64
65
export interface ActiveValidationVerdict {
66
  passed: boolean;
67
  deterministic: ValidationResult;
68
  llm?: LLMVoiceAuditVerdict;
69
  research_trace: ActiveValidatorTraceEvent[];
70
  active_turns: number;
71
  truncated?: boolean;
72
  reason: string | null;
73
}
74
75
const DEFAULT_ACTIVE_MAX_TURNS = 6;
76
const DEFAULT_ACTIVE_MAX_TOKENS = 4096;
77
78
/**
79
 * Nigel-style active validator for comms-factory candidates.
80
 *
81
 * The validator may use research tools to check contradiction or freshness, but
82
 * it may not expand the candidate's fact allowance. If a true fact is missing
83
 * from card.deployed_facts, it must fail as card_missing_fact.
84
 */
85
export async function auditTextActive(
86
  text: string,
87
  opts: ActiveValidationOptions,
88
): Promise<ActiveValidationVerdict> {
89
  const voice = opts.voice ?? INFINEX_VOICE;
90
  const deterministic = await deterministicFirst(text, opts);
91
  if (!deterministic.passed) {
92
    return {
93
      passed: false,
94
      deterministic: deterministic.deterministic,
95
      research_trace: [],
96
      active_turns: 0,
97
      reason: deterministic.reason,
98
    };
99
  }
100
101
  const model = opts.model ?? process.env.COMMS_ACTIVE_VALIDATOR_MODEL ?? DEFAULT_LLM_VALIDATOR_MODEL;
102
  const client = opts.client ?? new Anthropic();
103
  const maxTurns = opts.max_tool_turns ?? intFromEnv("COMMS_ACTIVE_VALIDATOR_MAX_TOOL_TURNS", DEFAULT_ACTIVE_MAX_TURNS);
104
  const executor = opts.tool_executor ?? executeResearchToolCall;
105
  const scope = opts.classification_scope ?? "primary";
106
  const messages: AnthropicCreateParams["messages"] = [
107
    {
108
      role: "user",
109
      content: buildActiveValidatorUserMessage(text, opts),
110
    },
111
  ];
112
  const tools = [
113
    ...buildResearchTools(),
114
    ...buildLLMAuditTools(voice, { classification_scope: scope }),
115
  ];
116
  const trace: ActiveValidatorTraceEvent[] = [];
117
118
  for (let turn = 1; turn <= maxTurns; turn++) {
119
    const response = await client.messages.create({
120
      model,
121
      max_tokens: opts.max_tokens ?? intFromEnv("COMMS_ACTIVE_VALIDATOR_MAX_TOKENS", DEFAULT_ACTIVE_MAX_TOKENS),
122
      system: buildActiveValidatorSystemPrompt(voice, { classification_scope: scope }),
123
      tools,
124
      messages,
125
    });
126
127
    const toolNamesForTurn = toolNames(response.content);
128
    const preview = textPreview(response.content);
129
    trace.push({
130
      type: "turn",
131
      turn,
132
      model,
133
      tool_names: toolNamesForTurn,
134
      ...(preview ? { text_preview: preview } : {}),
135
    });
136
137
    const verdict = parseLLMAuditToolUse(response.content, model);
138
    if (verdict) {
139
      trace.push({ type: "verdict", turn, passed: verdict.passed });
140
      return {
141
        passed: verdict.passed,
142
        deterministic: deterministic.deterministic,
143
        llm: verdict,
144
        research_trace: trace,
145
        active_turns: turn,
146
        reason: verdict.passed ? null : reasonFromLLM(verdict),
147
      };
148
    }
149
150
    const researchCalls = researchToolCalls(response.content);
151
    if (researchCalls.length === 0) {
152
      trace.push({
153
        type: "silent",
154
        turn,
155
        ...(preview ? { text_preview: preview } : {}),
156
      });
157
      return {
158
        passed: false,
159
        deterministic: deterministic.deterministic,
160
        research_trace: trace,
161
        active_turns: turn,
162
        reason: "active-validator-silent",
163
      };
164
    }
165
166
    for (const call of researchCalls) {
167
      trace.push({ type: "tool_call", turn, name: call.name, input: call.input });
168
    }
169
    const toolResults = await Promise.all(
170
      researchCalls.map((call) => executor(call.name, call.input, call.id)),
171
    );
172
    for (let i = 0; i < toolResults.length; i++) {
173
      const result = toolResults[i];
174
      const call = researchCalls[i];
175
      if (!result || !call) continue;
176
      trace.push({
177
        type: "tool_result",
178
        turn,
179
        name: call.name,
180
        content_preview: result.content.slice(0, 1000),
181
      });
182
    }
183
184
    messages.push({ role: "assistant", content: response.content });
185
    messages.push({
186
      role: "user",
187
      content: toolResults.map((result) => ({
188
        type: "tool_result" as const,
189
        tool_use_id: result.tool_use_id,
190
        content: result.content,
191
      })),
192
    });
193
  }
194
195
  trace.push({
196
    type: "truncated",
197
    turn: maxTurns,
198
    reason: "max_tool_turns reached before audit_pass/audit_fail",
199
  });
200
  return {
201
    passed: false,
202
    deterministic: deterministic.deterministic,
203
    research_trace: trace,
204
    active_turns: maxTurns,
205
    truncated: true,
206
    reason: "active-validator-max-tool-turns",
207
  };
208
}
209
210
export function buildActiveValidatorSystemPrompt(
211
  voice: CharacterSpec = INFINEX_VOICE,
212
  opts: { classification_scope?: ClassificationScope } = {},
213
): string {
214
  return [
215
    buildLLMValidatorSystemPrompt(voice, opts),
216
    "",
217
    "# Active factual audit",
218
    "",
219
    "You have research tools. Use them only when a factual, numeric, partner, product, URL, market, date, or recent-announcement claim needs verification.",
220
    "The ReleaseCard is the allowance boundary. A candidate may assert only claims that are present in card.deployed_facts.",
221
    "If research proves the candidate true but the fact is not in card.deployed_facts, fail with voice/factual rule `card_missing_fact`; do not pass it.",
222
    "If research contradicts the card or candidate, fail with factual rule `research_contradiction` and cite the tool result.",
223
    "If the candidate makes a claim that cannot be supported from card.deployed_facts or fact_context, fail with `unsupported_claim`.",
224
    "Do not rewrite the copy. Emit one audit_pass or audit_fail verdict after any needed research.",
225
  ].join("\n");
226
}
227
228
export function buildActiveValidatorUserMessage(
229
  text: string,
230
  opts: ActiveValidationOptions,
231
): string {
232
  return JSON.stringify(
233
    {
234
      text,
235
      channel: opts.channel ?? "",
236
      surface: opts.surface ?? "",
237
      job: opts.job ?? "",
238
      declared_beats: opts.beats?.beats ?? [],
239
      deployed_facts_used: opts.deployed_facts_used ?? [],
240
      not_said: opts.not_said ?? [],
241
      card: {
242
        id: opts.card.id,
243
        kind: opts.card.kind,
244
        title: opts.card.title,
245
        audience: opts.card.audience,
246
        deployed_facts: opts.card.deployed_facts,
247
      },
248
      instruction:
249
        "Audit the candidate. Research may detect contradiction or staleness, but it must not expand the fact allowance beyond card.deployed_facts.",
250
    },
251
    null,
252
    2,
253
  );
254
}
255
256
function deterministicFirst(
257
  text: string,
258
  opts: ActiveValidationOptions,
259
): { passed: boolean; deterministic: ValidationResult; reason: string | null } {
260
  const deterministic = validate(text, {
261
    ...(opts.voice !== undefined ? { voice: opts.voice } : {}),
262
    ...(opts.beats !== undefined ? { beats: opts.beats } : {}),
263
    card: opts.card,
264
    ...(opts.deployed_facts_used !== undefined ? { deployed_facts_used: opts.deployed_facts_used } : {}),
265
    ...(opts.not_said !== undefined ? { not_said: opts.not_said } : {}),
266
  });
267
  const first = deterministic.failures[0];
268
  return {
269
    passed: deterministic.passed,
270
    deterministic,
271
    reason: first ? `${first.rule}: ${first.reason}` : deterministic.passed ? null : "deterministic-fail",
272
  };
273
}
274
275
function researchToolCalls(blocks: AnthropicContentBlock[]): Array<{
276
  id: string;
277
  name: string;
278
  input: Record<string, unknown>;
279
}> {
280
  const calls: Array<{ id: string; name: string; input: Record<string, unknown> }> = [];
281
  for (const block of blocks) {
282
