    if (block.type !== "tool_use") continue;
283
    if (!isResearchToolName(block.name)) continue;
284
    calls.push({
285
      id: block.id,
286
      name: block.name,
287
      input: isRecord(block.input) ? block.input : {},
288
    });
289
  }
290
  return calls;
291
}
292
293
function reasonFromLLM(verdict: LLMVoiceAuditVerdict): string {
294
  if (verdict.feedback) return verdict.feedback;
295
  const factual = verdict.factual_issues[0];
296
  if (factual) return `${factual.claim}: ${factual.evidence}`;
297
  const voice = verdict.voice_issues[0];
298
  if (voice) return `${voice.rule}: ${voice.fix}`;
299
  return "active-validator-fail";
300
}
301
302
function textPreview(blocks: AnthropicContentBlock[]): string {
303
  return blocks
304
    .filter((block): block is Extract<AnthropicContentBlock, { type: "text" }> => block.type === "text")
305
    .map((block) => block.text)
306
    .join("\n")
307
    .slice(0, 1000)
308
    .trim();
309
}
310
311
function toolNames(blocks: AnthropicContentBlock[]): string[] {
312
  return blocks
313
    .filter((block): block is Extract<AnthropicContentBlock, { type: "tool_use" }> => block.type === "tool_use")
314
    .map((block) => block.name);
315
}
316
317
function isRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {
318
  return typeof value === "object" && value !== null && !Array.isArray(value);
319
}
320
321
function intFromEnv(name: string, fallback: number): number {
322
  const raw = process.env[name];
323
  if (!raw) return fallback;
324
  const parsed = Number.parseInt(raw, 10);
325
  return Number.isFinite(parsed) && parsed > 0 ? parsed : fallback;
326
}
327

src/research-tools.ts

460 lines
1
import Anthropic from "@anthropic-ai/sdk";
2
import {
3
  grepPlatform,
4
  readPlatformFile,
5
} from "./fact-grounder/sources/platform-code.js";
6
import {
7
  allFeatures,
8
  lookupFeature,
9
  searchFeatures,
10
  type PartnerRegistryEntry,
11
} from "./fact-grounder/sources/partner-registry.js";
12
import { fetchInfinexPage } from "./fact-grounder/sources/infinex-pages.js";
13
import { fetchPublicPage } from "./fact-grounder/sources/public-page.js";
14
import {
15
  fetchRenderedPage,
16
  AgentBrowserUnavailableError,
17
} from "./fact-grounder/sources/rendered-page.js";
18
import {
19
  infinexWebSearch,
20
  infinexSearchRecentPosts,
21
  ProjectjinUnavailableError,
22
} from "./fact-grounder/sources/projectjin-research.js";
23
24
export type AnthropicTool = NonNullable<Parameters<Anthropic["messages"]["create"]>[0]["tools"]>[number];
25
26
export interface ResearchToolResult {
27
  tool_use_id: string;
28
  content: string;
29
}
30
31
export type ResearchToolName =
32
  | "grep_platform_code"
33
  | "read_platform_file"
34
  | "lookup_partner"
35
  | "fetch_infinex_page"
36
  | "fetch_public_page"
37
  | "fetch_rendered_page"
38
  | "fetch_json_api"
39
  | "infinex_web_search"
40
  | "infinex_search_recent_posts";
41
42
const RESEARCH_TOOL_NAMES = new Set<string>([
43
  "grep_platform_code",
44
  "read_platform_file",
45
  "lookup_partner",
46
  "fetch_infinex_page",
47
  "fetch_public_page",
48
  "fetch_rendered_page",
49
  "fetch_json_api",
50
  "infinex_web_search",
51
  "infinex_search_recent_posts",
52
]);
53
54
export const TOOL_RESULT_CAP = 4000;
55
const DEFAULT_JSON_API_ALLOWLIST = [
56
  "api.hyperliquid.xyz",
57
  "clob.polymarket.com",
58
  "gamma-api.polymarket.com",
59
];
60
61
export function isResearchToolName(name: string): name is ResearchToolName {
62
  return RESEARCH_TOOL_NAMES.has(name);
63
}
64
65
export function buildResearchTools(): AnthropicTool[] {
66
  return [
67
    {
68
      name: "grep_platform_code",
69
      description:
70
        "Search the Infinex platform monorepo using ripgrep. Use for finding constants, config values, feature flags, leverage caps, supported chain counts, or any hardcoded product fact. Returns file paths, line numbers, and matched content.",
71
      input_schema: {
72
        type: "object",
73
        properties: {
74
          pattern: { type: "string", description: "Ripgrep search pattern (plain text or regex)" },
75
          path: { type: "string", description: "Optional sub-path within the platform root, e.g. 'apps/perps-app/src'" },
76
          glob: { type: "string", description: "File glob, e.g. '*.ts'. Defaults to *.ts, *.tsx, *.json" },
77
          maxResults: { type: "number", description: "Max matches to return (default 20)" },
78
        },
79
        required: ["pattern"],
80
      },
81
    },
82
    {
83
      name: "read_platform_file",
84
      description:
85
        "Read a specific file from the platform monorepo. Use after grep_platform_code to read the full context around a match. Path is relative to the platform root.",
86
      input_schema: {
87
        type: "object",
88
        properties: {
89
          path: { type: "string", description: "Relative path within the platform root, e.g. 'apps/perps-app/src/app/[[...params]]/page.tsx'" },
90
          startLine: { type: "number", description: "First line to read (1-indexed)" },
91
          endLine: { type: "number", description: "Last line to read (1-indexed)" },
92
        },
93
        required: ["path"],
94
      },
95
    },
96
    {
97
      name: "lookup_partner",
98
      description:
99
        "Look up which third-party provider powers a specific Infinex product feature. Use this first when the copy mentions a feature (perps, prediction markets, swap routing, passkeys). Faster and more reliable than web search for partnership facts.",
100
      input_schema: {
101
        type: "object",
102
        properties: {
103
          feature: { type: "string", description: "Feature name, e.g. 'perps_trading', 'prediction_markets', 'passkey_custody', 'swap_routing'" },
104
          search: { type: "string", description: "Fuzzy search term if you don't know the exact feature name" },
105
        },
106
      },
107
    },
108
    {
109
      name: "fetch_infinex_page",
110
      description:
111
        "Fetch a page from any *.infinex.xyz domain. Product apps live on subdomains: perps -> https://perps.app.infinex.xyz, prediction markets -> https://predict.app.infinex.xyz, main site -> https://infinex.xyz. Pass a full URL for subdomains. Results are cached for 24h.",
112
      input_schema: {
113
        type: "object",
114
        properties: {
115
          path: { type: "string", description: "Full URL for subdomains (e.g. 'https://perps.app.infinex.xyz') or path for main site (e.g. '/earn')" },
116
        },
117
        required: ["path"],
118
      },
119
    },
120
    {
121
      name: "fetch_public_page",
122
      description:
123
        "Fetch a public HTTPS page and return stripped text. Use for official provider/protocol docs when platform code has not landed yet, especially for product mechanics: what enters, what converts, what settles, and what the user sees. Rejects local/private hosts. Results cached for 24h.",
124
      input_schema: {
125
        type: "object",
126
        properties: {
127
          url: { type: "string", description: "Full HTTPS URL to an official docs/product page" },
128
        },
129
        required: ["url"],
130
      },
131
    },
132
    {
133
      name: "fetch_rendered_page",
134
      description:
135
        "Fetch the live rendered view of a web page (Chrome headless via agent-browser). Returns what a user actually sees after JS has run, with frontend aliasing/normalization applied. Use before raw API calls when verifying any claim about what shows up in product. Optional clickAfterLoad opens a dropdown/menu. Output capped at 6000 chars.",
136
      input_schema: {
137
        type: "object",
138
        properties: {
139
          url: { type: "string", description: "Full URL to fetch (https:// preferred)" },
140
          selector: { type: "string", description: "Optional CSS selector to scope the extraction (default: body)" },
141
          waitFor: { type: "string", enum: ["networkidle", "load", "domcontentloaded"], description: "Wait strategy after navigation (default: networkidle)" },
142
          clickAfterLoad: { type: "string", description: "Optional CSS selector or @ref to click after page loads" },
143
          waitForText: { type: "string", description: "Optional substring to wait for before extracting" },
144
        },
145
        required: ["url"],
146
      },
147
    },
148
    {
149
      name: "fetch_json_api",
150
      description:
151
        "Fetch a public JSON API endpoint and return the parsed response. Use for live protocol data that is not in platform code yet. Prefer this over web search for machine-readable facts. Results capped at 4000 chars.",
152
      input_schema: {
153
        type: "object",
154
        properties: {
155
          url: { type: "string", description: "Full URL to fetch (GET)" },
156
          method: { type: "string", enum: ["GET", "POST"], description: "HTTP method (default GET)" },
157
          body: { type: "object", description: "JSON body for POST requests" },
158
          jq: { type: "string", description: "Optional: a plain-English description of which fields to inspect" },
159
        },
160
        required: ["url"],
161
      },
162
    },
163
    {
164
      name: "infinex_web_search",
165
      description:
166
        "Search the web via Grok/X for external verification. Use when internal sources do not resolve a claim, or when you need to verify that a partnership is current and publicly announced. Requires projectjin CLI.",
167
      input_schema: {
168
        type: "object",
169
        properties: {
170
          context: { type: "string", maxLength: 500, description: "Why you are running this search" },
171
          query: { type: "string", description: "Search query" },
172
        },
173
        required: ["context", "query"],
174
      },
175
    },
176
    {
177
      name: "infinex_search_recent_posts",
178
      description:
179
        "Search recent X/Twitter posts. Use as follow-up when web search is inconclusive, or when you need to verify a recent announcement by Infinex or a named partner. Requires projectjin CLI.",
180
      input_schema: {
181
        type: "object",
182
        properties: {
183
          context: { type: "string", maxLength: 500, description: "Why you are running this search" },
184
          query: { type: "string", description: "X API search syntax; supports from:user, exact quotes, etc." },
185
          hoursBack: { type: "number", description: "Hours back to search (1..168, default 24)" },
186
          maxResults: { type: "number", description: "Results to return (10..100, default 10)" },
187
          sortOrder: { type: "string", enum: ["recency", "relevancy"] },
188
        },
189
        required: ["context", "query"],
190
      },
191
    },
192
  ];
193
}
194
195
export function buildGrounderTools(): AnthropicTool[] {
196
  return [
197
    ...buildResearchTools(),
198
    {
199
      name: "record_fact",
200
      description:
201
        "Record a verified fact. Call this when you have found a reliable source for a claim in the source copy. Record the most specific and authoritative version of the fact.",
202
      input_schema: {
203
        type: "object",
204
        properties: {
205
          category: {
206
            type: "string",
207
            enum: ["partner", "capability", "number", "chain", "product", "url", "date", "ticker"],
208
            description: "The type of fact",
209
          },
210
          claim: { type: "string", description: "Human-readable claim, e.g. 'perps venue provider' or 'max leverage multiplier'" },
211
          value: { type: "string", description: "The verified value, e.g. 'Hyperliquid' or '50'" },
212
          source: {
213
            type: "string",
214
            enum: ["platform-code", "platform-docs", "partner-registry", "provider-docs", "infinex-page", "web-search", "operator-input"],
215
          },
216
          source_ref: { type: "string", description: "File path with line number, or URL" },
217
          confidence: { type: "number", minimum: 0, maximum: 1, description: "Confidence in this fact (0..1)" },
218
        },
219
        required: ["category", "claim", "value", "source", "source_ref", "confidence"],
220
      },
221
    },
222
    {
223
      name: "mark_unverifiable",
224
      description:
225
        "Mark a claim that could not be verified through any available source. This is an honest signal; better to mark unverifiable than to guess.",
226
      input_schema: {
227
        type: "object",
228
        properties: {
229
          claim: { type: "string", description: "The claim that could not be verified" },
230
          reason: { type: "string", description: "Why it could not be verified" },
231
        },
232
        required: ["claim", "reason"],
233
      },
234
    },
235
    {
236
      name: "done_grounding",
237
      description:
238
        "Signal that grounding is complete. Call when every potentially-verifiable claim in the source copy is either recorded via record_fact or marked via mark_unverifiable.",
239
      input_schema: {
240
        type: "object",
241
        properties: {
242
          summary: { type: "string", description: "One-line summary of what was found" },
243
        },
244
        required: ["summary"],
245
      },
246
    },
247
  ];
248
}
249
250
export async function executeResearchToolCall(
251
  toolName: string,
252
  toolInput: Record<string, unknown>,
253
  toolUseId: string,
254
): Promise<ResearchToolResult> {
255
  const raw = await executeResearchToolCallInner(toolName, toolInput);
256
  const content = raw.length > TOOL_RESULT_CAP
257
    ? raw.slice(0, TOOL_RESULT_CAP) + `\n[truncated - ${raw.length - TOOL_RESULT_CAP} chars omitted]`
258
    : raw;
259
  return { tool_use_id: toolUseId, content };
260
}
261
262
async function executeResearchToolCallInner(
263
  toolName: string,
264
  input: Record<string, unknown>,
265
): Promise<string> {
266
  try {
267
    switch (toolName) {
268
      case "grep_platform_code": {
269
        const matches = await grepPlatform(
270
          String(input.pattern ?? ""),
271
          {
272
            ...(typeof input.path === "string" ? { path: input.path } : {}),
273
            ...(typeof input.glob === "string" ? { glob: input.glob } : {}),
274
            ...(typeof input.maxResults === "number" ? { maxResults: input.maxResults } : {}),
275
          },
276
        );
277
        if (matches.length === 0) return "No matches found.";
278
        return matches.map((m) => `${m.file}:${m.line}: ${m.preview}`).join("\n");
279
      }
280
281
      case "read_platform_file": {
282
        const content = await readPlatformFile(String(input.path ?? ""), {
283
          ...(typeof input.startLine === "number" ? { startLine: input.startLine } : {}),
284
          ...(typeof input.endLine === "number" ? { endLine: input.endLine } : {}),
285
        });
286
        return content.slice(0, 8000);
287
      }
288
289
      case "lookup_partner": {
290
        if (typeof input.feature === "string") {
291
          const entry = lookupFeature(input.feature);
292
          if (entry) return JSON.stringify(entry, null, 2);
293
        }
294
        if (typeof input.search === "string") {
295
          const results = searchFeatures(input.search);
296
          if (results.length > 0) return JSON.stringify(results, null, 2);
297
        }
298
        const all = allFeatures().map((e: PartnerRegistryEntry) => e.feature).join(", ");
299
        return `No entry found. Available features: ${all}`;
300
      }
301
302
      case "fetch_rendered_page": {
303
        const url = String(input.url ?? "");
304
        const result = await fetchRenderedPage(url, {
305
          ...(typeof input.selector === "string" ? { selector: input.selector } : {}),
306
          ...(input.waitFor === "networkidle" || input.waitFor === "load" || input.waitFor === "domcontentloaded"
307
            ? { waitFor: input.waitFor } : {}),
308
          ...(typeof input.clickAfterLoad === "string" ? { clickAfterLoad: input.clickAfterLoad } : {}),
309
          ...(typeof input.waitForText === "string" ? { waitForText: input.waitForText } : {}),
310
        });
311
        return `URL: ${result.url}\n\n${result.text}`;
312
      }
313
314
      case "fetch_public_page": {
315
        const result = await fetchPublicPage(String(input.url ?? ""));
316
        return `URL: ${result.url}\n\n${result.text}`;
317
      }
318
319
      case "fetch_json_api": {
320
        const url = String(input.url ?? "");
321
        const parsed = (() => { try { return new URL(url); } catch { return null; } })();
322
        if (!parsed || parsed.protocol !== "https:") {
323
          return `fetch_json_api rejected: only https:// URLs are allowed (got ${url})`;
324
        }
325
        parsed.username = "";
326
        parsed.password = "";
327
        if (!isAllowedJsonApiHost(parsed.hostname)) {
328
          return `fetch_json_api rejected: host is not in allowlist (${parsed.hostname})`;
329
        }
330
        const method = input.method === "POST" ? "POST" : "GET";
331
        const body = method === "POST" && input.body ? JSON.stringify(input.body) : undefined;
332
        const resp = await fetch(parsed.toString(), {
333
          method,
334
          headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json", "User-Agent": "comms-factory/research-tools (internal)" },
335
          signal: AbortSignal.timeout(15_000),
336
          ...(body ? { body } : {}),
337
        });
338
        if (!resp.ok) {
339
          return `HTTP ${resp.status} from ${parsed.toString()}. Do not retry; mark dependent claims as unverifiable or fail validation.`;
340
        }
341
        const json = await resp.json();
342
        return compactJsonForModel(json);
343
      }
344
345
      case "fetch_infinex_page": {
346
        const result = await fetchInfinexPage(String(input.path ?? "/"));
347
        return result.text.slice(0, 6000);
348
      }
349
350
      case "infinex_web_search": {
351
        const result = await infinexWebSearch({
352
          context: String(input.context ?? ""),
353
          query: String(input.query ?? ""),
354
        });
355
        return `Summary: ${result.summary}\nCitations: ${result.citations.join(", ")}`;
356
      }
357
358
      case "infinex_search_recent_posts": {
359
        const result = await infinexSearchRecentPosts({
360
          context: String(input.context ?? ""),
361
          query: String(input.query ?? ""),
362
          ...(typeof input.hoursBack === "number" ? { hoursBack: input.hoursBack } : {}),
363
          ...(typeof input.maxResults === "number" ? { maxResults: input.maxResults } : {}),
364
          ...(input.sortOrder === "recency" || input.sortOrder === "relevancy" ? { sortOrder: input.sortOrder } : {}),
365
        });
366
        if (result.posts.length === 0) return "No recent posts found.";
367
        return result.posts.map((p) => `@${p.author} (${p.createdAt}): ${p.text}\n${p.url}`).join("\n\n");
368
      }
369
370
      default:
371
        return `Unknown tool: ${toolName}`;
372
    }
373
  } catch (err) {
374
    if (err instanceof ProjectjinUnavailableError || err instanceof AgentBrowserUnavailableError) {
375
      return `UNAVAILABLE: ${err.message}`;
376
    }
377
    return `ERROR: ${String(err)}`;
378
  }
379
}
380
381
export function compactJsonForModel(json: unknown): string {
382
  if (
383
    Array.isArray(json) && json.length === 2 &&
384
    json[0] && typeof json[0] === "object" && "universe" in (json[0] as object) &&
385
    Array.isArray((json[0] as Record<string, unknown>).universe) &&
386
    Array.isArray(json[1])
387
  ) {
388
    type HlToken = { name: string; fullName?: string | null; index: number; evmContract?: unknown };
389
    type HlMarket = { name: string; tokens: number[]; index: number; isCanonical?: boolean };
390
    const meta = json[0] as { universe: HlMarket[]; tokens: HlToken[] };
391
    const ctxs = json[1] as Array<{ coin?: string; dayNtlVlm?: string; [k: string]: unknown }>;
392
    const tokenMap = new Map<number, HlToken>(meta.tokens.map((t) => [t.index, t]));
393
    const ctxByCoin = new Map<string, { dayNtlVlm?: string }>(
394
      ctxs.filter((c): c is { coin: string; dayNtlVlm?: string } => typeof c.coin === "string")
395
        .map((c) => [c.coin, c]),
396
    );
397
    const seen = new Map<string, { pair: string; vol: number; fullName: string }>();
398
    for (const m of meta.universe) {
399
      if (m.tokens[1] !== 0) continue;
400
      const baseIdx = m.tokens[0];
401
      const tok = baseIdx !== undefined ? tokenMap.get(baseIdx) : undefined;
402
      const baseName = tok?.name ?? m.name;
403
      const fullName = tok?.fullName ?? "";
404
      const ctx = ctxByCoin.get(m.name);
405
      const vol = parseFloat(ctx?.dayNtlVlm ?? "0");
406
      const existing = seen.get(baseName);
407
      if (!existing || vol > existing.vol) {
408
        seen.set(baseName, { pair: `${baseName}/USDC`, vol, fullName });
409
      }
410
    }
411
    const rows = [...seen.values()].filter((r) => r.vol > 100_000).sort((a, b) => b.vol - a.vol);
412
    const lines = [
413
      `Hyperliquid spot markets - ${rows.length} with >$100K 24h volume (use this number in copy).`,
414
      `On-chain universe is ${meta.universe.length} but ${meta.universe.length - rows.length} had <$100K or zero volume - long-tail HIP-1 noise, not meaningfully tradeable. DO NOT use ${meta.universe.length} as a market count in facts.`,
415
    ];
416
    for (const r of rows) {
417
      const label = r.fullName ? ` (${r.fullName})` : "";
418
      lines.push(`  ${r.pair}: 24h vol $${(r.vol / 1e6).toFixed(2)}M${label}`);
419
    }
420
    lines.push("  (non-USDC pairs and <$100K pairs excluded from above)");
421
    return lines.join("\n");
422
  }
423
424
  if (json && typeof json === "object" && !Array.isArray(json) &&
425
    "universe" in (json as object) &&
426
    Array.isArray((json as Record<string, unknown>).universe)
427
  ) {
428
    type HlToken = { name: string; fullName?: string | null; index: number };
429
    type HlMarket = { name: string; tokens: number[] };
430
    const meta = json as { universe: HlMarket[]; tokens: HlToken[] };
431
    const tokenMap = new Map<number, HlToken>(meta.tokens.map((t) => [t.index, t]));
432
    const names = meta.universe.map((m) => {
433
      const baseIdx = m.tokens[0];
434
      const tok = baseIdx !== undefined ? tokenMap.get(baseIdx) : undefined;
435
      const name = tok?.name ?? m.name;
436
      const full = tok?.fullName ? ` (${tok.fullName})` : "";
437
      return `${name}/USDC${full}`;
438
    }).join(", ");
439
    return `Hyperliquid spot markets (${meta.universe.length}): ${names}`;
440
  }
441
442
  const minified = JSON.stringify(json);
443
  if (minified.length <= TOOL_RESULT_CAP) return minified;
444
445
  if (Array.isArray(json)) {
446
    const sample = JSON.stringify(json.slice(0, 3));
447
    return `Array of ${json.length} items. First 3: ${sample}\n[${json.length - 3} more items omitted]`;
448
  }
449
450
  return minified.slice(0, TOOL_RESULT_CAP) + `\n[truncated - ${minified.length - TOOL_RESULT_CAP} chars omitted]`;
451
}
452
453
function isAllowedJsonApiHost(hostname: string): boolean {
454
  const allowlist = (process.env.FACT_GROUNDER_JSON_API_ALLOWLIST ?? DEFAULT_JSON_API_ALLOWLIST.join(","))
455
    .split(",")
456
    .map((host) => host.trim().toLowerCase())
457
    .filter(Boolean);
458
  return allowlist.includes(hostname.toLowerCase());
459
}
460

src/orchestrator.ts

389 lines
1
import type { ReleaseCard } from "./card.js";
2
import type { Candidate } from "./generator.js";
3
import {
4
  runHistoryGuards,
5
  type HistoryGuardResult,
6
  type ShippedCopyRecord,
7
} from "./history-guards.js";
8
import {
9
  auditTextActive,
10
  type ActiveValidationOptions,
11
  type ActiveValidationVerdict,
12
} from "./validator-active.js";
13
import {
14
  auditTextHybrid,
15
  validate,
16
  type HybridValidationVerdict,
17
  type ValidationResult,
18
} from "./validator.js";
19
import type { LLMVoiceAuditOptions } from "./validator-llm.js";
20
import type { BeatSequence, CharacterSpec } from "./voice/types.js";
21
22
/**
23
 * Orchestrator. Per-channel best-candidate picker.
24
 *
25
 * Pipeline contract:
26
 *   generator -> orchestrator.pick() -> renderer -> ship gate
27
 *
28
 * Pick policy:
29
 *   1. Drop any candidate that fails the validator.
30
 *   2. Rank surviving candidates (TODO: ranking model — for now first-passes-wins).
31
 *   3. Return one pick per requested channel, or null when nothing survives.
32
 *
33
 * Channel-specific length/format constraints live HERE, not in the generator —
34
 * the generator produces text, the orchestrator shapes the channel cut.
35
 */
36
37
export interface Pick {
38
  channel: "web" | "x" | "in-product";
39
  candidate: Candidate;
40
  validation: ValidationResult;
41
}
42
43
export interface OrchestrationResult {
44
  picks: Pick[];
45
  rejected: Array<{ candidate: Candidate; validation: ValidationResult }>;
46
}
47
48
export const MAX_ATTEMPTS = 3;
49
50
const CHANNEL_MAX_LEN: Record<Pick["channel"], number> = {
51
  x: 280,
52
  web: 140,
53
  "in-product": 80,
54
};
55
56
export interface OrchestrateOptions {
57
  voice?: CharacterSpec;
58
  beats?: BeatSequence;
59
}
60
61
export interface RetryGenerateContext {
62
  attempt: number;
63
  feedback?: string;
64
}
65
66
export interface RetryAttempt {
67
  attempt: number;
68
  candidates: Candidate[];
69
  result: OrchestrationResult;
70
  feedback: string;
71
}
72
73
export interface RetryOrchestrationResult extends OrchestrationResult {
74
  attempts: RetryAttempt[];
75
  exhausted: boolean;
76
}
77
78
export type GenerateAttempt = (ctx: RetryGenerateContext) => Promise<Candidate[]>;
79
80
/**
81
 * Hybrid orchestration: regex first, then LLM judge for survivors.
82
 * Mirror of `orchestrate()` but async because the LLM is async.
83
 */
84
export interface HybridPick {
85
  channel: Pick["channel"];
86
  candidate: Candidate;
87
  verdict: HybridValidationVerdict;
88
}
89
90
export interface HybridOrchestrationResult {
91
  picks: HybridPick[];
92
  rejected: Array<{ candidate: Candidate; verdict: HybridValidationVerdict }>;
93
}
94
95
export interface ActivePick {
96
  channel: Pick["channel"];
97
  candidate: Candidate;
98
  verdict: ActiveValidationVerdict;
99
  history: HistoryGuardResult;
100
}
101
102
export interface ActiveOrchestrationResult {
103
  picks: ActivePick[];
104
  rejected: Array<{
105
    candidate: Candidate;
106
    verdict: ActiveValidationVerdict;
107
    history?: HistoryGuardResult;
108
  }>;
109
}
110
111
export interface ActiveOrchestrateOptions extends OrchestrateOptions {
112
  active_opts?: Omit<
113
    ActiveValidationOptions,
114
    "card" | "voice" | "beats" | "channel" | "deployed_facts_used" | "not_said"
115
  >;
116
  recentCopy?: ShippedCopyRecord[];
117
  recentCopyByChannel?: Partial<Record<Pick["channel"], ShippedCopyRecord[]>>;
118
  protagonist?: string;
119
}
120
121
export interface ActiveRetryAttempt {
122
  attempt: number;
123
  candidates: Candidate[];
124
  result: ActiveOrchestrationResult;
125
  feedback: string;
126
}
127
128
export interface ActiveRetryOrchestrationResult extends ActiveOrchestrationResult {
129
  attempts: ActiveRetryAttempt[];
130
  exhausted: boolean;
131
}
132
133
export async function orchestrateLLM(
134
  card: ReleaseCard,
135
  candidates: Candidate[],
136
  channels: Array<Pick["channel"]>,
137
  opts: OrchestrateOptions & { llm_opts?: Omit<LLMVoiceAuditOptions, "voice"> } = {},
138
): Promise<HybridOrchestrationResult> {
139
  const rejected: HybridOrchestrationResult["rejected"] = [];
140
  const picks: HybridPick[] = [];
141
  const baseAuditOpts: Parameters<typeof auditTextHybrid>[1] = { card };
142
  if (opts.voice) baseAuditOpts.voice = opts.voice;
143
  if (opts.beats) baseAuditOpts.beats = opts.beats;
144
  if (opts.llm_opts) baseAuditOpts.llm_opts = opts.llm_opts;
145
146
  for (const channel of channels) {
147
    const channelCandidates = candidates.filter((c) => c.channel === channel);
148
    const pool = channelCandidates.length > 0 ? channelCandidates : candidates;
149
150
    let chosen: HybridPick | null = null;
151
    for (const c of pool) {
152
      const auditOpts: Parameters<typeof auditTextHybrid>[1] = { ...baseAuditOpts };
153
      if (c.deployed_facts_used !== undefined) auditOpts.deployed_facts_used = c.deployed_facts_used;
154
      if (c.not_said !== undefined) auditOpts.not_said = c.not_said;
155
      const verdict = await auditTextHybrid(c.text, auditOpts);
156
      if (!verdict.passed) {
157
        rejected.push({ candidate: c, verdict });
158
        continue;
159
      }
160
      if (c.text.length > CHANNEL_MAX_LEN[channel]) {
161
        rejected.push({
162
          candidate: c,
163
          verdict: {
164
            ...verdict,
165
            passed: false,
166
            reason: `length: ${c.text.length} > ${CHANNEL_MAX_LEN[channel]} for channel ${channel}`,
167
          },
168
        });
169
        continue;
170
      }
171
      chosen = { channel, candidate: c, verdict };
172
      break;
173
    }
174
    if (chosen) picks.push(chosen);
175
  }
176
177
  return { picks, rejected };
178
}
179
180
export async function orchestrateActive(
181
  card: ReleaseCard,
182
  candidates: Candidate[],
183
  channels: Array<Pick["channel"]>,
184
  opts: ActiveOrchestrateOptions = {},
185
): Promise<ActiveOrchestrationResult> {
186
  const rejected: ActiveOrchestrationResult["rejected"] = [];
187
  const picks: ActivePick[] = [];
188
189
  for (const channel of channels) {
190
    const channelCandidates = candidates.filter((c) => c.channel === channel);
191
    const pool = channelCandidates.length > 0 ? channelCandidates : candidates;
192
193
    let chosen: ActivePick | null = null;
194
    for (const c of pool) {
195
      const verdict = await auditTextActive(c.text, {
196
        ...(opts.active_opts ?? {}),
197
        card,
198
        channel,
199
        ...(opts.voice !== undefined ? { voice: opts.voice } : {}),
200
        ...(opts.beats !== undefined ? { beats: opts.beats } : {}),
201
        ...(c.deployed_facts_used !== undefined ? { deployed_facts_used: c.deployed_facts_used } : {}),
202
        ...(c.not_said !== undefined ? { not_said: c.not_said } : {}),
203
      });
204
205
      if (!verdict.passed) {
206
        rejected.push({ candidate: c, verdict });
207
        continue;
208
      }
209
210
      if (c.text.length > CHANNEL_MAX_LEN[channel]) {
211
        rejected.push({
212
          candidate: c,
213
          verdict: {
214
            ...verdict,
215
            passed: false,
216
            reason: `length: ${c.text.length} > ${CHANNEL_MAX_LEN[channel]} for channel ${channel}`,
217
          },
218
        });
219
        continue;
220
      }
221
222
      const recentCopy = opts.recentCopyByChannel?.[channel] ?? opts.recentCopy ?? [];
223
      const history = runHistoryGuards(c, {
224
        channel,
225
        ...(opts.voice !== undefined ? { voice: opts.voice } : {}),
226
        recentCopy,
227
        ...(opts.protagonist !== undefined ? { protagonist: opts.protagonist } : {}),
228
      });
229
      if (!history.passed) {
230
        rejected.push({ candidate: c, verdict, history });
231
        continue;
232
      }
233
234
      chosen = { channel, candidate: c, verdict, history };
235
      break;
236
    }
237
    if (chosen) picks.push(chosen);
238
  }
239
240
  return { picks, rejected };
241
}
242
243
export function orchestrate(
244
  card: ReleaseCard,
245
  candidates: Candidate[],
246
  channels: Array<Pick["channel"]>,
247
  opts: OrchestrateOptions = {},
248
): OrchestrationResult {
249
  const rejected: OrchestrationResult["rejected"] = [];
250
  const picks: Pick[] = [];
251
  const validateOpts: Parameters<typeof validate>[1] = {};
252
  if (opts.voice) validateOpts.voice = opts.voice;
253
  if (opts.beats) validateOpts.beats = opts.beats;
254
  validateOpts.card = card;
255
256
  for (const channel of channels) {
257
    const channelCandidates = candidates.filter((c) => c.channel === channel);
258
    const pool = channelCandidates.length > 0 ? channelCandidates : candidates;
259
260
    let chosen: Pick | null = null;
261
    for (const c of pool) {
262
      const candidateValidateOpts: Parameters<typeof validate>[1] = { ...validateOpts };
263
      if (c.deployed_facts_used !== undefined) {
264
        candidateValidateOpts.deployed_facts_used = c.deployed_facts_used;
265
      }
266
      if (c.not_said !== undefined) {
267
        candidateValidateOpts.not_said = c.not_said;
268
      }
269
      const v = validate(c.text, candidateValidateOpts);
270
      if (!v.passed) {
271
        rejected.push({ candidate: c, validation: v });
272
        continue;
273
      }
274
      if (c.text.length > CHANNEL_MAX_LEN[channel]) {
275
        rejected.push({
276
          candidate: c,
277
          validation: {
278
            passed: false,
279
            failures: [
280
              {
281
                rule: "length",
282
                reason: `${c.text.length} > ${CHANNEL_MAX_LEN[channel]} for channel ${channel}`,
283
              },
284
            ],
285
          },
286
        });
287
        continue;
288
      }
289
      chosen = { channel, candidate: c, validation: v };
290
      break; // TODO: real ranking model — for now first-passes-wins
291
    }
292
    if (chosen) picks.push(chosen);
293
  }
294
295
  return { picks, rejected };
296
}
297
298
export async function orchestrateWithRetries(
299
  card: ReleaseCard,
300
  channels: Array<Pick["channel"]>,
301
  generateAttempt: GenerateAttempt,
302
  opts: OrchestrateOptions & { maxAttempts?: number } = {},
303
): Promise<RetryOrchestrationResult> {
304
  const maxAttempts = opts.maxAttempts ?? MAX_ATTEMPTS;
305
  const attempts: RetryAttempt[] = [];
306
  let feedback: string | undefined;
307
  let lastResult: OrchestrationResult = { picks: [], rejected: [] };
308
309
  for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= maxAttempts; attempt++) {
310
    const candidates = await generateAttempt({ attempt, ...(feedback ? { feedback } : {}) });
311
    const result = orchestrate(card, candidates, channels, opts);
312
    feedback = summarizeFailures(result, channels);
313
    attempts.push({ attempt, candidates, result, feedback });
314
    lastResult = result;
315
    if (result.picks.length >= channels.length) {
316
      return { ...result, attempts, exhausted: false };
317
    }
318
  }
319
320
  return { ...lastResult, attempts, exhausted: true };
321
}
322
323
export async function orchestrateActiveWithRetries(
324
  card: ReleaseCard,
325
  channels: Array<Pick["channel"]>,
326
  generateAttempt: GenerateAttempt,
327
  opts: ActiveOrchestrateOptions & { maxAttempts?: number } = {},
328
): Promise<ActiveRetryOrchestrationResult> {
329
  const maxAttempts = opts.maxAttempts ?? MAX_ATTEMPTS;
330
  const attempts: ActiveRetryAttempt[] = [];
331
  let feedback: string | undefined;
332
  let lastResult: ActiveOrchestrationResult = { picks: [], rejected: [] };
333
334
  for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= maxAttempts; attempt++) {
335
    const candidates = await generateAttempt({ attempt, ...(feedback ? { feedback } : {}) });
336
    const result = await orchestrateActive(card, candidates, channels, opts);
337
    feedback = summarizeActiveFailures(result, channels);
338
    attempts.push({ attempt, candidates, result, feedback });
339
    lastResult = result;
340
    if (result.picks.length >= channels.length) {
341
      return { ...result, attempts, exhausted: false };
342
    }
343
  }
344
345
  return { ...lastResult, attempts, exhausted: true };
346
}
347
348
function summarizeFailures(result: OrchestrationResult, channels: Array<Pick["channel"]>): string {
349
  const pickedChannels = new Set(result.picks.map((p) => p.channel));
350
  const missing = channels.filter((channel) => !pickedChannels.has(channel));
351
  const lines: string[] = [];
352
  if (missing.length > 0) lines.push(`Missing valid picks for channels: ${missing.join(", ")}`);
353
354
  const seen = new Set<string>();
355
  for (const rejected of result.rejected) {
356
    const firstFailure = rejected.validation.failures[0];
357
    if (!firstFailure) continue;
358
    const key = `${rejected.candidate.id}:${firstFailure.rule}:${firstFailure.reason}`;
359
    if (seen.has(key)) continue;
360
    seen.add(key);
361
    lines.push(`${rejected.candidate.id}: ${firstFailure.rule} — ${firstFailure.reason}`);
362
    if (lines.length >= 8) break;
363
  }
364
365
  return lines.join("\n") || "No candidate survived validation.";
366
}
367
368
function summarizeActiveFailures(result: ActiveOrchestrationResult, channels: Array<Pick["channel"]>): string {
369
  const pickedChannels = new Set(result.picks.map((p) => p.channel));
370
  const missing = channels.filter((channel) => !pickedChannels.has(channel));
371
  const lines: string[] = [];
372
  if (missing.length > 0) lines.push(`Missing valid active picks for channels: ${missing.join(", ")}`);
373
374
  const seen = new Set<string>();
375
  for (const rejected of result.rejected) {
376
    const historyFailure = rejected.history?.failures[0];
377
    const reason = historyFailure
378
      ? `${historyFailure.rule}: ${historyFailure.reason}`
379
      : rejected.verdict.reason ?? "active-validator-fail";
380
    const key = `${rejected.candidate.id}:${reason}`;
381
    if (seen.has(key)) continue;
382
    seen.add(key);
383
    lines.push(`${rejected.candidate.id}: ${reason}`);
384
    if (lines.length >= 8) break;
385
  }
386
387
  return lines.join("\n") || "No candidate survived active validation.";
388
}
389

Tests

The guardrails that currently lock validator and Director behavior.

src/__tests__/actor-director.test.ts

470 lines
1
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
2
import type { ReleaseCard } from "../card.js";
3
import {
4
  actorOutputToCandidates,
5
  buildActorAssignmentMessage,
6
  buildDirectorUserMessage,
7
  generateActorAttempt,
8
  parseActorOutput,
9
  type ActorOutput,
10
} from "../actor-director.js";
11
import { buildActorMemoryPack, buildDirectorMemoryPack } from "../actor-memory.js";
12
import { orchestrateActorDirectorWithRetries, validateActorMovement } from "../actor-orchestrator.js";
13
import { INFINEX_VOICE } from "../voice/infinex.js";
14
import { ALL_TEMPO_NAMES } from "../voice/types.js";
15
16
const CARD: ReleaseCard = {
17
  id: "actor-test",
18
  title: "Bridge deposits",
19
  ship_date: "2026-05-26",
20
  audience: ["web", "x"],
21
  deployed_facts: [
22
    "Bridge.xyz is the fiat deposit provider for Infinex",
23
  ],
24
  kind: "split",
25
  from: "bank deposit",
26
  to: "USDC in Infinex",
27
  split_semantics: "fiat enters and settles as USDC",
28
  through_action: "to reveal that the bank wall moved off the user",
29
  obstacle: "reader expects rails mechanics",
30
  reader_prior: "bank and wallet are separate systems",
31
  not_the_point: "do not list rails",
32
  lining: "surface: a deposit route. underneath: responsibility moved into the account.",
33
};
34
35
describe("actor/director architecture", () => {
36
  it("builds source-indexed actor and director memory from canonical artifacts", () => {
37
    const actor = buildActorMemoryPack(INFINEX_VOICE);
38
    const director = buildDirectorMemoryPack(INFINEX_VOICE);
39
40
    expect(actor.system_prompt).toContain("Canonical source index");
41
    expect(actor.system_prompt).toContain("Mirodan Ch. I: Basic Concepts");
42
    expect(actor.system_prompt).toContain("Actor Memory");
43
    expect(actor.system_prompt).toContain("You are the Actor");
44
    expect(actor.source_index.filter((entry) => entry.role === "chapter").every((entry) => entry.exists)).toBe(true);
45
    expect(actor.source_index.filter((entry) => entry.role === "chapter").every((entry) => entry.sha256)).toBe(true);
46
47
    expect(director.system_prompt).toContain("Director Memory");
48
    expect(director.system_prompt).toContain("Classify primary tempo from all 24 tempi");
49
    expect(director.system_prompt).toContain("flexible/indirect pathing");
50
    expect(director.system_prompt).toContain("Inner Attitudes are two-Motion-Factor compounds");
51
    expect(director.system_prompt).toContain("Mobile (Time + Flow)");
52
    expect(director.system_prompt).toContain("Remote (Space + Flow)");
53
    expect(director.system_prompt).toContain("Awake (Space + Time)");
54
    expect(director.system_prompt).toContain("canonical shorthand");
55
    expect(director.system_prompt).toContain("Commanding demonstration or acceptance of a 'bold resolve'");
56
    expect(director.system_prompt).toContain("Directional intent is orthogonal to factor shape and motor");
57
    expect(director.system_prompt).not.toContain("operator hint");
58
    expect(director.system_prompt).not.toContain("institutional landing");
59
    expect(director.system_prompt).toContain("Voice Mirodan kernel");
60
    expect(director.system_prompt).toContain("Voice drive table");
61
    expect(director.system_prompt).toContain("stable|penetrating|flow -> primary=spell");
62
    expect(director.source_index.some((entry) => entry.id === "source-location-memory" && entry.exists)).toBe(true);
63
  });
64
65
  it("gives the Director all 24 tempi and evidence requirements", () => {
66
    const director = buildDirectorMemoryPack(INFINEX_VOICE);
67
    for (const tempo of ALL_TEMPO_NAMES) {

      expect(director.system_prompt).toContain(`- ${tempo}:`);
69

70
    expect(director.system_prompt).toContain("If you call something Flexible");
71
    expect(director.system_prompt).toContain("If you call something Strong");
72
    expect(director.system_prompt).toContain("working actions");
73
  });
74
75
  it("parses actor table work and performances without declared tempo fields", () => {

    const parsed = parseActorOutput(JSON.stringify(actorOutput("The wall moved.", "The wall moved.")), ["x", "web"]);
77

78
79

80

81

314
function actorOutput(
315
  xText: string,
316
  webText: string,
317
  opts: {
318
    xOptions?: string[];
319
    webOptions?: string[];
320
    selectedX?: number;
321
    selectedWeb?: number;
322
  } = {},
323
): ActorOutput {
324
  const not_said = CARD.deployed_facts.map((fact) => ({ fact, reason: "not needed for this line" }));
325
  const xOptions = opts.xOptions ?? [xText, `${xText} 2`, `${xText} 3`, `${xText} 4`, `${xText} 5`];
326
  const webOptions = opts.webOptions ?? [webText, `${webText} 2`, `${webText} 3`, `${webText} 4`, `${webText} 5`];
327
  return {
328
    warmup: {
329
      mode: "daily_pages",
330
      daily_pages: {
331
        page_1_given_circumstances: "Fixture daily-pages content for parser coverage; not production Actor guidance.",
332
        page_2_character_rehearsal: "Fixture character-rehearsal content.",
333
        page_3_false_starts_and_adjustments: "Fixture false-start content.",
334
        left_right_choices: [{
335
          left: "fixture left option",
336
          right: "fixture right option",
337
          decision: "fixture decision",
338
        }],
339
        what_felt_false: ["fixture rejected option"],
340
        warmed_state: "fixture warmed state",
341
      },
342
      notes: ["fixture warmup note"],
343
      warmed_state: "fixture warmed state",
344
    },
345
    table_work: {
346
      thesis: "Fixture thesis.",
347
      through_action: "to reveal the fixture result",
348
      obstacle: "fixture obstacle",
349
      reader_prior: "fixture prior",
350
      lining: "fixture surface and lining",
351
      not_the_point: "fixture exclusion",
352
      channel_beat_plans: {
353
        x: [
354
          {
355
            verb: "to reveal",
356
            working_action: "pressing",
357
            physical_score: "fixture pressing score",
358
            micro_objective: "fixture micro objective",
359
            obstacle_local: "fixture obstacle",
360
          },
361
          {
362
            verb: "to reveal",
363
            working_action: "punching",
364
            preparation_from: "pressing",
365
            physical_score: "fixture punching score",
366
            micro_objective: "fixture micro objective",
367
            obstacle_local: "fixture obstacle",
368
          },
369
        ],
370
        web: [
371
          {
372
            verb: "to reveal",
373
            working_action: "pressing",
374
            physical_score: "fixture pressing score",
375
            micro_objective: "fixture micro objective",
376
            obstacle_local: "fixture obstacle",
377
          },
378
        ],
379
      },
380
    },
381
    performances: {
382
      x: xOptions.map((text, i) => ({
383
        text,
384
        rationale: `x ${i + 1}`,
385
        movement_receipt: [{
386
          text_span: text,
387
          objective_verb: "to reveal",
388
          working_action: "punching",
389
          evidence: "fixture punching receipt",
390
        }],
391
        deployed_facts_used: [],
392
        not_said,
393
      })),
394
      web: webOptions.map((text, i) => ({
395
        text,
396
        rationale: `web ${i + 1}`,
397
        movement_receipt: [{
398
          text_span: text,
399
          objective_verb: "to reveal",
400
          working_action: "pressing",
401
          evidence: "fixture pressing receipt",
402
        }],
403
        deployed_facts_used: [],
404
        not_said,
405
      })),
406
    },
407
    selected_performances: {
408
      x: {
409
        selected_option: opts.selectedX ?? 1,
410
        selection_rationale: "strongest x performance",
411
      },
412
      web: {
413
        selected_option: opts.selectedWeb ?? 1,
414
        selection_rationale: "strongest web performance",
415
      },
416
    },
417
  };
418
}
419
420
function directorOutput(passed: boolean) {
421
  return {
422
    passed,
423
    primary_tempo: passed ? "sombre" : "commanding",
424
    primary_confidence: 0.8,
425
    motion_evidence: {
426
      weight: {
427
        pole: "Strong",
428
        evidence: ["observation: fixture weight evidence"],
429
        source_refs: ["combined-reference §8.1 working-action table"],
430
      },
431
      space: {
432
        pole: "Direct",
433
        evidence: ["observation: fixture space evidence"],
434
        source_refs: ["combined-reference §8.1 working-action table"],
435
      },
436
      time: passed
437
        ? {
438
            pole: "Sustained",
439
            evidence: ["observation: fixture sustained evidence"],
440
            source_refs: ["combined-reference §8.1 working-action table"],
441
          }
442
        : {
443
            pole: "Quick",
444
            evidence: ["observation: fixture quick evidence"],
445
            source_refs: ["combined-reference §8.1 working-action table"],
446
          },
447
      flow: {
448
        pole: "Bound",
449
        evidence: ["observation: fixture flow evidence"],
450
        source_refs: ["combined-reference §5 fusion/flow notes"],
451
      },
452
    },
453
    working_actions: [{
454
      action: passed ? "pressing" : "punching",
455
      evidence: passed ? "observation: fixture pressing evidence" : "observation: fixture punching evidence",
456
      source_refs: ["combined-reference §8.1 working-action table", "combined-reference §8.2 prep hierarchy"],
457
    }],
458
    drive_read: passed ? "spell" : "passion",
459
    placement_read: passed ? "Stable + Penetrating + Flow" : "Stable + Penetrating + Time",
460
    infinex_fit: {
461
      legal: passed,
462
      reason: passed ? "fixture pass" : "fixture fail",
463
      nearest_allowed_read: "sombre",
464
    },
465
    factual_issues: [],
466
    voice_issues: passed ? [] : ["fixture voice issue"],
467
    notes_for_actor: passed ? [] : ["Fixture note for retry handling."],
468
  };
469
}
470

src/__tests__/validator.test.ts

import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
2
import type { ReleaseCard } from "../card.js";
3
import {
4
  auditClaimContract,
5
  auditClaimTripwires,
6
  auditBeats,
7
  auditTextHybrid,
8
  auditUnsupportedClaims,
9
  classifyTempoBlind,
10
  rejectAIslop,
11
  rejectAntagonism,
12
  rejectClaimedPalettes,
13
  rejectCliches,
14
  rejectKainBaggage,
15
  rejectListicleVoice,
16
  rejectVisualSlop,
17
  validate,
18
} from "../validator.js";
19
import type { LLMVoiceAuditOptions } from "../validator-llm.js";
20
import { INFINEX_VOICE } from "../voice/infinex.js";
21
22
const CARD: ReleaseCard = {
23
  id: "test-launch",
24
  kind: "launch-tier",
25
  title: "Fact A Launch",
26
  ship_date: "2026-05-13",
27
  audience: ["x"],
28
  deployed_facts: ["Fact A is live", "Fact B stays private"],
29
  headline: "Fact A",
30
  tier_reason: "test card",
31
};
32
33
describe("rejectCliches", () => {
34
  it("flags 'game-changer'", () => {
35
    expect(rejectCliches("a real game-changer for trading").passed).toBe(false);
36
  });
37
  it("flags 'unlock'", () => {
38
    expect(rejectCliches("Unlocking new yield primitives").passed).toBe(false);
39
  });
40
  it("flags 'seamless'", () => {
41
    expect(rejectCliches("a seamless onboarding flow").passed).toBe(false);
42
  });
43
  it("flags 'paradigm'", () => {
44
    expect(rejectCliches("a paradigm shift for DeFi").passed).toBe(false);
45
  });
46
  it("flags 'leverage' as verb", () => {
47
    expect(rejectCliches("leverage your portfolio").passed).toBe(false);
48
  });
49
  it("does NOT flag 'leverage ratio' (financial term, not the cliché)", () => {
50
    expect(rejectCliches("leverage ratio capped at 10x").passed).toBe(true);
51
  });
52
  it("does NOT flag 'leveraged long' (financial term)", () => {
53
    expect(rejectCliches("a leveraged long on ETH").passed).toBe(true);
54
  });
55
  it("passes clean copy", () => {
56
    expect(rejectCliches("Bridge USDC from Base to Arbitrum in one click.").passed).toBe(true);
57
  });
58
  it("passes single short headline", () => {
59
    expect(rejectCliches("JUST IN: Polymarket integrates Phantom Wallet.").passed).toBe(true);
60
  });
61
});

describe("rejectAIslop", () => {
109
  it("flags 'innovative'", () => {

111

112
  it("flags 'cutting-edge'", () => {
113

114
  });
115
  it("flags 'users will appreciate'", () => {

  it("flags ANY em-dash (zero-tolerance per operator 2026-05-25)", () => {

126

128

129
    ).toBe(false);

  });

  it("passes clean tweet-length copy", () => {

describe("validate (composite)", () => {
218
  it("passes a clean Polymarket-style caption", () => {
219
    const r = validate("JUST IN: Spot perps now live on Base.");
220
    expect(r.passed).toBe(true);
221
    expect(r.failures).toEqual([]);
222
  });
223
  it("accumulates multiple failures", () => {
224
    const r = validate(
225
      "We're thrilled to unlock a game-changing, seamless, innovative experience.",
226
    );
227
    expect(r.passed).toBe(false);
228
    expect(r.failures.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);
229
  });
230
  it("returns the failing rule names", () => {
231
    const r = validate("3 reasons this is a paradigm shift.");
232
    expect(r.passed).toBe(false);
233
    const names = r.failures.map((f) => f.rule);
234
    expect(names).toContain("cliches");
235
    expect(names).toContain("listicle-voice");
236
  });
237
});
238
