describe("auditClaimContract", () => {
240
  it("fails when a candidate omits fact receipts", () => {
241
    const failures = auditClaimContract("Fact A is live.", { card: CARD });
242
    expect(failures.map((f) => f.reason)).toContain("candidate omitted deployed_facts_used receipt");
243
    expect(failures.map((f) => f.reason)).toContain("candidate omitted not_said receipt");
244
  });
245
246
  it("fails when deployed_facts_used contains a non-card fact", () => {
247
    const failures = auditClaimContract("Fact A is live.", {
248
      card: CARD,
249
      deployed_facts_used: ["Fact A is live", "Invented claim"],
250
      not_said: [{ fact: "Fact B stays private", reason: "not needed for this line" }],
251
    });
252
    expect(failures.map((f) => f.reason)).toContain(
253
      'deployed_facts_used contains non-card fact: "Invented claim"',
254
    );
255
  });
256
257
  it("passes when used facts are present and unused facts are accounted for", () => {
258
    const failures = auditClaimContract("Fact A is live.", {
259
      card: CARD,
260
      deployed_facts_used: ["Fact A is live"],
261
      not_said: [{ fact: "Fact B stays private", reason: "would dilute the short launch line" }],
262
    });
263
    expect(failures).toEqual([]);
264
  });
265
266
  it("does not make validate fail hard on strict receipt bookkeeping by default", () => {
267
    const r = validate("Fact A is live.", {
268
      card: CARD,
269
      deployed_facts_used: ["Invented claim"],
270
      not_said: [{ fact: "Fact B stays private", reason: "not used" }],
271
    });
272
    expect(r.passed).toBe(true);
273
    expect(r.failures.map((f) => f.rule)).not.toContain("fact-contract");
274
  });
275
276
  it("can still run the old strict receipt audit when explicitly requested", () => {
277
    const r = validate("Fact A is live.", {
278
      card: CARD,
279
      deployed_facts_used: ["Invented claim"],
280
      not_said: [{ fact: "Fact B stays private", reason: "not used" }],
281
      fact_contract: "strict",
282
    });
283
    expect(r.passed).toBe(false);
284
    expect(r.failures.map((f) => f.rule)).toContain("fact-contract");
285
  });
286
287
  it("fails when copy asserts an extra numeric claim outside deployed_facts", () => {
288
    const failures = auditClaimContract("Fact A is live. Also supports 100 markets.", {
289
      card: CARD,
290
      deployed_facts_used: ["Fact A is live"],
291
      not_said: [{ fact: "Fact B stays private", reason: "not relevant" }],
292
    });
293
    expect(failures.map((f) => f.rule)).toContain("unsupported-claim");
294
    expect(failures.map((f) => f.reason).join("\n")).toContain("100");
295
  });
296
297
  it("fails on unsupported superlative/status claims even with a valid receipt", () => {
298
    const failures = auditUnsupportedClaims("Fact A is live. The first wallet to do this.", CARD);
299
    expect(failures.map((f) => f.rule)).toContain("unsupported-claim");
300
    expect(failures.map((f) => f.reason).join("\n")).toContain("first wallet");
301
  });
302
303
  it("fails on unsupported readiness claims even when the launch fact is valid", () => {
304
    const failures = auditClaimContract("Fact A is live, ready to trade.", {
305
      card: CARD,
306
      deployed_facts_used: ["Fact A is live"],
307
      not_said: [{ fact: "Fact B stays private", reason: "not relevant" }],
308
    });
309
    expect(failures.map((f) => f.rule)).toContain("unsupported-claim");
310
    expect(failures.map((f) => f.reason).join("\n")).toContain("ready to trade");
311
  });
312
313
  it("allows product_page_url without requiring it in deployed_facts", () => {
314
    const failures = auditClaimTripwires("Fact A is live. https://example.com/release", {
315
      ...CARD,
316
      product_page_url: "https://example.com/release",
317
    });
318
    expect(failures).toEqual([]);
319
  });
320
321
  it("keeps validate's hard fact gate to deterministic numeric/URL tripwires", () => {
322
    const railCard: ReleaseCard = {
323
      ...CARD,
324
      deployed_facts: [
325
        "Bridge.xyz supported fiat deposit rails: USD (ACH/wire, US account+routing number), EUR (SEPA IBAN), MXN (SPEI CLABE), BRL (PIX BR code), GBP (FPS, beta), COP (Bre-B, beta)",
326
        "Bridge.xyz fiat deposit settlement output: Fiat deposits are converted to a target crypto (e.g. USDC) and delivered on-chain to a configured destination wallet address on a specified payment rail (e.g. Ethereum)",
327
      ],
328
    };
329
    const r = validate(
330
      "Your Infinex account has a routing number. Wire it the way you'd wire a bank. It lands as USDC, on-chain.",
331
      { card: railCard, deployed_facts_used: ["wrong"], not_said: [] },
332
    );
333
    expect(r.passed).toBe(true);
334
    expect(r.failures).toEqual([]);
335
  });
336
});
337
338
describe("blind tempo classification", () => {
339
  it("classifies a paragraph without reading the declared beat", () => {
340
    const paragraph =
341
      "Today: spot Hyperliquid is live in Infinex. Same account, same passkey, the orderbook where your portfolio already lives.";
342
    const classified = classifyTempoBlind(paragraph, INFINEX_VOICE);
343
    expect(classified.tempo).toBe("commanding");
344
    expect(classified.confidence).toBeGreaterThan(0.4);
345
  });
346
347
  it("passes when declared beat matches the blind classification", () => {
348
    const paragraph =
349
      "Spot was harder than perps. The orderbook semantics had to feel native, not bolted on. We held it back until that was true.";
350
    const audit = auditBeats(paragraph, { beats: [{ tempo: "practical" }] }, INFINEX_VOICE);
351
    expect(audit[0]?.passed).toBe(true);
352
    expect(audit[0]?.classified_tempo).toBe("practical");
353
  });
354
355
  it("passes with in-placement drift when blind read differs from declaration but stays inside the voice's locked tempi", () => {
356
    // Mirodan: tempo is what the audience reads, not what the actor declares.
357
    // Pass if classified ∈ voice.tempi, even if classified ≠ declared.
358
    // The regex classifier is constrained to voice.tempi by construction, so
359
    // any classification it returns is in-placement by definition.
360
    const paragraph =
361
      "Today: spot Hyperliquid is live in Infinex. Same account, same passkey, the orderbook where your portfolio already lives.";
362
    const result = validate(paragraph, {
363
      beats: { beats: [{ tempo: "irradiant" }] },
364
      voice: INFINEX_VOICE,
365
    });
366
    expect(result.passed).toBe(true);
367
    expect(result.failures.map((f) => f.rule)).not.toContain("beat-tempo-fit");
368
    expect(result.beat_audit?.[0]?.classified_tempo).toBe("commanding");
369
    expect(result.beat_audit?.[0]?.passed).toBe(true);
370
    expect(result.beat_audit?.[0]?.reason).toContain("audience read commanding");
371
  });
372
});
373
374
describe("auditTextHybrid", () => {
375
  function makeStubClient(responses: Array<Record<string, unknown>>): { client: NonNullable<LLMVoiceAuditOptions["client"]>; calls: number } {
376
    let i = 0;
377
    const state = { calls: 0 };
378
    const client = {
379
      messages: {
380
        create: async () => {
381
          state.calls += 1;
382
          const body = responses[i] ?? responses[responses.length - 1];
383
          i += 1;
384
          return { content: [{ type: "tool_use", id: `toolu_${state.calls}`, name: body?.name, input: body?.input } as never] };
385
        },
386
      },
387
    } as unknown as NonNullable<LLMVoiceAuditOptions["client"]>;
388
    return { client, calls: state.calls };
389
  }
390
391
  it("short-circuits on regex failure WITHOUT making an LLM call", async () => {
392
    const state = makeStubClient([]);
393
    const verdict = await auditTextHybrid("This is a game-changing, next-gen, seamless experience!", {
394
      voice: INFINEX_VOICE,
395
      llm_opts: { client: state.client },
396
    });
397
    expect(verdict.passed).toBe(false);
398
    expect(verdict.deterministic.passed).toBe(false);
399
    expect(verdict.llm).toBeUndefined();
400
    expect(verdict.reason).toContain("cliche");
401
  });
402
403
  it("calls the LLM judge when the regex layer passes", async () => {
404
    const state = makeStubClient([
405
      {
406
        name: "audit_pass",
407
        input: {
408
          notes: "fits",
409
          independent_classification: {
410
            tempo: "commanding",
411
            motifs: ["pressing", "punching"],
412
            detected_drive: "spell-vision",
413
            confidence: 0.85,
414
            rationale: "decisive landing",
415
          },
416
        },
417
      },
418
    ]);
419
    const verdict = await auditTextHybrid("Spot Hyperliquid is live in Infinex.", {
420
      voice: INFINEX_VOICE,
421
      llm_opts: { client: state.client },
422
    });
423
    expect(verdict.deterministic.passed).toBe(true);
424
    expect(verdict.llm?.passed).toBe(true);
425
    expect(verdict.passed).toBe(true);
426
    expect(verdict.reason).toBeNull();
427
  });
428
429
  it("fails when regex passes but LLM judge fails on character drift", async () => {
430
    const state = makeStubClient([
431
      {
432
        name: "audit_fail",
433
        input: {
434
          feedback: "Off-spec drive: Passion. Aspirational framing.",
435
          independent_classification: {
436
            tempo: "unknown",
437
            motifs: ["pressing"],
438
            detected_drive: "passion",
439
            confidence: 0.8,
440
            rationale: "rallying-cry shape",
441
          },
442
        },
443
      },
444
    ]);
445
    // A phrase that escapes regex but is off-character.
446
    const verdict = await auditTextHybrid("Reimagine what your portfolio could be.", {
447
      voice: INFINEX_VOICE,
448
      llm_opts: { client: state.client },
449
    });
450
    expect(verdict.deterministic.passed).toBe(true);
451
    expect(verdict.passed).toBe(false);
452
    expect(verdict.llm?.passed).toBe(false);
453
    expect(verdict.reason).toContain("Passion");
454
  });
455
});
456

src/__tests__/validator-llm.test.ts

352 lines
1
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
2
import {
3
  auditBeatsLLM,
4
  auditCopySetLLM,
5
  auditTextLLM,
6
  buildLLMAuditTools,
7
  buildLLMCopySetAuditTools,
8
  buildLLMValidatorSystemPrompt,
9
  parseLLMCopySetToolUse,
10
  parseLLMAuditToolUse,
11
} from "../validator-llm.js";
12
import { INFINEX_VOICE } from "../voice/infinex.js";
13
14
function toolUse(name: "audit_pass" | "audit_fail", input: Record<string, unknown>) {
15
  return {
16
    type: "tool_use",
17
    id: `toolu_${name}`,
18
    name,
19
    input,
20
  } as never;
21
}
22
23
describe("LLM validator tools", () => {
24
  it("requires independent_classification on both verdict tools", () => {
25
    const tools = buildLLMAuditTools(INFINEX_VOICE);
26
    const pass = tools.find((tool) => tool.name === "audit_pass");
27
    const fail = tools.find((tool) => tool.name === "audit_fail");
28
    expect(pass?.input_schema).toMatchObject({
29
      required: ["notes", "independent_classification"],
30
    });
31
    expect(fail?.input_schema).toMatchObject({
32
      required: ["feedback", "independent_classification"],
33
    });
34
  });
35
36
  it("defines a batched copy-set audit tool", () => {
37
    const tool = buildLLMCopySetAuditTools(INFINEX_VOICE)[0];
38
    expect(tool?.name).toBe("audit_copy_set");
39
    expect(tool?.input_schema).toMatchObject({ required: ["items"] });
40
  });
41
42
  it("keeps the blind-classification instruction explicit in the prompt", () => {
43
    const prompt = buildLLMValidatorSystemPrompt(INFINEX_VOICE);
44
    expect(prompt).toContain("BLIND to any `declared_tempo`");
45
    expect(prompt).toContain("Set declared_tempo aside");
46
    expect(prompt).toContain("unknown");
47
  });
48
49
  it("loads the same Mirodan substrate the generator uses", () => {
50
    const prompt = buildLLMValidatorSystemPrompt(INFINEX_VOICE);
51
    expect(prompt).toContain("stable|penetrating|flow");
52
    expect(prompt).toContain("primary = bottom-left");
53
    expect(prompt).toContain("introvert = top-left");
54
    expect(prompt).toContain("primary=spell");
55
  });
56
57
  it("requires detected_drive on both single and batched verdict tools", () => {
58
    const tools = buildLLMAuditTools(INFINEX_VOICE);
59
    for (const tool of tools) {
60
      const cls = (tool.input_schema as { properties: Record<string, { required?: string[]; properties?: Record<string, unknown> }> }).properties.independent_classification;
61
      expect(cls?.required).toContain("detected_drive");
62
      const driveSchema = cls?.properties?.detected_drive as { enum?: string[] } | undefined;
63
      expect(driveSchema?.enum).toContain("passion");
64
      expect(driveSchema?.enum).toContain("spell-vision");
65
    }
66
    const batched = buildLLMCopySetAuditTools(INFINEX_VOICE)[0];
67
    const itemsSchema = (batched?.input_schema as { properties: { items: { items: { properties: { independent_classification: { required?: string[]; properties?: { detected_drive?: { enum?: string[] } } } } } } } }).properties.items.items.properties.independent_classification;
68
    expect(itemsSchema?.required).toContain("detected_drive");
69
    expect(itemsSchema?.properties?.detected_drive?.enum).toContain("passion");
70
  });
71
72
  it("restricts primary-scope tempo enum to main_tempi + unknown", () => {
73
    const tools = buildLLMAuditTools(INFINEX_VOICE, { classification_scope: "primary" });
74
    const cls = (tools[0]?.input_schema as { properties: { independent_classification: { properties: { tempo: { enum?: string[] } } } } }).properties.independent_classification.properties.tempo;
75
    const allowed = new Set(cls.enum ?? []);
76
    expect(allowed.has("commanding")).toBe(true);
77
    expect(allowed.has("practical")).toBe(true);
78
    expect(allowed.has("sombre")).toBe(true);
79
    expect(allowed.has("irradiant")).toBe(true);
80
    expect(allowed.has("sociable")).toBe(true);
81
    expect(allowed.has("unknown")).toBe(true);
82
    // reserve tempi must be absent
83
    expect(allowed.has("self-contained")).toBe(false);
84
    expect(allowed.has("diffused")).toBe(false);
85
    expect(allowed.has("overpowering")).toBe(false);
86
  });
87
88
  it("widens beat-scope tempo enum to include reserve tempi", () => {
89
    const tools = buildLLMAuditTools(INFINEX_VOICE, { classification_scope: "beat" });
90
    const cls = (tools[0]?.input_schema as { properties: { independent_classification: { properties: { tempo: { enum?: string[] } } } } }).properties.independent_classification.properties.tempo;
91
    const allowed = new Set(cls.enum ?? []);
92
    expect(allowed.has("commanding")).toBe(true);
93
    expect(allowed.has("self-contained")).toBe(true);
94
    expect(allowed.has("diffused")).toBe(true);
95
    expect(allowed.has("overpowering")).toBe(true);
96
    expect(allowed.has("unknown")).toBe(true);
97
  });
98
99
  it("beat-scope prompt names reserve tempi as classifiable", () => {
100
    const primaryPrompt = buildLLMValidatorSystemPrompt(INFINEX_VOICE, { classification_scope: "primary" });
101
    const beatPrompt = buildLLMValidatorSystemPrompt(INFINEX_VOICE, { classification_scope: "beat" });
102
    expect(primaryPrompt).toContain("ONLY valid primary classifications");
103
    expect(primaryPrompt).toContain("DO NOT classify as primary");
104
    expect(beatPrompt).toContain("BEAT-scope audit");
105
    expect(beatPrompt).toContain("also classifiable in beat-scope");
106
    expect(beatPrompt).not.toContain("DO NOT classify as primary");
107
  });
108
109
  it("parses detected_drive on independent_classification and defaults to unknown when missing", () => {
110
    const withDrive = parseLLMAuditToolUse([
111
      toolUse("audit_fail", {
112
        feedback: "off-spec drive",
113
        independent_classification: {
114
          tempo: "unknown",
115
          motifs: ["pressing"],
116
          detected_drive: "passion",
117
          confidence: 0.8,
118
          rationale: "rallying-cry shape",
119
        },
120
      }),
121
    ]);
122
    expect(withDrive?.independent_classification.detected_drive).toBe("passion");
123
124
    const withoutDrive = parseLLMAuditToolUse([
125
      toolUse("audit_pass", {
126
        notes: "fits",
127
        independent_classification: {
128
          tempo: "commanding",
129
          motifs: ["pressing", "punching"],
130
          confidence: 0.9,
131
          rationale: "decisive",
132
          // detected_drive intentionally omitted
133
        },
134
      }),
135
    ]);
136
    expect(withoutDrive?.independent_classification.detected_drive).toBe("unknown");
137
  });
138
139
  it("parses audit_pass independent classification", () => {
140
    const verdict = parseLLMAuditToolUse([
141
      toolUse("audit_pass", {
142
        notes: "fits",
143
        independent_classification: {
144
          tempo: "commanding",
145
          motifs: ["pressing", "punching"],
146
          confidence: 0.91,
147
          rationale: "decisive launch line",
148
        },
149
      }),
150
    ]);
151
    expect(verdict?.passed).toBe(true);
152
    expect(verdict?.independent_classification.tempo).toBe("commanding");
153
    expect(verdict?.independent_classification.motifs).toEqual(["pressing", "punching"]);
154
  });
155
156
  it("parses audit_fail issue lists", () => {
157
    const verdict = parseLLMAuditToolUse([
158
      toolUse("audit_fail", {
159
        feedback: "remove marketing copy",
160
        voice_issues: [{ line: "Enjoy the seamless experience", rule: "marketing_in_utility", fix: "Name the action." }],
161
        factual_issues: [{ claim: "100 markets", evidence: "not in context", severity: "hard_fail" }],
162
        independent_classification: {
163
          tempo: "unknown",
164
          motifs: ["floating"],
165
          confidence: 0.7,
166
          rationale: "generic marketing",
167
        },
168
      }),
169
    ]);
170
    expect(verdict?.passed).toBe(false);
171
    expect(verdict?.voice_issues[0]?.rule).toBe("marketing_in_utility");
172
    expect(verdict?.factual_issues[0]?.severity).toBe("hard_fail");
173
    expect(verdict?.independent_classification.tempo).toBe("unknown");
174
  });
175
176
  it("parses batched copy-set verdicts", () => {
177
    const result = parseLLMCopySetToolUse([
178
      {
179
        type: "tool_use",
180
        id: "toolu_batch",
181
        name: "audit_copy_set",
182
        input: {
183
          items: [
184
            {
185
              id: "H01",
186
              passed: false,
187
              feedback: "generic marketing",
188
              voice_issues: [{ line: "Change the way you crypto.", rule: "allergen", fix: "Replace." }],
189
              independent_classification: {
190
                tempo: "unknown",
191
                motifs: ["floating"],
192
                confidence: 0.9,
193
                rationale: "generic slogan",
194
              },
195
            },
196
          ],
197
        },
198
      } as never,
199
    ]);
200
    expect(result?.items).toHaveLength(1);
201
    expect(result?.items[0]?.id).toBe("H01");
202
    expect(result?.items[0]?.passed).toBe(false);
203
  });
204
});
205
206
describe("auditTextLLM", () => {
207
  it("uses Anthropic tool output as the verdict", async () => {
208
    const client = {
209
      messages: {
210
        create: async () => ({
211
          content: [
212
            toolUse("audit_pass", {
213
              notes: "clean task label",
214
              independent_classification: {
215
                tempo: "commanding",
216
                motifs: ["pressing", "punching"],
217
                confidence: 0.82,
218
                rationale: "short decisive command",
219
              },
220
            }),
221
          ],
222
        }),
223
      },
224
    };
225
    const verdict = await auditTextLLM("Import an external wallet.", {
226
      client,
227
      voice: INFINEX_VOICE,
228
    });
229
    expect(verdict.passed).toBe(true);
230
    expect(verdict.independent_classification.tempo).toBe("commanding");
231
  });
232
});
233
234
describe("auditCopySetLLM", () => {
235
  it("audits multiple copy samples in one model call", async () => {
236
    let calls = 0;
237
    const client = {
238
      messages: {
239
        create: async () => {
240
          calls += 1;
241
          return {
242
            content: [
243
              {
244
                type: "tool_use",
245
                id: "toolu_batch",
246
                name: "audit_copy_set",
247
                input: {
248
                  items: [
249
                    {
250
                      id: "S01",
251
                      passed: false,
252
                      feedback: "generic marketing",
253
                      independent_classification: {
254
                        tempo: "unknown",
255
                        motifs: ["floating"],
256
                        confidence: 0.9,
257
                        rationale: "generic slogan",
258
                      },
259
                    },
260
                    {
261
                      id: "S02",
262
                      passed: true,
263
                      feedback: "clean label",
264
                      independent_classification: {
265
                        tempo: "commanding",
266
                        motifs: ["pressing", "punching"],
267
                        confidence: 0.82,
268
                        rationale: "dry command",
269
                      },
270
                    },
271
                  ],
272
                },
273
              } as never,
274
            ],
275
          };
276
        },
277
      },
278
    };
279
    const result = await auditCopySetLLM([
280
      { id: "S01", text: "Change the way you crypto." },
281
      { id: "S02", text: "Import an external wallet." },
282
    ], { client, voice: INFINEX_VOICE });
283
    expect(calls).toBe(1);
284
    expect(result.items.map((item) => item.id)).toEqual(["S01", "S02"]);
285
  });
286
});
287
288
describe("auditBeatsLLM", () => {
289
  it("passes with in-placement drift when Sonnet's blind read differs from declared but stays inside voice.tempi", async () => {
290
    // Mirodan: tempo is what the audience reads, not what the actor declares.
291
    // Sonnet reads "commanding" while generator declared "irradiant" — both in
292
    // Infinex's locked tempi → pass with drift reason.
293
    const client = {
294
      messages: {
295
        create: async () => ({
296
          content: [
297
            toolUse("audit_pass", {
298
              notes: "voice fits, audience reads a different in-placement tempo",
299
              independent_classification: {
300
                tempo: "commanding",
301
                motifs: ["pressing", "punching"],
302
                confidence: 0.86,
303
                rationale: "decisive launch line",
304
              },
305
            }),
306
          ],
307
        }),
308
      },
309
    };
310
    const result = await auditBeatsLLM("Today: spot Hyperliquid is live.", {
311
      beats: [{ tempo: "irradiant" }],
312
    }, {
313
      client,
314
      voice: INFINEX_VOICE,
315
    });
316
    expect(result[0]?.passed).toBe(true);
317
    expect(result[0]?.classified_tempo).toBe("commanding");
318
    expect(result[0]?.reason).toContain("audience read commanding");
319
  });
320
321
  it("fails when Sonnet's blind read is outside the voice's locked tempi", async () => {
322
    // Off-placement: Sonnet reads "human" (a Near-attitude tempo), but Infinex
323
    // is Stable-attitude — "human" is not in INFINEX_VOICE.tempi → fail.
324
    const client = {
325
      messages: {
326
        create: async () => ({
327
          content: [
328
            toolUse("audit_pass", {
329
              notes: "voice fits, but classified tempo is off-placement",
330
              independent_classification: {
331
                tempo: "human",
332
                motifs: ["floating", "gliding"],
333
                confidence: 0.74,
334
                rationale: "slow tenderness reads as Near-Human",
335
              },
336
            }),
337
          ],
338
        }),
339
      },
340
    };
341
    const result = await auditBeatsLLM("Today: spot Hyperliquid is live.", {
342
      beats: [{ tempo: "irradiant" }],
343
    }, {
344
      client,
345
      voice: INFINEX_VOICE,
346
    });
347
    expect(result[0]?.passed).toBe(false);
348
    expect(result[0]?.classified_tempo).toBe("human");
349
    expect(result[0]?.reason).toContain("outside the voice's locked tempi");
350
  });
351
});
352

src/__tests__/validator-active.test.ts

157 lines
1
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
2
import type { ReleaseCard } from "../card.js";
3
import {
4
  auditTextActive,
5
  buildActiveValidatorSystemPrompt,
6
} from "../validator-active.js";
7
import { INFINEX_VOICE } from "../voice/infinex.js";
8
9
const CARD: ReleaseCard = {
10
  id: "active-test",
11
  kind: "launch-tier",
12
  title: "Perps Launch",
13
  ship_date: "2026-05-21",
14
  audience: ["x"],
15
  deployed_facts: ["perps provider: Hyperliquid"],
16
  headline: "Perps",
17
  tier_reason: "test card",
18
};
19
20
function toolUse(name: string, input: Record<string, unknown>, id = `toolu_${name}`) {
21
  return {
22
    type: "tool_use",
23
    id,
24
    name,
25
    input,
26
  } as never;
27
}
28
29
describe("active validator", () => {
30
  it("keeps the card allowance boundary explicit in the prompt", () => {
31
    const prompt = buildActiveValidatorSystemPrompt(INFINEX_VOICE);
32
    expect(prompt).toContain("ReleaseCard is the allowance boundary");
33
    expect(prompt).toContain("card_missing_fact");
34
    expect(prompt).toContain("research_contradiction");
35
  });
36
37
  it("short-circuits deterministic failures before spending on active validation", async () => {
38
    let calls = 0;
39
    const client = {
40
      messages: {
41
        create: async () => {
42
          calls += 1;
43
          return { content: [] as never };
44
        },
45
      },
46
    };
47
48
    const verdict = await auditTextActive("Game-changing, next-gen, seamless experience.", {
49
      card: CARD,
50
      voice: INFINEX_VOICE,
51
      deployed_facts_used: ["perps provider: Hyperliquid"],
52
      not_said: [],
53
      client,
54
    });
55
56
    expect(verdict.passed).toBe(false);
57
    expect(verdict.reason).toContain("cliches");
58
    expect(calls).toBe(0);
59
    expect(verdict.research_trace).toEqual([]);
60
  });
61
62
  it("executes research tools and threads tool results back before verdict", async () => {
63
    const messagesSeen: unknown[] = [];
64
    let calls = 0;
65
    const client = {
66
      messages: {
67
        create: async (params: { messages: unknown }) => {
68
          calls += 1;
69
          messagesSeen.push(params.messages);
70
          if (calls === 1) {
71
            return {
72
              content: [
73
                toolUse("lookup_partner", { feature: "perps_trading" }, "toolu_lookup"),
74
              ] as never,
75
            };
76
          }
77
          return {
78
            content: [
79
              toolUse("audit_pass", {
80
                notes: "card fact confirmed",
81
                independent_classification: {
82
                  tempo: "commanding",
83
                  motifs: ["pressing", "punching"],
84
                  detected_drive: "spell-vision",
85
                  confidence: 0.84,
86
                  rationale: "dry decisive launch line",
87
                },
88
              }, "toolu_pass"),
89
            ] as never,
90
          };
91
        },
92
      },
93
    };
94
    const toolCalls: string[] = [];
95
    const toolExecutor = async (name: string, _input: Record<string, unknown>, id: string) => {
96
      toolCalls.push(name);
97
      return { tool_use_id: id, content: "provider: Hyperliquid" };
98
    };
99
100
    const verdict = await auditTextActive("Perps provider: Hyperliquid.", {
101
      card: CARD,
102
      voice: INFINEX_VOICE,
103
      deployed_facts_used: ["perps provider: Hyperliquid"],
104
      not_said: [],
105
      client,
106
      tool_executor: toolExecutor,
107
    });
108
109
    expect(verdict.passed).toBe(true);
110
    expect(toolCalls).toEqual(["lookup_partner"]);
111
    expect(verdict.research_trace.some((event) => event.type === "tool_call")).toBe(true);
112
    expect(verdict.research_trace.some((event) => event.type === "tool_result")).toBe(true);
113
    expect(JSON.stringify(messagesSeen[1])).toContain("tool_result");
114
  });
115
116
  it("surfaces card_missing_fact as an active-validator failure", async () => {
117
    const client = {
118
      messages: {
119
        create: async () => ({
120
          content: [
121
            toolUse("audit_fail", {
122
              feedback: "card_missing_fact: true but absent from card.deployed_facts",
123
              voice_issues: [
124
                {
125
                  line: "Perps provider: Hyperliquid.",
126
                  rule: "card_missing_fact",
127
                  fix: "Add the fact to the ReleaseCard or remove the claim.",
128
                },
129
              ],
130
              factual_issues: [],
131
              independent_classification: {
132
                tempo: "commanding",
133
                motifs: ["pressing", "punching"],
134
                detected_drive: "spell-vision",
135
                confidence: 0.83,
136
                rationale: "decisive product statement",
137
              },
138
            }),
139
          ] as never,
140
        }),
141
      },
142
    };
143
144
    const verdict = await auditTextActive("Perps provider: Hyperliquid.", {
145
      card: CARD,
146
      voice: INFINEX_VOICE,
147
      deployed_facts_used: ["perps provider: Hyperliquid"],
148
      not_said: [],
149
      client,
150
    });
151
152
    expect(verdict.passed).toBe(false);
153
    expect(verdict.reason).toContain("card_missing_fact");
154
    expect(verdict.llm?.voice_issues[0]?.rule).toBe("card_missing_fact");
155
  });
156
});
157

src/__tests__/orchestrator.test.ts

203 lines
1
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
2
import type { ReleaseCard } from "../card.js";
3
import type { Candidate } from "../generator.js";
4
import { orchestrateActiveWithRetries, orchestrateLLM, orchestrateWithRetries } from "../orchestrator.js";
5
import type { LLMVoiceAuditOptions } from "../validator-llm.js";
6
import { INFINEX_VOICE } from "../voice/infinex.js";
7
8
const CARD: ReleaseCard = {
9
  id: "retry-test",
10
  kind: "launch-tier",
11
  title: "Fact A Launch",
12
  ship_date: "2026-05-13",
13
  audience: ["x"],
14
  deployed_facts: ["Fact A is live"],
15
  headline: "Fact A",
16
  tier_reason: "test card",
17
};
18
19
function candidate(overrides: Partial<Candidate>): Candidate {
20
  return {
21
    id: "candidate",
22
    text: "Fact A is live.",
23
    channel: "x",
24
    declared_beats: [],
25
    deployed_facts_used: ["Fact A is live"],
26
    not_said: [],
27
    source: "stub",
28
    ...overrides,
29
  };
30
}
31
32
describe("orchestrateWithRetries", () => {
33
  it("feeds validation feedback into the next generation attempt", async () => {
34
    const feedbackSeen: Array<string | undefined> = [];
35
    const result = await orchestrateWithRetries(
36
      CARD,
37
      ["x"],
38
      async ({ feedback }) => {
39
        feedbackSeen.push(feedback);
40
        if (feedbackSeen.length === 1) {
41
          return [candidate({ id: "bad", text: "A game-changing launch." })];
42
        }
43
        return [candidate({ id: "good" })];
44
      },
45
    );
46
47
    expect(result.exhausted).toBe(false);
48
    expect(result.attempts).toHaveLength(2);
49
    expect(result.picks[0]?.candidate.id).toBe("good");
50
    expect(feedbackSeen[0]).toBeUndefined();
51
    expect(feedbackSeen[1]).toContain("cliches");
52
  });
53
54
  it("stops after maxAttempts when no candidate passes", async () => {
55
    let calls = 0;
56
    const result = await orchestrateWithRetries(
57
      CARD,
58
      ["x"],
59
      async () => {
60
        calls += 1;
61
        return [candidate({ id: `bad-${calls}`, text: "A game-changing launch." })];
62
      },
63
      { maxAttempts: 2 },
64
    );
65
66
    expect(calls).toBe(2);
67
    expect(result.exhausted).toBe(true);
68
    expect(result.picks).toEqual([]);
69
    expect(result.attempts).toHaveLength(2);
70
  });
71
});
72
73
describe("orchestrateLLM (hybrid: regex pre-filter + LLM judge)", () => {
74
  function makeStubClient(passResponses = true): NonNullable<LLMVoiceAuditOptions["client"]> {
75
    return {
76
      messages: {
77
        create: async () =>
78
          ({
79
            content: [
80
              {
81
                type: "tool_use",
82
                id: "toolu_orch",
83
                name: passResponses ? "audit_pass" : "audit_fail",
84
                input: passResponses
85
                  ? {
86
                      notes: "fits",
87
                      independent_classification: {
88
                        tempo: "commanding",
89
                        motifs: ["pressing", "punching"],
90
                        detected_drive: "spell-vision",
91
                        confidence: 0.85,
92
                        rationale: "decisive landing",
93
                      },
94
                    }
95
                  : {
96
                      feedback: "drift",
97
                      independent_classification: {
98
                        tempo: "unknown",
99
                        motifs: ["pressing"],
100
                        detected_drive: "passion",
101
                        confidence: 0.7,
102
                        rationale: "Passion-pulled",
103
                      },
104
                    },
105
              } as never,
106
            ],
107
          }),
108
      },
109
    } as unknown as NonNullable<LLMVoiceAuditOptions["client"]>;
110
  }
111
112
  it("picks a candidate that survives regex AND the LLM judge", async () => {
113
    const result = await orchestrateLLM(
114
      CARD,
115
      [candidate({ id: "clean", text: "Fact A is live." })],
116
      ["x"],
117
      { voice: INFINEX_VOICE, llm_opts: { client: makeStubClient(true) } },
118
    );
119
    expect(result.picks).toHaveLength(1);
120
    expect(result.picks[0]?.candidate.id).toBe("clean");
121
    expect(result.picks[0]?.verdict.passed).toBe(true);
122
  });
123
124
  it("rejects a candidate that fails regex without making an LLM call", async () => {
125
    let llmCalls = 0;
126
    const client = {
127
      messages: {
128
        create: async () => {
129
          llmCalls += 1;
130
          return { content: [] };
131
        },
132
      },
133
    } as unknown as NonNullable<LLMVoiceAuditOptions["client"]>;
134
    const result = await orchestrateLLM(
135
      CARD,
136
      [candidate({ id: "slop", text: "Game-changing, next-gen, seamless experience." })],
137
      ["x"],
138
      { voice: INFINEX_VOICE, llm_opts: { client } },
139
    );
140
    expect(result.picks).toEqual([]);
141
    expect(result.rejected).toHaveLength(1);
142
    expect(llmCalls).toBe(0);
143
  });
144
});
145
146
describe("orchestrateActiveWithRetries", () => {
147
  function makeActiveClient(): NonNullable<LLMVoiceAuditOptions["client"]> {
148
    return {
149
      messages: {
150
        create: async () =>
151
          ({
152
            content: [
153
              {
154
                type: "tool_use",
155
                id: "toolu_active",
156
                name: "audit_pass",
157
                input: {
158
                  notes: "fits",
159
                  independent_classification: {
160
                    tempo: "commanding",
161
                    motifs: ["pressing", "punching"],
162
                    detected_drive: "spell-vision",
163
                    confidence: 0.86,
164
                    rationale: "decisive product line",
165
                  },
166
                },
167
              } as never,
168
            ],
169
          }),
170
      },
171
    } as unknown as NonNullable<LLMVoiceAuditOptions["client"]>;
172
  }
173
174
  it("feeds active-validator and history-guard failures into retry feedback", async () => {
175
    const feedbackSeen: Array<string | undefined> = [];
176
    const result = await orchestrateActiveWithRetries(
177
      CARD,
178
      ["x"],
179
      async ({ feedback }) => {
180
        feedbackSeen.push(feedback);
181
        if (feedbackSeen.length === 1) {
182
          return [candidate({ id: "repeated", text: "Fact A is live." })];
183
        }
184
        return [candidate({ id: "fresh", text: "For web, Fact A is live." })];
185
      },
186
      {
187
        voice: INFINEX_VOICE,
188
        active_opts: { client: makeActiveClient() },
189
        recentCopyByChannel: {
190
          x: [
191
            { id: "prior-1", channel: "x", text: "Fact A is live.", primary_tempo: "practical" },
192
          ],
193
        },
194
        maxAttempts: 2,
195
      },
196
    );
197
198
    expect(result.exhausted).toBe(false);
199
    expect(result.picks[0]?.candidate.id).toBe("fresh");
200
    expect(feedbackSeen[1]).toContain("history:repeated-opener");
201
  });
202
});
203

src/__tests__/voice.test.ts

23 lines
1
import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
2
import { INFINEX_VOICE } from "../voice/infinex.js";
3
4
describe("Infinex voice spec v2 substrate", () => {
5
  it("carries the Mirodan kernel and drive table as first-class voice data", () => {
6
    expect(INFINEX_VOICE.mirodan_kernel).toContain("Motion Factors");
7
    expect(INFINEX_VOICE.mirodan_kernel).toContain("Subdued, hidden, or introvert does not mean absent");
8
    expect(INFINEX_VOICE.mirodan_kernel).toContain("axis is a structural read, not a playable verb");
9
    expect(INFINEX_VOICE.drive_table).toContain("primary = bottom-left");
10
    expect(INFINEX_VOICE.drive_table).toContain("extravert = top-right");
11
    expect(INFINEX_VOICE.drive_table).toContain("stable|penetrating|flow");
12
    expect(INFINEX_VOICE.drive_table).toContain("primary=spell");
13
  });
14
15
  it("models Diagram D with all four drive slots, not just a two-word axis", () => {
16
    expect(INFINEX_VOICE.drive_primary).toBe("spell");
17
    expect(INFINEX_VOICE.drive_secondary).toBe("doing");
18
    expect(INFINEX_VOICE.drive_introvert).toBe("passion");
19
    expect(INFINEX_VOICE.drive_extravert).toBe("vision");
20
    expect(INFINEX_VOICE.drive_axis).toBe("Spell → Vision");
21
  });
22
});
23
